au-mic-c.coverage_note: 33 Decisions rows total. Only ~7 rows trace to a cacheable, full-text source: the insolation/equilibrium-temp/angular-diameter/color-temp chain and the L=0.102 input are anchored to Donati 2023 (docs/phase3/_papers/2304.09642.md, full text present, logL=-0.99 and Teff 3665 K confirmed), and the gravity/density/scale-height rows are internally derived from the mass/radius and check out numerically. The cfg-decisive orbital and physical rows (eccentricity, arg_periastron, sma, period, inclination, mass, radius) cite Martioli 2021 and Mallorquin 2024 — NEITHER is in the _papers cache and Mallorquin has no resolvable arXiv id anywhere (report bib or host bib docs/phase3/_bib/au-mic.yaml, which omits both papers entirely). These values DO match db/planets_curated.json (so no PHASE2_DESYNC), but they cannot be verified against the cited paper full text offline (UNVERIFIABLE). Several framework/analog citations (Lopez&Fortney 2014 1311.0329, Owen&Wu 2017 1705.10810, Fulton 2017 1703.10375, Showman 2009 0809.2089, Cale 2021 2109.13996, Wittrock 2023 2310.10719, Szabo 2021 2108.07984) are absent from the cache; Plavchan 2020 (2006.13248) and Zeng 2019 (1906.04253) exist only as 50-byte stub .md files (subject-class header only, full text only in the .html siblings). Note: docs/phase3/_bib/au-mic-c.yaml does not exist; only the host bib au-mic.yaml is present, and it does not contain the c discovery/characterization papers. The most material findings are the high-severity internal contradiction on tidally_locked (table says false/pseudo-synchronous/3:2; prose + intro + KSP note adopt pure 1:1 synchronous), the physically-implausible forced 1:1 lock on an e=0.18 orbit in the prose, and the unverifiable decision-driving e=0.18 which the one cache-verifiable system paper (Donati 2023) finds 'compatible with 0'.

findings.claim: Decisions row: tidally_locked = `false (pseudo-synchronous; 3:2 candidate)`, confidence high — e=0.18 drives 3:2-capture regime, not pure 1:1.
findings.source_says: The report's own Rotation & spin synthesis says the opposite: 'c is likely fully locked into 1:1 spin-orbit synchronization ... the dominant tidal damping for puffy planets favors 1:1, and the cfg adopts the synchronous state'; the KSP note sets 'Rotation period = orbital period = 18.859023 days'; and the intro (line 20) calls c 'a tidally-locked warm sub-Neptune'. The cfg-decisive rotation pick is self-contradictory.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:34 (row=false/3:2) vs docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:20 ('tidally-locked warm sub-Neptune') and docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:153-164 ('likely fully locked into 1:1 spin-orbit synchronization ... the cfg adopts the synchronous state ... Rotation period = orbital period = 18.859023 days').

findings.claim: Rotation synthesis adopts pure 1:1 spin-orbit synchronization ('the cfg adopts the synchronous state', rotationPeriod = orbital period) for AU Mic c.
findings.source_says: The report itself states e = 0.18 for c. A forced 1:1 tidal lock on an eccentric (e=0.18 >> 0.01) orbit is physically inconsistent — the Decisions row correctly notes pseudo-synchronous spin (Hut 1981 omega/n ~ 1.24, 3:2 regime), but the prose overrides this with pure 1:1, which is the flagged 'forced 1:1 lock on eccentric orbit' pattern.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:153-158 ('likely fully locked into 1:1 spin-orbit synchronization ... the cfg adopts the synchronous state') against e=0.18 at docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:36,154.

findings.claim: eccentricity = 0.18, Basis 'Mallorquin 2024 ESPRESSO + TESS joint fit' (confidence medium). This value drives the pseudo-synchronous/3:2 rotation argument and the seasonal-modulation section.
findings.source_says: Mallorquin 2024 (2024A&A...689A.132M) is absent from the _papers cache (no arXiv id is printed anywhere in the report or the host bib, and no cache file matches) — full text cannot be checked offline. Note: the value matches db/planets_curated.json (AU Mic c orbital.eccentricity 0.18, source 'Mallorquin et al. 2024'), so it is NOT a Phase2 desync; but it is unverifiable against the cited paper. Tension: the one cache-verifiable system paper, Donati 2023 (2304.09642), finds c's eccentricity 'compatible with 0' (error bar ~0.07-0.08).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:36; Mallorquin absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ and docs/phase3/_bib/au-mic.yaml. Contrast cache 2304.09642.md:218 'Adjusting the eccentricity of planets b and c ... eccentricities compatible with 0 (with error bars of 0.04 and 0.08 ...) ... justifying our a priori assumption of circular orbits'.

findings.claim: argument_of_periastron_deg = 72.8, Basis 'Mallorquin 2024' (confidence medium).
findings.source_says: not in cache — Mallorquin 2024 has no cached full text and no resolvable arXiv id; cannot verify offline. Value matches db/planets_curated.json (argument_of_periapsis_deg 72.8), so not a desync, but unverifiable against the source paper.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:37; db/planets_curated.json AU Mic c orbital.argument_of_periapsis_deg = 72.8 (source 'Mallorquin et al. 2024'). No 2024A&A...689A.132M / arXiv file in docs/phase3/_papers/.

findings.source_says: not in cache — Mallorquin 2024 absent. Value matches db/planets_curated.json (0.119) and is internally consistent with the derived insolation/Teq/angular-diameter math, so low risk, but the source paper itself cannot be opened offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:39; db/planets_curated.json AU Mic c orbital.semi_major_axis_au = 0.119. No Mallorquin file in docs/phase3/_papers/.

findings.source_says: not in cache — Martioli 2021 (arXiv:2102.05288) is absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ (no .md/.html) and is not listed in the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/au-mic.yaml. The 18.86 d period is corroborated indirectly by Donati 2023 prose (Szabo 2022 transit times), but the cited paper's exact value 18.859023 d cannot be verified offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:38; no 2102.05288.* in docs/phase3/_papers/; not in docs/phase3/_bib/au-mic.yaml papers list.

findings.source_says: not in cache — both cited papers (Martioli 2021 / Mallorquin 2024) are absent from the _papers cache. Value matches db/planets_curated.json (89.46), so not a desync, but unverifiable against the source.

The one verifiable system paper, Donati 2023, gives c mass 14.2 (+4.8/-3.5) M_earth — consistent in magnitude but a different value/source; the report's 14.46 cannot be confirmed against its cited paper offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:41; db/planets_curated.json AU Mic c physical.true_mass_mearth 14.46/3.24; cache 2304.09642.md abstract 'masses of ... and 14.2(+4.8,-3.5) M_earth for b and c'.

findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-c.md:42; db/planets_curated.json AU Mic c physical.radius_rearth 2.79/0.18. No Mallorquin/Martioli file in docs/phase3/_papers/.

findings.source_says: not in cache — Lopez & Fortney 2014 (arXiv:1311.0329) and Owen & Wu 2017 (arXiv:1705.10810) are both absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ and from the host bib; their scaling claims cannot be verified offline. eps-eri.coverage_note: 36 Decisions rows. Cacheable & confirmed against cached paper text: mass_msun, radius_rsun, teff_k (1112.0447.md Baines 2012: theta_LD 2.153 mas, R 0.74, Teff 5039, M 0.82, age ~1 Gyr); rotation_period_days (0711.0806.md Frohlich P1 11.348/P2 11.553, k 0.088); activity_cycle_years 2.92 (2002.11009.md Coffaro); metallicity_fe_h_dex -0.13 (astro-ph_0311541.md Santos HD 22049 = -0.13+/-0.04); companion_jovian_present (1810.03794.md Mawet Msini 0.72, edge-on 0.78@i89, coplanar 1.19; 2108.02305.md Llop-Sayson M_b 0.66 -0.09/+0.12 @ i78.81, a3.48, P7.37, e0.067); disk inclination 34+/-2 cross-confirmed in Mawet cache. spectral_type K2V (Keenan&McNeil) corroborated by Llop-Sayson Table 1 cache. age_gyr=0.44 Barnes 2007 not in cache but Coffaro cache explicitly states 'age of 440 Myr found by Barnes 2007 through gyro-chronology'. luminosity_lsun 0.32 not printed in Baines cache body (Table 3 omitted) but DB-confirmed. Backed by db/stellar_props_curated.json + db/disks_curated.json (Phase 2 layer) but the cited papers are in NEITHER bib YAML NOR _papers cache (so paper text unverifiable offline): all disk rows (Backman 2009, Greaves 1998/2014, MacGregor 2015, Booth 2017, Su 2017), limb_darkening (Claret 2018), spot_coverage (Roettenbacher 2016). These disk rows match the curated DB exactly (asteroid 3 AU/120 K, intermediate 20 AU, cold 64.4 AU/35 K, incl 34, mass 0.04) so are not desync'd — treated as verified-via-DB. The bib YAML note's '[Fe/H] = Sousa 2008' is itself inconsistent with the DB+report (Santos 2004 -0.13 recommended in both), but the report aligns with the canonical DB so no finding raised. Two genuine VALUE_MISMATCH rows (x_ray Lx min/max contradicting Coffaro's factor-2 / 2e28 average), one wrong arXiv id for Coffaro, one unverifiable exact log R'HK, one intro/table count contradiction.

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_max = 28.9, Basis 'Coffaro 2020 — XMM cycle maximum; cycle amplitude factor ~4'
findings.source_says: Coffaro 2020 reports average L_X = 2.0e28 erg/s with amplitude 1.3e28 erg/s; the per-observation L_X table (units 1e28 erg/s) maxes at 2.55 (Obs 9), i.e. log L_X,max = 28.41. The abstract states the cycle amplitude is 'only a factor 2', not 4. log L_X = 28.9 corresponds to ~7.9e28 erg/s, ~3x the actual observed maximum.
findings.evidence: 2002.11009.md line 7 (abstract): 'average X-ray luminosity results to be 2 × 10^28 erg/s, with an amplitude that is only a factor 2 throughout the cycle'; line 216: 'L_X of 2.0×10^28 erg/s, with an amplitude of 1.3×10^28 erg/s'; per-obs table (lines 80-88) L_X column max = 2.55, min = 1.35 (×10^28). DB activity block also records only log(Lx/Lbol)=-4.79, no 28.9 figure.

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_min = 28.3, Basis 'Coffaro 2020 — XMM cycle minimum'
findings.source_says: 28.3 (=log of 2.0e28) is Coffaro's cycle-AVERAGE L_X, not the minimum. The observed minimum is L_X = 1.35e28 (Obs 2), i.e. log L_X,min = 28.13. The report has labeled the average as the minimum.
findings.evidence: 2002.11009.md line 216: 'average X-ray luminosity L_X of 2.0×10^28 erg/s'; per-obs table line 81 (Obs 2, Rev 2775) L_X = 1.35 ×10^28 → log = 28.13.

findings.claim: Both X-ray rows (and the cycle row) cite 'Coffaro 2020', whose Bibliography entry prints arXiv:2003.07069.
findings.source_says: The cached Coffaro 2020 paper (2020A&A...636A..49C) has arxiv_id 2002.11009 per the bib YAML; 2003.07069 is NOT in the _papers cache and is the wrong arXiv id. The row is still resolvable via the YAML bibcode, but the printed arXiv id is incorrect.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/eps-eri.md:343 prints 'arXiv:2003.07069'; docs/phase3/_bib/eps-eri.yaml lines 48-49 map 2020A&A...636A..49C -> arxiv_id '2002.11009'; `ls 2003.07069*` returns no cache file.

findings.claim: activity_log_rhk = -4.496, Basis 'Gomes da Silva 2021 — AMBRE-HARPS, 553 spectra (Phase 2 recommended)'
findings.source_says: The cached Gomes da Silva 2021 paper (2012.10199.md) is the correct paper (AMBRE-HARPS, 1674 FGK stars) but does NOT contain the per-star eps Eri / HD 22049 entry in its cached text (the per-star catalog is a machine-readable VizieR table not reproduced). The -4.49 found in the cache is the sample-wide active-MS population peak, not the eps Eri value. The exact -4.496 figure cannot be confirmed offline. The value IS corroborated by db/stellar_props_curated.json (recommended -4.496).
findings.evidence: 2012.10199.md: grep for '22049'/'Eridani'/'4.496' finds nothing for eps Eri; line 280 '-4.49 (active, MS)' is the population mode. DB activity_measurements confirms -4.496 (Gomes da Silva 2021, recommended).

findings.claim: Scenario paragraph states '18 cfg picks; 15 canonical-aligned, 3 tie-break'.
findings.source_says: The actual Decisions table contains 36 rows (with at least 7 explicit tie-break/low-confidence rows: limb_darkening_alpha_h plus the three disk_*_opacity tie-breaks and the synthesized tint rows). The stated 18/15/3 breakdown is inconsistent with the rendered table.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/eps-eri.md:50-52 ('18 cfg picks; 15 canonical-aligned, 3 tie-break'); Decisions table lines 58-93 = 36 rows.

40-eridani-a.coverage_note: Host report (slug 40-eridani-a, no trailing planet letter); no companion bib needed. 6 of the 7 bib papers are cached as full text in _papers (Ma 2018 1807.07098, Boyajian 2012 1208.2431, Diaz 2018 1801.03970, Burrows 2024 2404.17494, Bond 2017 1709.00478, Rains 2020 2004.02343). Jenkins 2011 (2011A&A...531A...8J) has no arXiv id and is status:skipped (VizieR-only), so the activity_log_rhk citation is not directly cacheable. Of the 16 Decisions rows: 7 cite cached papers and were verified against them (mass=Ma, radius/teff/L=Boyajian, metallicity=Diaz, age=Bond, rotation+vulcan_disposition=Burrows); 3 are self-derived/tie-break rows that need no paper (stellar_color_temp_k, visual_surface_tint, and one derived diameter — see finding); 6 cite sources absent from the cache (Keenan&McNeil 1989 spectral type, Jenkins 2011 logR'HK, generic ROSAT x-ray baseline, WDS/Tokovinin separation, van Leeuwen 2007 V mag) — most of these have cached cross-corroboration (Ma 2018 confirms K0.5V and V~4.4; Burrows 2024 Table 1 independently lists logR'HK=-4.99 and V=4.4) so their VALUES are sound even though the named source is uncacheable. The DB (stellar_props_curated.json, planets_curated.json) was checked for PHASE2_DESYNC: no curated planet (consistent with the report's refutation note), and the age recommended-flag claims in Canonical alternatives (Ma 2018 recommended:true, Bond 2017 recommended:false) match the DB exactly. One bibliography-prose desync found against the corrected binary_orbits.json (B-C orbit P/e).

findings.claim: Value 1.9 arcsec, confidence=high, with the formula stated in the same cell as: derived = 2 R★ / a × (180·3600/π); a = 400 AU.
findings.source_says: The formula as written (2·R★/a, i.e. full angular DIAMETER) with R★ = 0.8061 R☉ and a = 400 AU evaluates to 3.87 arcsec, not 1.9. The stated 1.9 arcsec is exactly the angular RADIUS (R★/a = 1.93 arcsec), which omits the explicit 2× factor and contradicts the field's own 'diameter' label.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/40-eridani-a.md:91 (Decisions row). Recomputed: (2×0.8061×6.957e8 m)/(400×1.496e11 m)×(180×3600/π)=3.866 arcsec; angular radius R★/a=1.933 arcsec. The value 1.9 matches the radius, not the diameter the formula and field name specify.

findings.claim: K0.5 V, confidence=high, Basis: Keenan & McNeil 1989 — IAU MK system.
findings.source_says: Keenan & McNeil 1989 is in neither the bib YAML nor the _papers cache, so the named source cannot be checked offline. The VALUE is corroborated by cached Ma 2018, which explicitly calls HD 26965 a 'K0.5V' star.
findings.evidence: _papers/1807.07098.md line 23: 'a nearby bright K0.5V star with V=4.4 mag, HD 26965'. The cited attribution (Keenan & McNeil 1989) is absent from docs/phase3/_bib/40-eridani-a.yaml and the cache.

findings.claim: −4.99, confidence=medium, Basis: Jenkins et al. 2011 — Ca II H&K HARPS chromospheric survey; Henry 1996 −4.94 corroborates.
findings.source_says: Jenkins 2011 (2011A&A...531A...8J) has arxiv_id:null and status:skipped in the bib YAML (VizieR-only, no ar5iv), so it is absent from the _papers cache and cannot be verified offline; Henry 1996 is likewise not cached. However the VALUE -4.99 is independently corroborated by cached Burrows 2024.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/40-eridani-a.yaml lines 45-49 (Jenkins arxiv_id null, status skipped). _papers/2404.17494.md (Burrows 2024) Table 1 / Sec. 2: 'moderate activity level of log R_HK' = -4.99'. The report itself flags this as an Open item (lines 447-454: 'CDS table row for HD 26965 was not directly fetched').

findings.source_says: No specific paper, bibcode, or arXiv id is cited — only a generic 'ROSAT all-sky survey baseline'. There is no entry in the bib YAML or _papers cache that could ever have verified this number; it is unsourced. (The row is self-labeled confidence=low and is not cfg-decisive.)
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/40-eridani-a.md:87 — Basis names no author/year/bibcode, just 'ROSAT all-sky survey baseline'. No ROSAT paper appears in docs/phase3/_bib/40-eridani-a.yaml.

findings.source_says: The WDS catalog and Tokovinin MSC 2018 are not in the _papers cache or bib YAML and cannot be checked offline. The value is internally self-consistent across the report's prose but unverifiable against any cached source.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/40-eridani-a.md:92; neither WDS J04153-0739 nor Tokovinin 2018 appears in docs/phase3/_bib/40-eridani-a.yaml or _papers/.

findings.claim: 4.43, confidence=high, Basis: Hipparcos van Leeuwen 2007.
findings.source_says: van Leeuwen 2007 is not in the _papers cache or bib YAML, so the precise 4.43 cannot be confirmed offline. Cached sources give V=4.4 to one decimal (Ma 2018, Burrows 2024) and Rains 2020 Table 1 lists V_T=4.51; the 4.43 is plausible and consistent to 1 dp but the exact two-decimal value and its van Leeuwen attribution are unverifiable.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/40-eridani-a.md:93. _papers/1807.07098.md line 23 'V=4.4'; _papers/2404.17494.md line 25 'V=4.4'; _papers/2004.02343.md:40 lists 40 Eri A V_T 4.51. van Leeuwen 2007 absent from bib/cache.

55-cnc.coverage_note: All 3 decision-driving papers (von Braun 2011 / arXiv:1107.1936, Bourrier 2018 / arXiv:1807.04301, Moutou 2025 / arXiv:2510.11523) are present in both the bib YAML and the _papers cache. Of 21 Decisions rows: 16 cite a cacheable paper and were verified against it (spectral_type, mass_msun, radius_rsun, teff_k, luminosity_lsun, age_gyr, rotation_period_days, activity_log_rhk, activity_cycle_years, large_scale_field_g_min, large_scale_field_g_max, stellar_color_temp_k, visual_spot_coverage_max, companion_present, plus metallicity context corroborated in-cache via Moutou, and stellar_color_temp_k derived from von Braun Teff). 5 rows are self-declared tie-break/synthesis values with no paper claim to verify (x_ray_log_lx_cgs_min, x_ray_log_lx_cgs_max, limb_darkening_alpha_h, visual_surface_tint_hex_primary, visual_corona_extent_radii) and are properly labeled as tie-breaks, not divergences. DB (db/stellar_props_curated.json) fully corroborates spectype K0IV-V, teff 5196 (von Braun, recommended), mass 0.905 (von Braun evolutionary, recommended) with Crida 1.015 as the non-recommended alternative, and radius 0.943 (von Braun interferometry, recommended) with the explicit re-anchor note matching the report's prose — no PHASE2_DESYNC. von Braun cache lists theta_LD 0.711, R 0.943, L 0.582, Teff 5196, HZ 0.67-1.32 exactly; it does NOT tabulate a mass, but the 0.905 mass is sourced via Bourrier Table 1 which itself attributes it to von Braun 2011 (1807.04301.md:22), so the attribution chain is intact. No internal contradictions, no physical implausibilities, no policy violations (no \"divergence\" prose; mass/radius alternatives are labeled recommended-vs-documented-alternative, not inlined competing cfg values). Only one low-severity rounding note (large_scale_field_g_max Folsom attribution 3.4 G -> ~3 G).

findings.claim: Basis (row 69): "Moutou 2025 — field near cycle maximum (Folsom 2020 measured ~3 G in 2017)"; cfg value 3.0
findings.source_says: Moutou cache states Folsom's reconstructed large-scale field had an average strength of 3.4 G; Moutou's own max-phase value is "up to 3 G during the maximum".
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2510.11523.md:52 "...mostly poloidal, with 80 (20)% of the magnetic energy in the dipole (respectively, the quadrupole) and an average strength of 3.4 G." and 2510.11523.md:164 "the large-scale magnetic field strength is 0.3 G during the cycle minimum and up to 3 G during the maximum". The cfg value 3.0 is exactly Moutou's max statement; only the parenthetical Folsom attribution rounds 3.4 G -> ~3 G (qualified with "~"), so the cfg-decisive number is sound.

61-vir-d.coverage_note: 37 Decisions rows total. CACHEABLE SOURCES: essentially none of the decision-driving Basis citations are in the offline _papers cache. The Vogt 2010 discovery paper (anchors e/omega/P/a/Msini — 5 rows) has no arXiv id anywhere (not in report bib, not in docs/phase3/_bib/61-vir.yaml) and is absent from cache. All atmosphere/interior/tidal theory papers (Owen&Wu17, Lopez&Fortney14, Howe14, Madhusudhan12/21, Moses13, Morley15, Lavvas17, Showman09, Lewis14, Hut81, Henning&Hurford14, Bolmont20, Lewis69 — anchoring ~10 rows) are also absent. The only cited paper present in cache is Mamajek 2008 (0807.1686), which is a context paper (sets 6.1 Gyr age) and is NOT the Basis of any Decisions row; I confirmed it lists 61 Vir/HD 115617 with activity-age 6.1 Gyr (line 803). VERIFIED ROWS (9): the math-derived rows checked by independent calculation — insolation_s_earth (3.62), insolation periastron (8.57) / apoastron (1.99), equilibrium_temp_k A=0 (383) and A=0.3 (351), eq_temp periastron (476) / apoastron (330), seasonal_amplitude_factor (4.31), surface_gravity (0.916), density (1.01) — all reproduce within rounding using DB L=0.8222 L_sun and the table's M/R; plus the 5 Vogt orbital/mass values cross-checked against db/planets_curated.json (exact match, though paper-text-unverifiable). gj-896-a.coverage_note:
This is a STELLAR component report (GJ 896 A; the trailing "-a" is the binary component designation, not a planet letter — the planet is gj-896-a-b). The single decision-driving paper, Curiel et al. 2022 (arXiv:2208.14553, 2022AJ....164...93C), is present in both the bib YAML and the _papers cache (2208.14553.md), so all paper-sourced rows are cacheable and were verified offline.

All 19 Decisions rows verify cleanly:
- Paper-cited measurements confirmed EXACTLY against 2208.14553.md: mass_msun 0.43599±0.00092 (line 107/201, full combined fit); radius_rsun ~0.35 (line 191, §1.1, quoted from Morin 2008/Pearce 2020, method=unverified); rotation_period_days 1.061 and rotational_inclination ~60° (paper §1.1 + line 173 "60°±20°"); spectral_type M3.5 Ve (§1.1 + DB); binary_companion GJ 896 B M4.5, m=0.16527 M☉, a_AB=31.635±0.033 au, P_AB=229.06 yr, retrograde, Φ=148° (line 201/89/173 — all exact); age ~0.1–0.95 Gyr (Parsamyan 1995 950 Myr vs Riedel 2011/Zuckerman 2013 ≲100 Myr PMS, §1.1); activity/flare/magnetic rows qualitatively grounded in the compiled EQ Peg literature the paper cites (Pallavicini 1985, Benz 1995, Robrade 2004, Liefke 2008, Crosley & Osten 2018, Villadsen & Hallinan 2019, Bower 2009/2011, Morin 2008 ZDI).
- The 0.39 M☉ caveat in the mass row is correct: §1.1 gives 0.39 M☉ as the prior-literature value, line 107/157 gives 0.436 M☉ as this work's fit.
- Synthesized rows (teff_k ~3300, luminosity ~0.013–0.022, visual_surface_tint #d05a2e, stellar_color_temp_k ~3300, visual_spot_coverage_max 0.2) are all explicitly flagged "synthesized/tie-break, not Phase 2" with NO false paper attribution; the paper genuinely measures no Teff or luminosity (only an HZ mass-luminosity approximation, lines 153-155), and the bib YAML documents that Cifuentes 2020 / Schweitzer 2019 catalog Teff/L were checked and found BLANK. Stefan-Boltzmann L≈0.014 L☉ and the ~0.02 L☉ HZ-relation estimate were both independently reproduced.
- Gaia-DR3-sourced rows (distance_pc 6.263 / parallax 159.66 mas — confirmed by paper line 103 Π_A=159.663±0.034 mas; apparent_magnitude_v 10.29) are external-catalog values, properly attributed to Gaia DR3 rather than to any paper; the V mag itself is not in any cached paper but carries no false paper attribution and is not cfg-decisive.

PHASE2 cross-check: db/stellar_props_curated.json["GJ 896 A"] matches the report exactly (mass 0.43599±0.00092 binary_orbit recommended; radius 0.35 unverified recommended; rotation 1.061 unverified recommended; spectype "M3.5 Ve"; NO teff/luminosity arrays). db/planets_curated.json has the GJ 896 A b companion (covered by the separate gj-896-a-b report). No desync.

Internal/physical consistency: angular sizes (0.291° at 0.64 AU, 0.444° at perihelion 0.42 AU) and insolation (~0.03–0.05 S_earth, "a few percent of Earth's") were recomputed and match the report. No lava/tidal-lock/aurora-default issues (stellar report). No POLICY_VIOLATION: no cfg value diverges from a canonical reading, and synthesized values are openly labeled rather than dressed as measurements. No findings.

findings.claim: T_eq ≈ 1015 K, basis 'Gillon 2017 / TEPCat (2017NatAs...1E..56G); project formula 278.3·L^0.25/√a = 1021 K agrees'
findings.source_says: Gillon 2017 cache (1703.01430.md) is abstract-only ('ar5iv full-text HTML unavailable for this paper'); the abstract contains no equilibrium temperature, and TEPCat is not a cached source. The 1015 K figure cannot be confirmed offline. The independent project-formula value 1021 K is self-consistent, and Ligi 2019 (cached) quotes a blackbody T_eq of 1036 K for b — both within ~2% of 1015, so the value is plausible but not verifiable from the cited anchor.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1703.01430.md line 5 header '## Abstract (arXiv landing page — ar5iv full-text HTML unavailable for this paper)'; grep for '1015|equilibrium|temperature' in 1703.01430.md returns nothing. docs/phase3/_papers/1909.10058.md:290 'the black-body equilibrium temperature for this planet is 1036 K'. docs/phase3/hd-219134-b.md:47

findings.source_says: The stated value and the stated formula are mutually inconsistent: T_eq·√2·(1−A)^0.25 with T_eq=1015 and A=0.1 evaluates to ~1398 K, not 1250 K. The value 1250 K is roughly consistent with the substellar 2^0.25 factor (1015·2^0.25 ≈ 1207 K), not the √2 factor written in the cell. Either the formula factor or the quoted number is wrong. The downstream lava/partial-melt narrative depends on this peak temperature.

40-eridani-c.coverage_note: 18 Decisions rows. 14 fully verified against cached sources. Cacheable/decision-driving sources: mass (Mason 2017, 1707.03635 — mass 0.2041±0.0064, mass sum 0.776±0.024, P=230.09 yr, a=6.93″, e=0.4300 all verbatim), radius/teff/[Fe/H]/spectral-type (Mann 2015 Table 5, 1501.01635.html — R=0.274±0.011, Teff=3167±60, [Fe/H]=−0.21±0.08, SpT M4.7; Table 7 Dartmouth model R=0.242/Teff=3362 and the three-outlier §5.4 claim also confirmed verbatim), age + AGB-spin-up narrative (Bond 2017, 1709.00478 — total age ~1.8 Gyr, cooling age 122 Myr, Salaris 2009 IFMR M_initial~1.8 M☉, Fuhrmann 2014 spin-up citation all confirmed), rotation (Shan 2024, 2401.09550 — 8.56 d for J04153-076 M4.5 V, quality flag D, 27 debated/68 provisional, Shan first author / Kemmer co-author all confirmed), HZ scaling source (Kopparapu 2014, 1404.5292 — paper identity confirmed; note the bib YAML mislabels 1404.5292 as 'Kervella et al. 2014' but the report's own bibliography correctly attributes it to Kopparapu). NOT cacheable / problems: luminosity (Cifuentes 2020 per-star row absent from cached paper — VizieR-only); Hα pEW (Schöfer 2019 lacks the star, Newton 2017 cache file is the WRONG paper — a condensed-matter physics article, Reiners 2018 row gives no usable pEW). Burrows 2024 (no-planet justification) and Tokovinin 2018, Heintz 1974, van Leeuwen 2007, Cummings 2018, Fuhrmann 2014 are context papers without arXiv ids in the bib and are not Decisions-table anchors. DB cross-check: db/stellar_props_curated.json '40 Eridani C' matches every recommended stellar value in the Decisions table and confirms no curated planet — no Phase 2 desync. Companion-event B/A magnitude arithmetic is internally self-consistent except the A-BC separation (2000 AU) which contradicts the report's own 83″ (~416 AU) and ~8000-yr period (~470 AU).

findings.claim: Basis states 'A-BC mean separation ≈ 2000 AU. A at apparent V ≈ -9 from C (M_V ≈ 5.9 + distance modulus −15)... a much fainter but still naked-eye orange distant point on a strikingly different scale from B.'
findings.source_says: The report's own intro gives the A-BC angular separation as ~83″ at 5.01 pc (→ ~416 AU projected) and an outer period of ~8000 yr (Tokovinin MSC 2018). Kepler's third law with system mass ~1.6 M☉ and P=8000 yr gives a semi-major axis ~470 AU, consistent with the 83″ projection. A separation of 2000 AU would require P~70,000 yr — ~9× the report's stated ~8000-yr orbit.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/40-eridani-c.md:60 (row) and :8-10 (intro '~83″', '~8000-yr outer orbit') and :230-235 (Visual styling '~2000 AU'). Arithmetic: 83″×5.01pc=416 AU; a³=M·P² → a=470 AU for P=8000 yr, Mtot=1.6. At the physically correct ~420-470 AU, M_V 5.9 + distance modulus(−18.2 to −18.5) → V≈−12.3 to −12.6, i.e. comparable to B (V≈−13), not the faint V≈−9 distant point the row asserts. The 2000 AU figure is the only input that yields V≈−9, so the row's rendering conclusion (A much fainter, different scale from B) is reversed under the report's own orbital data.

55-cnc-d.coverage_note: 31 Decisions rows audited. Cacheable/verified sources: 3 of the 4 cited papers are present in the _papers cache (Moutou 2025 = 2510.11523, von Braun 2011 = 1107.1936, Bourrier 2018 = 1807.04301); Marcy 2002 is intro/historical only (no Decisions row depends on it). VERIFIED directly against cache or correct derivation (26 rows): sidereal_period_days=4799 (cache 'P=4799.3 d'); mass_mjup=3.8 (cache 'minimum mass of 3.8 M Jup'); mass_type=Msini; stellar_illumination_color_temp_k=5196 (von Braun cache Teff=5196 K + DB); equilibrium_temp_k A=0 (recomputed 102.7→103), A=0.3 (94.0); insolation_s_earth (L/a²=0.0186→0.019); star_apparent_angular_diameter_deg (2·0.943R☉/5.6AU=0.0897→0.090, internally consistent with prose '⅙ the Sun's size' = 0.088°); tidally_locked=false and rotation prose (5.6 AU, far outside tidal regime — physically sound); plus the obliquity/albedo/cloud/atmosphere/ring tie-breaks (all low-confidence aesthetic/policy, correctly flagged). The Canonical alternatives section is present and correctly documents the 4799 vs 5574 d divergence (Bourrier values e=0.13, a=5.957, M=991.6, cycle=3822 d / ~10.5 yr ALL match Bourrier cache Table 8 lines 100-111, 263); the 9σ and 13.15-yr and f–d 4-AU-gap claims all match Moutou cache. NOT fully verifiable offline (5 rows flagged): the three Moutou-attributed numeric values eccentricity=0.0913, semi_major_axis_au=5.6, and mass_mearth=1214±41 are NOT in the Moutou cache prose — they live in Moutou's Table 8, which is referenced (2510.11523.md:154) but NOT reproduced in the cache; they ARE present in the verified Phase 2 DB (db/planets_curated.json:773-808, recommended=true). So the report's statement (line 174) that these were 'value-checked against the cached text' is imprecise — they were DB-backed, not paper-text-checked; mass_mjup=3.8 is the only Moutou physical value literally in the cache. The remaining two (argument_of_periastron_deg=−90, inclination_deg=~89.7) cite 'NASA Archive'/'raw value', which is in neither the bib nor the cache (UNSOURCED), though both are explicitly low-confidence non-decisive placeholders. No PHASE2_DESYNC: the report agrees with planets_curated.json for d. No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE, INTERNAL_CONTRADICTION, or POLICY_VIOLATION found (the ring is correctly handled as an artistic option with ring_observed=false and is excluded from the divergence section per policy).

findings.claim: mass_mearth = 1214 (M sin i), confidence HIGH, Basis 'Moutou 2025 — RV minimum mass'; report (line 174) asserts the Moutou values were 'value-checked against the cached text'.
findings.source_says: The Moutou 2025 cache states only 'a minimum mass of 3.8 M Jup' (abstract/line 7) and references 'best fit model parameters are listed in Table 8' (line 154) — but Table 8 itself is NOT reproduced in the cache. The literal value '1214' (or '1214 ± 41 M⊕') appears nowhere in 2510.11523.md. 3.8 M_Jup * 317.8 = 1207, consistent with 1214 to ~0.6%, so the value is plausible, but the exact figure cannot be confirmed against the cached paper text offline. (The value 1214.1 ± 41.3 IS present in db/planets_curated.json:801 attributed to Moutou 2025, recommended=true, so it traces to the verified Phase 2 DB, not the cached paper text.)
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2510.11523.md:7 ('minimum mass of 3.8 M Jup'), :154 ('best fit model parameters are listed in Table 8' — table absent from cache); db/planets_curated.json:801 (mass_mearth 1214.1, Moutou 2025). Report claim at docs/phase3/55-cnc-d.md:39 and :174.

55-cnc-b.coverage_note: 24 Decisions rows total. Cacheable-source rows that fully VERIFIED (19): stellar_illumination_color_temp_k=5196 and the L=0.582 used for T_eq both confirmed in von Braun 1107.1936.md Table 1 (Teff 5196±24, L 0.582±0.014, R 0.943±0.010); all four derived-physics rows recomputed exactly (T_eq A=0 = 707.6≈708; T_eq A=0.3 = 647.3≈647; insolation L/a^2 = 41.8; star_apparent_angular_diameter 2R*/a with R*=0.943 = 4.26 deg); mass_type/atmosphere_present and the tie-break rows (tidally_locked false/pseudo-sync — physically appropriate for a warm Jupiter on e<0.01 orbit, no forced 1:1 lock, no lava/implausibility; obliquity, bond_albedo, cloud_cover, cloud_morphology, cloud/atmosphere tints, reference_pressure, composition) are internally consistent tie-breaks at flagged low/medium confidence. The Bourrier 2018 cross-check values (P 14.6516, a 0.1134, e 0.00, M sin i 255.4 / 0.8036 M_Jup, omega -21.5) are all fully verified in 1807.04301.md (lines 102-111). 5 rows are NOT fully verified. The four decision-DRIVING recommended values attributed to 'Moutou 2025' (period, a, eccentricity, mass) are UNVERIFIABLE offline: the cached paper 2510.11523.md (218 lines) is the correct paper but its 55 Cnc A planet parameter table (Table 8, referenced at line 154) is truncated/absent from the cache — only the abstract '14.65 d', the 55 Cnc B planet Table 4, and the outer-planet-d figures are present. The report's Bibliography claims these were 'value-checked against the cached text', but the cached text does not contain them. Note: db/planets_curated.json is in sync (identical Moutou-sourced values, recommended=true), so this is NOT a PHASE2_DESYNC and NOT a value fabrication relative to the DB — but offline-cache confirmation is impossible. The argument_of_periastron (23, NASA Archive) and inclination (89.7, raw) cite sources absent from both bib and cache (UNSOURCED), both low-confidence/non-decisive.

au-mic-d.coverage_note: 35 Decisions rows total. CACHEABLE/VERIFIED sources: only 2 cited papers have full text in docs/phase3/_papers/ — Donati 2023 (2304.09642, via au-mic.yaml bibcode map) which confirms L=0.102 L_sun (logL=-0.99) and Teff=3665 K, and Atri 2019 (1910.09871) which confirms Earth column density ~1036 g/cm2 (report says 1030, fine). Sergeev 2020 (2004.03007) and Plavchan 2020 (2006.13248) are cached but cited only in prose, not Decisions rows. ~14 rows are independently verifiable/consistent: the 6 Wittrock orbital+mass rows are NOT in cache but are exactly corroborated by db/planets_curated.json + planets_raw.json (same bibcode 2023AJ....166..232W); the 8 derived rows (insolation 9.3, eq_temp 478/466, scale_height 8.7, gravity 1.01, density 5.45, angular_diameter 4.4, color_temp 3665, obliquity 0) all reproduce within rounding (recomputed 9.25, ~486/473, 8.79, 1.01, 5.47, 4.37 — eq_temp uses a ~274 constant, ~2% low but not material). The CRITICAL gap: the cornerstone discovery paper Wittrock 2023 (arXiv:2310.10719) is absent from the cache and the planet-level bib au-mic-d.yaml does not exist (only the host au-mic.yaml, which lists only stellar-parameter papers, none of d's planet/atmosphere refs). ~7 other decision-driving papers are also absent (Driscoll&Barnes 2015 1506.08077, Owen&Wu 2017 1705.10810, Tristan 2023 2306.00077, Chen&Kipping 2017 1603.08614, Vinson 2017 1709.00007, Reiners&Christensen 2010, Goldreich&Soter 1966). Reiners&Christensen 2010 and Tristan 2023 grep-hits were false (unrelated papers). No PHASE2_DESYNC: report matches curated DB exactly. No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE: tidal lock on e=0.003 is fine (below 0.01); lava accent explicitly flagged as tidal-heating, NOT insolation melt; aurora/B-field tied to thin-CO2 chemistry, not asserted reflexively. Divergences are properly documented in a present '## Canonical alternatives' section. Net: the rows that drive cfg values are either DB-corroborated (orbital/mass) or self-consistent derived; the unverifiable findings are mostly low/medium because their numbers are corroborated elsewhere or are low-weight, but the wholesale absence of Wittrock 2023 and the missing planet bib means no row sourced to those papers could be checked against actual paper text offline.

It cannot be opened offline. findings.claim: Tidal damping ~10^5 yr for Q~100 (Goldreich & Soter 1966) << 22 Myr; e=0.003 < ~0.01 threshold for 3:2 (Vinson 2017, arXiv:1709.00007), so 1:1 lock unambiguous.

findings.claim: Report Bibliography (line 321) titles Donati 2023 as 'The magnetic field topology and filling of the very active M dwarf AU Mic' and labels it 'instrument / non-decisive', but the insolation row (line 57) relies on L=0.102 L_sun from 'Donati 2023, recommended'.
findings.source_says: The cached paper at arXiv 2304.09642 (mapped from bibcode 2023MNRAS.525..455D in au-mic.yaml) is titled 'The magnetic field and multiple planets of the young dwarf AU Mic' — not the title the report prints. Author (Donati), year (2023) and bibcode are correct; only the title string is wrong. The substantive value the report draws (logL/Lsun = -0.99 -> L=0.102) IS confirmed: paper line 53 '| log(L*/Lsun) | -0.99 +/- 0.01 |' and Teff=3665 K (line 39), so the decision-driving numbers are correct.

55-cnc-e.coverage_note: 37 Decisions rows audited. Sources resolve as follows: Bourrier 2018 (arXiv:1807.04301) CACHED + verified — anchors orbit/mass/radius/density/inclination/arg-periastron/period/eccentricity/μ/H-He-loss (all exact matches incl. e=0.05+0.03, i=83.59, a=0.0154, R=1.88±0.03 over 530-750nm, ρ=6.7/6.66, M=7.99/8.0, dry μ=13.49 g/mol, ~80000 yr H loss). Demory 2016 (arXiv:1505.00269) CACHED + verified — dayside T_B min 1365 +219/-257, max 2528 +224/-229, full range 1273-2816, T_eq ~2400, factor-3.7 variability, Bolmont 2013 tidal cite, Io/volcanic interpretation (all exact). von Braun 2011 (arXiv:1107.1936) CACHED + verified — Teff 5196±24, L 0.582±0.014. All ~12 derived rows (T_eq A=0 1959, A=0.3 1792, insolation 2454, gravity 2.26, density 6.63, angular diam 32.6°, period 63638 s) reproduce to <1%. Tie-break rows (pressure 1 bar, tints, cloud fraction/morphology) are correctly labeled tie-breaks and the atmosphere divergence has a proper \"## Canonical alternatives\" section (no POLICY_VIOLATION). NO PHASE2_DESYNC: report matches db/planets_curated.json for 55 Cnc e (recommended Bourrier values + Moutou 9.35 Msini as non-recommended). NO PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE: lava/magma-ocean is justified by measured T_eq~1959 K + Demory dayside 2400-2816 K (well above silicate solidus), tidal lock on e=0.05 orbit is defended via circularization (e consistent with 0 at 2σ). Only 1 of the 3 named papers (Hu 2024) lacks a cacheable source; everything decision-decisive except the Hu CO/CO₂ composition is independently verifiable offline. 34/37 fully verified; 3 flagged (1 medium UNVERIFIABLE for Hu-2024 dependency, 1 low UNVERIFIABLE for Moutou-9.35 absent-from-cache-but-in-DB, 1 low INTERNAL_CONTRADICTION for substellar-peak formula).

findings.claim: Row Basis says "Moutou 2025 Msini 9.35" as a context note alongside the decision-driving Bourrier 2018 value of 8.0.
findings.source_says: The decision-driving 8.0/7.99 is fully verified in Bourrier (abstract: M_p=8.0±0.3; Table line 109: 7.99 +0.32/-0.33). The Moutou "Msini 9.35" figure could NOT be confirmed from the cached Moutou paper (2510.11523.md): the 55 Cnc A inner-planet parameter table with planet e's minimum mass is not present in the cached markdown — only the 55 Cnc B planet table (m_b 3.5, m_c 5.3 M⊕) and the abstract surfaced. HOWEVER the 9.35±0.44 Msini value IS present in db/planets_curated.json for 55 Cnc e (source Moutou et al. 2025, recommended:false), so the figure traces to the curated DB even though it is absent from the offline paper cache.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2510.11523.md (planet-e min-mass table absent from cache; only B-planet rows at lines 193/195); db/planets_curated.json 55 Cnc e physical[1]: {"mass_mearth":9.35,"uncertainty_mearth":0.44,"mass_type":"Msini","source":"Moutou et al. 2025","recommended":false}

findings.claim: Row 60: value 2700 K, Basis = "derived bare-rock subsolar peak (A=0.1, no redistribution) ≈ T_eq·(8/3)^0.25; matches Demory 2016 ~2816 K upper bound".
findings.source_says: The stated formula T_eq·(8/3)^0.25 evaluates to ~2503 K (using the report's own T_eq(A=0)=1959 K), not 2700 K. The 2700 K value is instead justified by the second clause (Demory's ~2816 K occultation upper bound, confirmed in 1505.00269.md line 116/173: T_max=2816 +358/-368 K). So the value is defensible within the Demory envelope, but the cited derivation formula does not reproduce the tabulated number — a minor internal arithmetic inconsistency in the Basis text.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/55-cnc-e.md:60 ; arithmetic: 1959*(8/3)^0.25 = 2503 K; Demory upper bound 2816 K in docs/phase3/_papers/1505.00269.md:116

findings.claim: Rows 51 & 53 and the Canonical-alternatives divergence rest decisively on "Hu 2024 — JWST emission favors a volatile (CO/CO₂) secondary atmosphere over bare lava", driving atmosphere_present=true / pressure=100000 Pa.
findings.source_says: Hu et al. 2024 (2024Natur.630..609H) has NO arXiv preprint and its full text is NOT in the _papers cache. The host bib (docs/phase3/_bib/55-cnc.yaml) lists arxiv_id: null, status: skipped, category: manual_followup. The CO/CO₂ secondary-atmosphere claim therefore cannot be verified offline. The report itself flags this honestly ("no arXiv preprint — read via the Nature abstract"; Open-items: re-verify when full text pasted). The complementary Bourrier interior evidence (heavyweight few-percent-radius envelope, H/He excluded) IS verified in cache (1807.04301.md line 259, 255), so the atmosphere-present decision is partially independently supported, but the specific CO/CO₂ composition is unverifiable.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/55-cnc.yaml (Hu entry: arxiv_id null, status skipped, manual_followup); no docs/phase3/_papers/ file for Hu 2024; report self-flag at docs/phase3/55-cnc-e.md:247-254 and 282

au-mic.coverage_note: 33 Decisions rows audited. Cacheable/verifiable sources: of the decision-driving rows, 7 are fully verifiable against cached full text — radius_rsun (Gallenne 2022, _papers/2207.04116.md θ_LD 0.825 mas → R 0.862±0.052), teff_k (Cristofari 2023, _papers/2303.11241.md Teff 3665±31), metallicity value (Cristofari [M/H] 0.12±0.10, same file), luminosity_lsun (Donati 2023, _papers/2304.09642.md logL=−0.99±0.01; alt 0.09 from Plavchan 2009 confirmed in Klein 2021 _papers/2011.13357.md), magnetic_dipole_strength_kG (Donati 550±30 G), magnetic_total_field_kG_rms (Donati ⟨B⟩=2.61±0.05 kG), age_gyr (Mamajek & Bell, _papers/1409.2737.md). mass_msun (0.50±0.03) and rotation_period_days (4.86) are cross-verifiable via cached Klein 2021 (Table: 'Mass 0.50±0.03 P20') and Messina 2017 / Cristofari (Prot 4.863±0.010) even though Plavchan 2020's own full text (_papers/2006.13248.md) is only the arXiv subject header (body absent). v_sin_i_km_s ~9.0 confirmed in Donati (9.0±0.5 for R=0.862; Kochukhov & Reiners 9.2±0.1). spectral_type M1Ve corroborated by Donati/Cristofari. NOT cacheable / no offline source: Tsikoudi & Kellett 2000 (X-ray; no arXiv, status skipped), Cully 1993 & Tristan 2023 & Davenport 2020 (flare rows; no _papers entries — Tristan 2306.00077 cited in bib but absent from cache), all disk-imaging papers (Krist 2005, Schneider 2014/1406.7303, Boccaletti 2015/1510.06434 & 2018/1804.04574, Chiang & Fung 2017/1707.08970, Strubbe & Chiang 2006/astro-ph 0606435, Chen 2005) — none present in _papers; disk geometry/morphology values are nonetheless corroborated by db/disks_curated.json and by cached Gallenne/Klein intros (35–210 AU edge-on, 4–10 km/s features), except disk_inner_radius_au (DB recommends 10, report uses 35) and disk_dust_temperature_k=50 (DB null, unsourced). Visual/tie-break rows (limb_darkening Claret 2018, surface tint, flare color Kowalski 2013, substructure-animation) are explicit synthesis/tie-break picks with a present '## Canonical alternatives' section, so no policy violation; their cited grids/papers are uncached but the values are flagged as derived rather than measured. Mass/radius/luminosity DOCUMENTED-DIVERGENCE labels are policy-compliant (Canonical alternatives section exists and values are not inlined). Separately noted but outside the Decisions table: the Rotation prose claims differential rotation is 'comparable to the solar rate', whereas Donati 2023 explicitly states it is '~30% weaker' / 'about two thirds that of the Sun' (_papers/2304.09642.md:262) — a prose-level mischaracterization, not a table row.

findings.claim: spot_coverage_max_fraction = 0.12, Basis: 'Plavchan 2020 + Donati 2023 — multi-spot models require >=10% disk coverage; visible asymmetry of TESS lightcurve'; Surface section adds 'Donati 2023 ZDI inversion resolves these as latitudinal bands at +/-30 to +/-60 deg with >10% total disk coverage.'
findings.source_says: Donati 2023 does NOT derive a spot/brightness map or a >=10% disk-coverage figure. It explicitly states it 'did not attempt to model low-level brightness inhomogeneities' and that surface-brightness inhomogeneities have 'only a small impact' (photometric amplitude of a few percent). Its magnetic features sit at latitude ~30 deg, not '+/-30 to +/-60 deg' bands. Plavchan 2020 full text is not in cache (only the arXiv subject header) so the >=10% claim cannot be sourced offline.
findings.evidence: _papers/2304.09642.md line 124 'We did not attempt to model low-level brightness inhomogeneities'; line 118 'distortions ... dominated by magnetic effects, with only a small impact of surface brightness inhomogeneities (... a few percent)'; line 142 magnetic regions 'at a latitude of 30 deg'. docs/phase3/au-mic.md:77 and :112-114.

findings.claim: visual_spot_coverage_max = 0.12, Basis: 'same as spot_coverage_max_fraction; for animated activity layer'
findings.source_says: Inherits the same unsupported >=10%-coverage attribution to Donati 2023, which made no spot-coverage measurement (Stokes-V ZDI only; brightness inhomogeneities explicitly not modelled).
findings.evidence: _papers/2304.09642.md line 124; docs/phase3/au-mic.md:83

findings.claim: Decisions row: '+0.12 ± 0.10 ... Cristofari 2023 — direct SPIRou spectroscopy [M/H] (Phase 2 recommended ...)'
findings.source_says: The Surface section (lines 130-132) contradicts the row: it states the +0.12 is the 'β Pic MG mean — Shkolnik 2017; no direct AU Mic measurement is on file in the DB.' The cached Cristofari paper confirms [M/H]=0.12±0.10 IS a direct AU Mic SPIRou measurement, and db/stellar_props_curated.json carries it as the recommended value — so the Surface prose attribution (Shkolnik 2017 / no direct measurement) is wrong and conflicts with the Decisions row.
findings.evidence: _papers/2303.11241.md line 160 '[M/H]=0.12±0.10 dex' for AU Mic (direct SPIRou fit); docs/phase3/au-mic.md:68 (row) vs :130-132 (prose 'β Pic MG mean — Shkolnik 2017; no direct AU Mic measurement is on file in the DB')

findings.claim: flare_rate_per_day = 5.6, Basis: 'Tristan 2023 — TESS Sectors 1+27 flare census; 26.3 flares per TESS sector (~27.4 d) integrated above ~10^31 erg'
findings.source_says: The cited per-sector figure is internally inconsistent with the value: 26.3 flares per ~27.4 d sector = ~0.96 flares/day, not 5.6/day. The Atmosphere section instead states '>=150 events per sector' which would give ~5.5/day — so the Decisions cell's '26.3 per sector' contradicts both the 5.6/day value and the report's own prose. Tristan 2023 (arXiv:2306.00077) full text is absent from the _papers cache and not in the bib YAML, so the underlying number cannot be verified offline either.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic.md:73 ('26.3 flares per TESS sector (~27.4 d)' with value 5.6) vs :152-154 ('counted >= 150 events per sector ... the mean flare rate is 5.6 events/day'); no _papers/2306.00077.md present

findings.claim: flare_energy_log_erg_max = 34.5, Basis: 'Cully 1993 EUVE 1992 mega-flare; consistent with Tristan 2023 upper-end TESS optical events >= 10^34 erg'
findings.source_says: not in cache — Cully 1993 has no arXiv id and no _papers entry; Tristan 2023 (2306.00077) full text is also absent. The 10^34.5 erg figure cannot be checked against the cited sources offline.
findings.evidence: no _papers file for Cully 1993 or 2306.00077; bib au-mic.yaml lists neither; docs/phase3/au-mic.md:74

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs = 29.35, Basis: 'Tsikoudi & Kellett 2000 — ROSAT quiescent Lx = 2.24e29 erg/s, log(Lx/Lbol) ≈ −3.24 ...' (decision-driving activity proxy)
findings.source_says: not in cache — Tsikoudi & Kellett 2000 has no arXiv id (bib YAML marks it status: skipped, manual_followup) and there is no _papers full text. The Lx value cannot be verified offline. (The derived log(Lx/Lbol) = log(2.24e29 / [0.102*3.828e33]) ≈ −3.24 is arithmetically self-consistent with the report's own L, but the primary Lx number is unsourced offline.)
findings.evidence: _bib/au-mic.yaml lines 56-61 (arxiv_id: null, status: skipped); no _papers file; docs/phase3/au-mic.md:72

findings.claim: disk_inner_radius_au = 35, Basis: 'Schneider 2014 HST/STIS deep imaging — surface-brightness break at ~35 AU'
findings.source_says: db/disks_curated.json recommended Schneider-2014 entry has inner_radius_au = 10 (with an explicit audit note: 'inner edge corrected 35 -> 10 AU'); the ~35 AU is recorded only as the surface-brightness break, not the inner radius. The report adopts 35 as the inner radius, ignoring the DB's audited recommended value of 10. Schneider 2014 (arXiv:1406.7303) full text is also absent from the _papers cache, so the 35-vs-10 choice cannot be re-derived offline.
findings.evidence: db/disks_curated.json AU Mic recommended entry: inner_radius_au=10, outer_radius_au=210, morphology '...surface-brightness break at ~35 AU (audit 2026-05-29: inner edge corrected 35 -> 10 AU)'; docs/phase3/au-mic.md:85

findings.claim: disk_dust_temperature_k = 50, Basis: 'Chen 2005 Spitzer SED-fit cold component; ... around 0.092 L☉ host'
findings.source_says: not in cache — Chen 2005 (2005ApJ...634.1372C) has no arXiv id, is not in the bib YAML papers list, and has no _papers entry; Strubbe & Chiang 2006 (the corroborating ~50 K source) is likewise uncached. db/disks_curated.json carries dust_temperature_k = null for every AU Mic disk entry. The 50 K value is not verifiable from any offline source.
findings.evidence: bib au-mic.yaml has no Chen/Strubbe entry; no _papers files; db/disks_curated.json AU Mic dust_temperature_k=null; docs/phase3/au-mic.md:87 (also note the '0.092 L☉' here contradicts the adopted 0.102 L☉ used everywhere else)

findings.claim: age_gyr = 0.023 (i.e. 23 Myr), Basis: 'Mamajek & Bell 2014 — β Pic MG isochronal 22 ± 3 Myr ...'
findings.source_says: Mamajek & Bell 2014 give both a F/G-star isochronal age of 22 ± 3 Myr and an overall reconciled median of 23 ± 3 Myr. The row's value (0.023 = 23 Myr) matches the overall median while its Basis text cites the 22 ± 3 Myr isochronal figure, and the intro/scenario prose repeatedly says '~22 Myr'. Both figures are in the paper, so this is a minor value-vs-text mismatch, not a source error.
findings.evidence: _papers/1409.2737.md line 65 'median isochronal age of 22 Myr (±3 stat) ... overall median BPMG age of 23 ± 3 Myr'; docs/phase3/au-mic.md:69 (value 0.023, text '22 ± 3 Myr') vs :7,:46,:105 ('~22 Myr')

hd-219134-f.coverage_note: 26 Decisions rows. 21 fully verified: the 7 orbit/mass rows (eccentricity 0.0, arg-periastron 0, mean-anomaly 263, period 22.805 d, a 0.14574 AU, mass 8.9 Msini, and the controversy basis) all confirmed against cached Vogt 2015 (1509.07912.md Table line 130-139 + abstract) and db/planets_curated.json; 7 derived rows (insolation 12.4, T_eq A=0 523, T_eq A=0.3 478, surface gravity 2.2, density 6.1, angular diameter 2.66, stellar color temp 4858) recomputed and confirmed; stellar_illumination_color_temp_k 4858 and the implicit L=0.264/R=0.726/M=0.696 inputs confirmed against cached Ligi 2019 (1909.10058.md:119,121 + abstract) and host report. 8 explicitly self-declared tie-break/aesthetic rows (obliquity, bond_albedo, ref pressure, composition, two tints, cloud cover, cloud morphology) are policy-compliant (no divergence claimed; intro states 'No documented divergence', so no Canonical-alternatives section required). Cacheable sources: 3 of the report's cited decision-relevant papers are cached and verified (Vogt 2015, Johnson 2016, Ligi 2019). 3 cited papers are NOT cacheable for this report (Owen & Wu 2017 / 1705.10810, Lopez & Fortney 2014 / 1311.0329, Zeng 2016 / 1512.08827) — absent from both docs/phase3/_papers/ and the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/hd-219134.yaml, hence UNSOURCED. Note: Vogt 2015 internally letters the 22.8 d planet 'd' and the 94.2 d planet 'f', but the report/DB/Johnson-2016 use the standard NASA-archive lettering where f = the 22.8 d planet (confirmed in 1602.05200.md:143 and hd-219134.yaml note); this is correct, not a finding. No PHASE2_DESYNC (DB matches: f present, e=0, Msini 8.9, recommended Vogt 2015). No lava/molten or reflexive-aurora physical-implausibility issues; tidal-lock=false is consistent with e=0.

findings.claim: Scale height = 75 km, 'derived: H = kT / (mu m_H g) with T = 523 K, mu ~ 4, g ~ 22 m/s^2 ~ 75 km'.
findings.source_says: The report's own stated formula and inputs do not produce 75 km. With T=523 K, mu=4, g=22 m/s^2: H = kT/(mu*m_H*g) = 1.380649e-23*523 / (4*1.6726e-27*22) ~= 49 km, not 75 km. To get 75 km the report would need mu ~= 2.6 (pure H2) or g ~= 14.5 m/s^2 instead of the stated mu=4, g=22.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-f.md:55 ('atmosphere_scale_height_km | 75 ... H = kT / (mu m_H g) with T = 523 K, mu ~ 4, g ~ 22 m/s^2 ~ 75 km'); recomputation with those exact inputs yields ~49 km.

findings.claim: atmosphere_present = true (medium confidence), justified as 'near the photoevaporation-valley boundary (Owen & Wu 2017): a planet of this mass at 0.146 AU around a K dwarf can plausibly retain a thin-to-moderate volatile envelope.' Decision-driving for the whole atmosphere/cloud block.
findings.source_says: not in cache
findings.evidence: Owen & Wu 2017 is cited in docs/phase3/hd-219134-f.md:52,180-183 as arXiv:1705.10810 (2017ApJ...847...29O), but 1705.10810.md is absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ and the paper is NOT listed in the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/hd-219134.yaml (which contains only Ligi 2019, Gillon 2017, Motalebi 2015, Vogt 2015, Johnson 2016). It appears only in unrelated trappist-1 bibs, not this system's. Neither bib nor cache for this report -> cannot have been verified.

findings.claim: radius_rearth = 2.0 (low confidence), 'Zeng 2016 rocky ~1.9 R-earth; Lopez & Fortney 2014 with a thin envelope ~2.0-2.5 R-earth -> cfg picks 2.0 as mid-range.' This estimate feeds surface_gravity (2.2), density (6.1) and scale height (75) rows.
findings.source_says: not in cache
findings.evidence: Lopez & Fortney 2014 (arXiv:1311.0329, 2014ApJ...792....1L) and Zeng 2016 (arXiv:1512.08827, 2016ApJ...819..127Z) are cited in docs/phase3/hd-219134-f.md:45,184-194 but neither 1311.0329.md nor 1512.08827.md is in docs/phase3/_papers/, and neither is in the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/hd-219134.yaml. The cited mass-radius scaling values (~1.9 R-earth, ~2.0-2.5 R-earth) cannot be verified offline. Row is self-flagged LOW CONFIDENCE / estimate-only, but it drives three downstream derived rows.

findings.claim: Basis: 'Vogt 2015 pl_controv_flag = 1; f's 22.8 d period coincides with the Johnson 2016 22.83 d stellar S_HK rotation signal...' and prose 'Vogt 2015 flagged this with pl_controv_flag = 1.'
findings.source_says: Vogt et al. 2015 did NOT flag f as activity-driven. In the cached paper (1509.07912.md:200) Vogt explicitly tested the 22.8 d signal for spot modulation and CONCLUDED against it: 'This suggests that the P = 22.8-day signal is not the product of spot modulation, and that it has been present and stable throughout the full time span of our observations.' pl_controv_flag is a NASA Exoplanet Archive database field, not a Vogt 2015 designation; the actual basis for the contested status is Johnson et al. 2016's 22.83 d S_HK rotation period (1602.05200.md:143), which the report does correctly credit elsewhere in the same cell.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-f.md:36 and :86-87; vs docs/phase3/_papers/1509.07912.md:200 (Vogt argues the 22.8 d signal is a real planet, not spot modulation) and 1602.05200.md:143 (Johnson 2016 22.83 d S_HK coincidence is the true controversy basis, also confirmed in db/stellar_props_curated.json:3029).

hd-219134.coverage_note: All 17 Decisions rows audited. Every paper-cited row had a cacheable, verifiable source: all 5 cited papers are present in docs/phase3/_papers/ (Ligi 2019 = 1909.10058, Johnson 2016 = 1602.05200, Motalebi 2015 = 1507.08532, Vogt 2015 = 1509.07912 are full ar5iv text; Gillon 2017 = 1703.01430 is abstract-only but the only values it sources — b/c masses+radii — are quoted verbatim in its abstract and cross-confirmed in db/planets_curated.json). Stellar-layer rows (spectral_type, mass, radius, teff, luminosity, rotation, activity log_rhk, activity_cycle, distance, V-mag) all match the cited papers AND db/stellar_props_curated.json / astrometry_raw / photometry_raw exactly: R=0.726+/-0.014 Rsun, theta_LD=1.035+/-0.021 mas, Teff=4858+/-50 K, L=0.264+/-0.004 Lsun, M=0.696+/-0.078 Msun, rho=1.82+/-0.19 (Ligi); log R'HK=-4.89, cycle 4230+/-100 d=11.7 yr, P_rot=22.83+/-0.03 d, S_HK amp 0.0077+/-0.0003 (Johnson); alt Teff 4941+/-50 K SPC, alt mass 0.78+/-0.02 SYCLIST, alt P_rot 42.3 d (Motalebi); v sin i=1.8 km/s -> ~20 d, planet f 22.805 d, h ~3.11 AU/2247 d/e=0.06 (Vogt); parallax 152.864 mas, distance 6.542 pc, V=5.53 gaia_dr3_converted (DB). No PHASE2_DESYNC: planet inventory b/c/d/f/g/h with f&g pl_controv_flag=1 and no planet e all match planets_raw/planets_curated. Non-paper rows (metallicity null, age null, color tint, spot coverage, corona extent, disk_present false) are correctly labeled tie-break/synthesis/policy rows at appropriate confidence. No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE (stellar report; no lava/lock/aurora) and no POLICY_VIOLATION (rotation contest is a documented Phase-2-recommended value with alternatives in Open items, tie-breaks labeled as such). Only one minor issue found.

findings.claim: Decisions row + Surface synthesis: Ligi 2019 direct mass 0.696 +/- 0.078 Msun and the Motalebi 2015 SYCLIST evolutionary mass 0.78 +/- 0.02 Msun 'agree at ~1.5 sigma'.
findings.source_says: Ligi 2019 (cache 1909.10058, line 142) and Motalebi 2015 (cache 1507.08532, line 112: 'M_star = 0.78 +/- 0.02 Msun ... SYCLIST') give the two values; Ligi says only that the model mass is 'consistent with our uncertainties'. Quantitatively the offset is 0.084 Msun, i.e. ~1.0-1.1 sigma (1.08 sigma using Ligi's 0.078 alone; 1.04 sigma in quadrature), or ~0.67 sigma against Ligi's own C2kSMO 0.755 +/- 0.040. The '~1.5 sigma' overstates the tension.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134.md:54 ('the two agree at ~1.5sigma'); cache 1909.10058.md:142 'M_star = 0.696 +/- 0.078 M_odot ... 0.81 +/- 0.03 (Gillon 2017)'; cache 1507.08532.md:112 'M_star = 0.78 +/- 0.02 M_odot ... SYCLIST'. The same '~1.5 sigma' phrasing is inherited verbatim from db/stellar_props_curated.json meta_notes, so it is source-traceable rather than fabricated; not cfg-decisive (cfg value is 0.696 regardless) and errs toward claiming MORE tension than exists.

au-mic-b.coverage_note: Decisions table has 33 data rows (the intro's '27 cfg picks' is wrong). Cacheable-source coverage is very thin. Of all decision-driving cited papers, only TWO have full text in docs/phase3/_papers/: Klein 2021 (2011.13357, 85 KB) — but it is only a context citation AND the report mis-cites its arXiv id as 2012.04970 — and Plavchan 2020 (2006.13248) which is a 50-byte TITLE-ONLY STUB (no body), so effectively uncacheable. The headline source Mallorquin 2024 has NO arXiv id anywhere and no cache file; Cale 2021, Allart 2023, Hirano 2020, Lopez & Fortney 2014, Tristan 2023, Lavie 2017, Sing 2016, Showman 2009, Szabo 2021 are all absent from the cache, and NO planet bib (_bib/au-mic-b.yaml) exists to register them — only the host stellar bib au-mic.yaml. ROWS_VERIFIED (11) counts only the self-consistent DERIVED rows, which I checked arithmetically against the cited L=0.102 L⊙ (Donati 2023, recommended) and DB-confirmed M/R/a: insolation_s_earth 20.8 (=0.102/0.07^2=20.9 ✓), equilibrium_temp_k A=0 593 (calc 595.6, rounding ✓), A=0.1 577 (calc 580 ✓), surface_gravity_g_earth 0.39 (=8.99/4.79^2=0.392 ✓), density_g_cc 0.45 (calc 0.451, =0.082 ρ_Earth, 0.275 ρ_Neptune ✓), atmosphere_scale_height_km 567 (calc 560 with g=3.8 ✓), star_apparent_angular_diameter_deg 6.6 (calc 6.56 with 0.862 R⊙ ✓), surface_morphology n/a (consistent with density), plus the seven Mallorquin headline orbital/physical rows (mass, radius, e, arg peri, a, i, period) which match db/planets_curated.json exactly (DB-corroborated though the paper text is uncacheable). All remaining sourced rows are UNVERIFIABLE offline. No PHASE2_DESYNC: report values agree with db/planets_curated.json and the host radius divergence (0.862 R⊙) in stellar_props_curated.json is correctly used.

findings.claim: `tidally_locked`=true (high), rotation prose says b is 'fully locked into 1:1 spin-orbit synchronization' with sidereal_period_days = orbital 8.463446 d, while `eccentricity`=0.07 (Mallorquin 2024) in the same table.
findings.source_says: An exact 1:1 lock requires a near-circular orbit. For e=0.07 (>0.01) tidal theory predicts pseudo-synchronous (super-synchronous) rotation, not strict 1:1. The report even notes e=0.07 drives ~14% insolation variation (line 169) yet still forces rotationPeriod=period exactly.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/au-mic-b.md:37 (`tidally_locked`=true), :39 (`eccentricity`=0.07), :158-159 ('fully locked into 1:1 spin-orbit synchronization'), :164-166 (KSP note: rotationPeriod should match period). db/planets_curated.json:2434 confirms eccentricity 0.07.

au-mic-e.coverage_note:
33 Decisions rows total. The planet bib docs/phase3/_bib/au-mic-e.yaml does NOT exist; only the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/au-mic.yaml is present. Of the report's cited sources: Donati 2023 (2304.09642), Sergeev 2020 (2004.03007), Klein 2021 (host-bib arXiv 2011.13357 — NOTE the report at line 258 mis-cites Klein as arXiv:2012.04970, which is uncached; the correct cached id is 2011.13357), Plavchan 2020 (2006.13248), and Gallenne 2022 radius (2207.04116) ARE cached. The cornerstone Donati 2025 e-discovery paper (2025A&A...700A.227D) — the SOLE source for period 33.11 d, mass 21.1 M⊕, and the e candidate's existence — has NO arXiv id in the report, is absent from the host bib YAML, and is NOT in the _papers cache; every row sourced to it is offline-unverifiable. Mallorquin 2024b non-confirmation (arXiv:2407.16461, with a direct quote), Owen & Wu 2017 (1705.10810), Chen & Kipping 2017 (1603.08614), Tristan 2023 (2306.00077), and Goldreich & Soter 1966 (pre-arXiv) are all referenced but NOT cached (uncacheable offline). ~25 rows verified: the stellar-derived chain (insolation 3.94→3.9, a 0.161, gravity 2.51→2.5, density 4.77→4.7, scale height 51.6→51, angular diameter 2.85→2.9) is internally self-consistent and the L=0.102 (logL -0.99) and Teff 3665 inputs check out against cached Donati 2023; equilibrium_temp_k 381/371 is a few % below standard-formula 392/382 but within derived tolerance and not cfg-decisive; the analog/tie-break rows (tints, albedo, cloud cover, obliquity, AoP, inclination, surface morphology) make no external-paper claim. DB cross-checks confirm AU Mic c a=0.119, R=2.79, M=14.46 (matching report prose) but reveal a true-mass-vs-minimum-mass desync on e. The intro's '27 cfg picks' undercounts the 33 schema rows (not flagged). Minor: report uses host M=0.51 M☉ vs host-bib recommended 0.50 (Plavchan) — <1% effect on a, not flagged.</coverage_note>

61-vir-c.coverage_note: 32 Decisions rows total. Sourcing is severely thin: NO planet-level bib YAML exists (docs/phase3/_bib/61-vir-c.yaml is absent), and the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/61-vir.yaml lists only 6 stellar papers — none of the planet-decision-driving citations. Of all papers cited as Basis, only Mamajek & Hillenbrand 2008 (0807.1686) is present in docs/phase3/_papers/, and it is a context (non-decision-driving) row whose specific 61 Vir age claim is not actually supported by the cached text. EVERY other cited theory/discovery paper (Vogt 2010, Owen & Wu 2017, Lopez & Fortney 2014, Howe 2014, Madhusudhan 2012/2021, Moses 2013, Morley 2015, Lavvas 2017, Kreidberg 2014, Showman 2009, Lewis 2014, Hut 1981, Henning & Hurford 2014, Bolmont 2020, Fulton 2017, Pavlenko 2012, Wyatt 2012) is absent from both the bib YAML and the cache and could never be verified offline. Of the 32 rows, only 7 are effectively verified: the 5 orbital/mass rows (e, omega, a, Msini, P) are corroborated against db/planets_curated.json (Vogt 2010, bibcode 2010ApJ...708.1366V — exact match) and insolation_s_earth=17.4 checks out against the DB luminosity (0.8222 Lsun) and a. The remaining ~23 rows rest on uncacheable theory citations (UNSOURCED). On top of the sourcing gap, two derived physical quantities are wrong: equilibrium_temp_k is internally inconsistent with the report's own insolation/L/a (claims 525/478 K, should be ~569/520 K), and star_apparent_angular_diameter_deg is ~10x too small (claims 0.235 deg, should be ~2.4 deg). Two stellar inputs (Teff 5552 vs DB 5538; R* 0.963 superseded vs DB 0.9867; age 6.1 vs DB-recommended 7.69 Gyr) are desynced from the curated Phase 2 anchors.

findings.claim: T_eq(A=0)=525 K, T_eq(A=0.3)=478 K, both 'high' confidence, 'derived' from L=0.82 Lsun and a=0.2175 AU; insolation_s_earth=17.4 in the same table.
findings.source_says: For the report's own stated insolation S=17.4 S_earth (which itself is correct for L=0.8222 Lsun, a=0.2175 AU), T_eq(A=0)=278.5*S^0.25=568.8 K and T_eq(A=0.3)=520.3 K. The table's 525/478 correspond instead to S~12.6, not 17.4. The equilibrium temperatures are internally inconsistent with the report's own insolation and stated L/a (off by ~8%, ~44 K).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/61-vir-c.md:46-48 (insolation 17.4; T_eq 525/478). DB db/stellar_props_curated.json '61 Vir' luminosity_measurements value_lsun=0.8222 (recommended). Computed: 278.5*17.4^0.25=568.8 K vs claimed 525.

findings.claim: 0.235 deg, 'high' confidence, derived = 2 x R*/a x (180/pi) with 0.963 Rsun at 0.2175 AU; prose: 'about half the Sun's apparent diameter from Earth' (docs/phase3/61-vir-c.md:169,252-253).
findings.source_says: 2*R*/a in degrees = 2*0.963*0.00465047/0.2175*(180/pi) = 2.36 deg (or 2.42 deg with the DB-recommended R*=0.9867 Rsun). The Sun subtends 0.533 deg from Earth, so 61 Vir from c is ~4.5x the Sun's apparent size, NOT half. The stated 0.235 deg is ~10x too small; the formula in the Basis is correct but the output and the comparison prose are wrong.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/61-vir-c.md:66 (0.235 deg). Computed with the report's own formula and R: 2.36 deg. Sun=0.533 deg. DB radius_measurements value_rsun=0.9867 (recommended).

The curated DB records the Mamajek 61 Vir activity-age as 6.4 Gyr, not 6.1 +/- 1.7. The specific number/uncertainty attributed to Mamajek is not supported by the cache and disagrees with the DB's own Mamajek value.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/0807.1686.md header ('Improved Age Estimation for Solar-Type Dwarfs ...'), grep for '61 Vir'/'6.1' returns only unrelated cluster/binary table rows. db/stellar_props_curated.json '61 Vir' age_measurements: Mamajek value_gyr=6.4. docs/phase3/61-vir-c.md:340-341.

fomalhaut.coverage_note: 35 Decisions rows total. Only 3 cited papers are cacheable/verifiable offline: Mamajek 2012 (1206.6353, fetched), Absil 2009 (0908.3133, fetched), and Royer 2007 (astro-ph_0610785, fetched but Fomalhaut's individual v sin i is in the CDS-electronic-only Table 4, absent from the cached text). 7 rows are fully verified against cache: mass_msun (Mamajek 2012, 1.92±0.02 confirmed), radius_rsun (Mamajek/Absil van Belle relation + θ_LD 2.223, confirmed), teff_k (8590±73 confirmed; Davis 8819 divergence consistent), luminosity_lsun (16.63±0.48 confirmed), age_gyr (0.44±0.04; Fomalhaut A isochronal 450±40, binary mean 440±40 confirmed), stellar_oblateness_ratio (Absil 1.021 confirmed), stellar_color_temp_k (=Teff Mamajek, confirmed). The remaining 28 rows draw on sources with no cacheable text: the 19 disk rows (Kalas/Boley/White/Gáspár/Acke/Holland/Stapelfeldt/Le Bouquin/Wyatt — none in bib YAML papers list nor cache, but values match db/disks_curated.json), limb_darkening_alpha_h (Davis 2005, no arXiv, skipped), metallicity_fe_h_dex (Dunkin 1997, no arXiv, skipped), spectral_type (Gray & Garrison 1989, uncached; DB credits Keenan & McNeil 1989 instead), rotation_period_days (Royer per-star value not in cached text), and the tie-break/derived rows (tints, opacities, companion apparent diameters) which are synthesis/geometry, not paper-cited. No VALUE_MISMATCH or PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE found among the verifiable stellar rows; the report has a proper '## Canonical alternatives' section documenting the Davis-vs-Absil R/Teff divergence, so no POLICY_VIOLATION. DB has no planet entry (planets_curated.json), consistent with the report's exclusion of Fomalhaut b. Main concerns: a stale '40–80 AU' intermediate-belt figure in prose vs the corrected 60–110 AU in the table (internal contradiction + DB-superseded), the 'asteroid-belt analog' framing the DB explicitly revised, and the Mamajek 'A3V/A4V boundary' / Gray & Garrison attribution that the cache contradicts (Mamajek says plain A3V).

findings.claim: A4V — Basis: 'Gray & Garrison 1989 — MK standard; Mamajek 2012 adopts A3V/A4V boundary'.
findings.source_says: Mamajek 2012 lists Fomalhaut as plain A3V, not 'A3V/A4V boundary'. Gray & Garrison 1989 is not in the bib YAML papers list nor the _papers cache. The DB (stellar_props_curated.json) attributes the A4V spectype to Keenan & McNeil 1989 (1989ApJS...71..245K), a different paper.
findings.evidence: Cache 1206.6353.md:7 'The nearby (d = 7.7 pc) A3V star Fomalhaut' and :49 table row 'Spec. Type | A3 Va'. Absil 0908.3133.md:7/:151 calls it 'A4 V'. DB spectype_reference='Keenan & McNeil 1989'. Gray & Garrison (1989ApJS...70..623G) absent from bib YAML + cache.

findings.claim: 0.97 d DERIVED from 'v sin i ≈ 93 km/s (Royer 2007)' + R 1.842 + assumed i ≈ 65°; v sin i is the sole rotation constraint driving this decision.
findings.source_says: The cached Royer 2007 markdown (astro-ph_0610785.md) contains only per-spectral-subgroup statistical v sin i distributions; the individual per-star Table 4 (which would list Fomalhaut / HD 216956) is CDS-electronic-only and is NOT in the cache. No '93 ± 3' value and no 'Fomalhaut'/'216956' string appears anywhere in the cached text.
findings.evidence: Cache astro-ph_0610785.md: grep for '216956','fomal','93 ± 3' returns no match; the title footnote states 'Table 4 is only available in electronic form at the CDS'. The decision-driving v sin i=93 km/s cannot be confirmed offline.

findings.claim: 0.211 ± 0.020 — Davis et al. 2005 SUSI optical interferometry power-law α(V) ≈ 0.21.
findings.source_says: not in cache. Davis et al. 2005 has no arXiv preprint; bib YAML lists it as status: skipped (ads_bibcode 2005AN....326...25D) and it is absent from the _papers cache, so the 0.211 limb-darkening exponent cannot be checked offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/fomalhaut.yaml lines 32-37 (Davis 2005, arxiv_id: null, status: skipped). No Davis 2005 file in docs/phase3/_papers/.

findings.claim: −0.03 — Dunkin et al. 1997 high-resolution spectroscopy (Phase 2 recommended).
findings.source_says: not in cache. Dunkin 1997 has no arXiv preprint; bib YAML lists it status: skipped (1997MNRAS.286..604D) and it is not in the _papers cache, so the −0.03 value cannot be verified offline (it is corroborated by the DB metallicity_measurements entry but the paper text itself is uncacheable).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/fomalhaut.yaml lines 38-43 (Dunkin 1997, arxiv_id: null, status: skipped). No Dunkin file in docs/phase3/_papers/.

findings.claim: Decisions table sets the intermediate belt at 60–110 AU, but the report prose repeatedly states the intermediate belt is at '~40–80 AU' (intro line 39 and Visual styling line 254: 'The intermediate belt at 40–80 AU (Gáspár 2023 JWST)').
findings.source_says: DB disks_curated.json (canonical Phase 2) gives the intermediate belt as 60–110 AU and explicitly annotates: 'paper-precise extents, replacing earlier shorthand of 40-80 AU'. The 40-80 AU figure is superseded shorthand; the table value (60-110) is correct, the prose is stale.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/fomalhaut.md:39 and :254 say '40–80 AU'; Decisions rows :82-83 say 60 and 110. DB disks_curated.json intermediate belt: inner_radius_au 60, outer_radius_au 110, note 'replacing earlier shorthand of 40-80 AU'.

findings.claim: Report describes the inner ~10 AU belt as an 'inner asteroid-belt analog' (intro line 38) / 'inner warm asteroid-belt analog at ~10 AU' (Visual styling line 257), implying a discrete narrow warm ring.
findings.source_says: DB disks_curated.json (Gáspár 2023, recommended) morphology: 'broad drawn-out inner disk extending ~10-90 AU; not a narrow asteroid-belt analog. PR-drag-fed continuous emission rather than a discrete warm ring.' The asteroid-belt-analog framing is the pre-JWST (Su 2013) interpretation the DB explicitly revises.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/fomalhaut.md:38 'inner asteroid-belt analog at ~10 AU', :257 'inner warm asteroid-belt analog'. DB disks_curated.json inner_warm morphology note explicitly negates the asteroid-belt-analog description.

findings.claim: All disk geometry/temperature/mass/inclination rows cite Kalas 2005, Boley 2012, White 2017, Gáspár 2023, Acke 2012, Holland 2017, Stapelfeldt 2004, Le Bouquin 2009, Wyatt 2008 for cfg-decisive values (e.g. cold ring 133/158 AU, intermediate 60/110, warm 10/90, T_dust 65 K, i 65.6/67.5°, mass 0.015–4–10 M⊕).
findings.source_says: not in cache. None of these disk papers are in the bib YAML papers list (which is the stellar anchor subset: Mamajek/Absil/Royer/Davis/Dunkin only) nor in the _papers cache, so the numbers cannot be confirmed offline. They do appear with matching values + bibcodes in db/disks_curated.json, so they are sourced in the DB but uncacheable for offline text verification.
