findings.claim: Surface synthesis prose (line 113) credits the θ_LD = 2.223 ± 0.022 mas to 'VLTI/VINCI (Di Folco 2004)'.
findings.source_says: The Decisions row (line 64) correctly credits θ_LD = 2.223 mas to Absil et al. 2009 (excess-corrected). Per the Absil cache, Di Folco 2004 gave θ_UD = 2.197 mas / θ_LD ≈ 2.228 mas; the 2.223-mas excess-corrected value is Absil 2009's result, not Di Folco 2004's. The cfg-decisive cell is correct; only the prose mis-attributes.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/fomalhaut.md:113-115 vs :64. Cache 0908.3133.md:20-21 (Di Folco UD 2.197 / LD 2.228) and :151 'updated mean limb-darkened diameter θ_LD = 2.223 ± 0.022 mas' from the Absil VINCI re-analysis.

gj-9066-c.coverage_note: 36 Decisions rows audited. 30 fully verified. CACHEABLE/VERIFIED decision-driving sources: the orbital + mass rows (eccentricity 0.46+/-0.04, argument_of_periastron 321.79+/-5.32, sidereal_period 771.36+/-1.34, semi_major_axis 0.88+/-0.02, mass_msini_mjup 0.21+/-0.02, mass_msini_mearth 66.74+/-6.36) all match Quirrenbach et al. 2022 GP-model Table 4 in docs/phase3/_papers/2203.16504.md:241-256 (the adopted right-column values) AND db/planets_curated.json:3934-3995 (Quirrenbach recommended:true). The Feng et al. 2020 alternates cited in-cell (e=0.33+/-0.10, P=767.887+/-7.5 d, a=0.87+/-0.04, Msini=0.225+/-0.032 MJup=71.6+/-10.3 Mearth) all match docs/phase3/_papers/2008.07998.md:107 (Table 2) and DB recommended:false. The factor-of-seven insolation quote and 'second giant planet around <0.3 Msun (with GJ 3512 b)' both verbatim-confirmed in 2203.16504.md:7,319,323. All derived rows recomputed and confirmed: periastron 0.475 / apoastron 1.285 AU; insolation 0.0033/0.011/0.0015 S_earth; T_eq 66/90/55 K (paper's own equilibrium estimate ~65 K matches, 2203.16504.md:325); angular diameters 0.099/0.184/0.068 deg from R_star=0.1646 Rsun (db/stellar_props_curated.json:3606). Host L=0.00254 Lsun, Teff=3100 K, spectype M4.5 V all match the recommended DB values. NOT CACHEABLE / could-not-verify-offline (3 rows): radius_rjup (Thorngren 2016 = arXiv:1511.07854 absent from cache AND bib YAML), atmosphere_composition (Lodders 2003, no arXiv, not cached), bond_albedo (Li 2018 not cited anywhere in bib/cache = UNSOURCED). All three are explicitly low-confidence tie-break aesthetic rows, non-decisive. Two arithmetic self-inconsistencies (density 0.6 vs formula 0.45; scale-height g=8.9 vs correct 7.2) and one PHASE2_DESYNC (radius_rearth falsely attributed to a DB radius that does not exist). No tidal-lock / lava / reflexive-aurora physical-implausibility issues; tidally_locked=false on the e=0.46 orbit is correct, equilibrium-temp rows are self-consistent with L and a. No POLICY_VIOLATION: all non-measured rows correctly labeled 'Tie-break', no 'divergence' language, no Canonical-alternatives section required, ring variant properly deferred to Open items.

findings.claim: radius_rearth = 9.6, Basis: '= 0.85 R_Jup x 11.21 (DB Phase 2 stores 9.6 R⊕ as the radius estimate, also low-confidence/RV-only)'
findings.source_says: db/planets_curated.json GJ 9066 c has NO radius field at all. The entry contains only 'orbital' and 'physical' (mass_mearth) blocks; no radius_rearth/radius_rjup is stored for this RV-only, non-transiting planet.
findings.evidence: db/planets_curated.json:3934-3995 (GJ 9066 c entry has keys pl_name/orbital/physical only; physical entries carry mass_mearth 66.74 and 71.6, no radius). Report claim at docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:59. The report fabricates a DB-stored radius that does not exist; secondary arithmetic slip: 0.85 x 11.209 = 9.53, rounds to 9.5 not 9.6.

findings.claim: density_g_cc = 0.6, Basis: 'derived: 0.21 M_Jup / (0.85 R_Jup)³ x ρ_Jup ≈ 0.6 g/cc; Saturn-like'
findings.source_says: The report's own stated formula evaluates to ~0.45 g/cc, not 0.6: (0.21/0.85³) x ρ_Jup(1.326 g/cc) = 0.342 x 1.326 = 0.45 g/cc. To obtain 0.6 g/cc the radius would have to be ~0.77 R_Jup, contradicting the adopted 0.85 R_Jup. 0.6 g/cc is closer to Saturn's mean density (0.69 g/cc) than the formula result, so the round value appears to be an unstated Saturn-analog pick rather than the cited derivation.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:60. Recomputed: 0.21 MJup at 0.85 RJup gives mean density ~0.45 g/cc; Jupiter mean density 1.326 g/cc, Saturn 0.69 g/cc. Low-confidence row (inherits radius low-confidence).

findings.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = 35, Basis: 'derived: H = kT/(μ m_H g) with T ≈ 66 K, μ = 2.3, g ≈ 8.9 m/s² (from 0.21 M_Jup, 0.85 R_Jup)'
findings.source_says: Surface gravity for 0.21 M_Jup at 0.85 R_Jup is g = 7.2 m/s², not 8.9 m/s² (overstated ~24%). The stated g = 8.9 m/s² would give H = 26.6 km, not 35 km; the reported H = 35 km is instead consistent with the correct g = 7.2 m/s² (H = 32.9 km ≈ 35). So the cited intermediate g is wrong while the final value is roughly right.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:71 and prose docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:139. Recomputed: g = G(0.21·MJup)/(0.85·RJup)² = 7.20 m/s²; H(g=7.2)=32.9 km, H(g=8.9)=26.6 km. Medium-confidence row; final value defensible, cited g is the error.

findings.claim: bond_albedo = 0.34, Basis: 'Tie-break: Saturn-analog 0.34 (Li 2018) for a cold ammonia-cloud sub-Saturn'
findings.source_says: not in cache; 'Li 2018' appears nowhere in the report's ## Bibliography section, is not listed in docs/phase3/_bib/gj-9066.yaml (which contains only 5 papers: Quirrenbach 2022, Feng 2020, Shan 2024, Cifuentes 2020, Schweitzer 2019), and has no entry in docs/phase3/_papers/. The citation could never have been verified offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:67 cites 'Li 2018'; grep of the report (no 'Li' in Bibliography) and docs/phase3/_bib/gj-9066.yaml:13-47 (no Li entry). Low-confidence aesthetic tie-break (Saturn Bond albedo ~0.34 is a real value, but the citation is unsourced in this project).

findings.claim: radius_rjup = 0.85, Basis: 'mass-radius relations (Saturn = 0.299 M_Jup, 0.843 R_Jup; Thorngren 2016 for cold sub-Saturns) bracket ~0.8–0.9 R_Jup'
findings.source_says: not in cache; Thorngren et al. 2016 (arXiv:1511.07854) is absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ and is NOT listed in docs/phase3/_bib/gj-9066.yaml (only appears in the report's prose Bibliography 'context' list). The specific claim that Thorngren 2016 cold-giant tracks bracket 0.8-0.9 R_Jup cannot be checked offline. The Saturn anchor facts cited (0.299 M_Jup, 0.843 R_Jup) are independently correct.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:58 and :121; cache check confirms 1511.07854.md MISSING; docs/phase3/_bib/gj-9066.yaml has no Thorngren entry. Low-confidence model-inferred row, explicitly flagged 'radius is entirely model-inferred'.

findings.claim: atmosphere_composition H₂ ~88%, He ~10%, CH₄ ~0.3%, NH₃ ~0.02%, trace H₂O, Basis: 'Solar-composition sub-Saturn default (Lodders 2003 protosolar)'
findings.source_says: not in cache; Lodders 2003 (ApJ 591, 1220; bibcode 2003ApJ...591.1220L) has no arXiv id, is not in docs/phase3/_bib/gj-9066.yaml, and is absent from docs/phase3/_papers/. The protosolar-abundance basis cannot be verified offline. Row is an explicit no-spectrum tie-break default.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/gj-9066-c.md:70 and :132; bib YAML lists no Lodders entry; cache has no 2003ApJ...591.1220L. Low-confidence aesthetic default, non-decisive.

hd-219134-c.coverage_note: 33 Decisions rows total; 27 fully verified. Cacheable & confirmed sources: Ligi et al. 2019 (docs/phase3/_papers/1909.10058.md, full text — confirms c radius alt 1.415±0.049, Gillon b/c masses/radii, R★=0.726, M★=0.696, L via stellar DB), Motalebi 2015 (1507.08532.md, full), Vogt 2015 (1509.07912.md, full). DB cross-checks all passed: db/planets_curated.json confirms c e=0.062, P=6.765 d, a=0.064 AU, M=4.36±0.22, R=1.511±0.047 (recommended) + Ligi 1.415 (recommended:false); db/stellar_props_curated.json confirms Teff/color-temp 4858 K, R★ 0.726, L 0.264; inclination 87.28 present in db/systems/hd_219134.json:278 (matches 'DB raw' attribution). No PHASE2_DESYNC. All numeric derivations reproduce (g=1.91, ρ=6.97≈7.0, S=64.5, T_eq(A=0)=788.5≈789, T_eq(A=0.3)=721, angular dia=6.05≈6.0). PARTIAL cache: Gillon et al. 2017 (1703.01430.md) is abstract-only (full text 'unavailable') — its quoted M/R for c ARE in the abstract and confirmed, but its T_eq=782 is not. UNCACHEABLE decision-driving sources (could never be verified offline): Owen & Wu 2017 (arXiv:1705.10810, absent), Schaefer & Fegley 2009 (arXiv:0905.4045, absent), Bolmont 2020 (2020A&A...644A.165B — not in bib YAML, no arXiv, absent from cache → UNSOURCED), Hut 1981 (no arXiv, pre-print). No '## Canonical alternatives' section exists, but none is required — all non-measured rows are correctly labeled 'Tie-break' within an allowed window and the report never claims 'divergence'; the Ligi alt radius is a recommended:false measurement, not a canonical/variant cfg split. The one substantive analysis error is dayside_surface_temp_k (970 K vs the ~1086 K its own stated formula produces), mirrored in the sibling b report.

findings.claim: dayside_surface_temp_k = 970, with Basis stating the formula 'airless substellar peak T_sub ≈ T_eq · √2 · (1−A)^0.25 ≈ 970 K'. The 970 K value is reused in the Surface synthesis (line 82), Morphology (line 102), Visual styling (line 167), and underpins the ocean_present/surface_ice_caps rows.
findings.source_says: The report's own stated formula does NOT yield 970 K. With the report's T_eq(A=0)=789 K (which I confirmed = 788.5 K) and bond_albedo A=0.1: 788.5 × √2 × (1−0.1)^0.25 = 1086 K. Even at A=0 it gives 1115 K. 970 K is ~11% below the formula result (970/1086 = 0.89). The sibling b report shows the identical defect: its stated formula gives 1406 K but it quotes 1250 K (ratio 0.89), so both reports apply an undocumented ~0.89 factor not present in the written formula.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:46 ('T_sub ≈ T_eq · √2 · (1−A)^0.25 ≈ 970 K'); cross-check docs/phase3/hd-219134-b.md:50 (same formula → quoted 1250 K, computed 1406 K). Numeric: 788.5*1.41421*0.9**0.25 = 1086 K. NOTE: the qualitative 'below basalt solidus ~1300 K, no surface melt' conclusion survives even at the correct 1086 K, so this is not cfg-decisive for the melt narrative — but the decision value itself is inconsistent with its cited derivation.

findings.claim: tidal_heating_w_m2 = 0.05–0.3, Basis 'Bolmont 2020 scaling at e = 0.062'. Also cited in the rotation prose (line 156).
findings.source_says: not in cache. Bolmont et al. 2020 (bibcode 2020A&A...644A.165B) is NOT listed in the host bib YAML (docs/phase3/_bib/hd-219134.yaml lists only Ligi 2019, Gillon 2017, Motalebi 2015, Vogt 2015, Johnson 2016), is cited in the report bibliography with NO arXiv id (line 213: 'Bolmont E. et al. 2020 — (2020A&A...644A.165B)'), and has no full text in docs/phase3/_papers/. It could never have been verified offline. (A file 2004.13731.md exists but is Leung et al. 'Ocean Loss for M Dwarf Terrestrial Exoplanets', not Bolmont.)
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:61; docs/phase3/_bib/hd-219134.yaml (papers list has no Bolmont entry); docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:213 (no arXiv); ls docs/phase3/_papers/ confirms no Bolmont 644.A165 md.

findings.claim: atmosphere_present = false; Basis cites 'H/He photoevaporated (Owen & Wu 2017)' and the report bibliography prints arXiv:1705.10810 for Owen & Wu 2017.
findings.source_says: not in cache. arXiv:1705.10810 (Owen & Wu 2017, The evaporation valley) has no .md/.html in docs/phase3/_papers/, so the photoevaporation claim ('places a 4.4 M⊕ core at 0.064 AU in the core-only regime') cannot be checked offline. The decision is co-supported by the measured 7.0 g/cc density (which IS verified via Gillon abstract + Ligi text), so the conclusion is not solely dependent on the uncacheable paper.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:48 and bibliography line 193–196 (arXiv:1705.10810); ls docs/phase3/_papers/ shows 1705.10810 absent.

findings.claim: atmosphere_composition = 'n/a (airless; trace surface-sourced exosphere)'; Atmosphere-synthesis prose attributes the exosphere scaling to 'Schaefer & Fegley 2009' (report bibliography prints arXiv:0905.4045).
findings.source_says: not in cache. arXiv:0905.4045 (Schaefer & Fegley 2009, silicate atmospheres of evaporating super-Earths) is absent from docs/phase3/_papers/, so the exosphere chemistry claim cannot be verified offline. Low impact: the cfg value is simply 'airless', a tie-break, and does not hinge on the exact scaling.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:50 and line 119–123; bibliography line 197–200 (arXiv:0905.4045); ls docs/phase3/_papers/ shows 0905.4045 absent.

findings.claim: equilibrium_temp_k (A=0) = 789, Basis 'project formula 278.3·L^0.25/√a; Gillon 2017 / TEPCat give 782 K'.
findings.source_says: The cfg value 789 is correctly derived (I reproduce 788.5 from L=0.264, a=0.064). However the corroborating '782 K' attributed to Gillon 2017 / TEPCat is unverifiable: the Gillon 2017 cache (docs/phase3/_papers/1703.01430.md) is only the arXiv landing-page abstract ('ar5iv full-text HTML unavailable for this paper') and contains no T_eq for c; grep for '782' returns nothing, and TEPCat is not a cached paper. The sub-claim is a context note, not the cfg value.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:43; docs/phase3/_papers/1703.01430.md (7-line abstract stub, no T_eq); grep '782' in 1703.01430.md returns empty.

findings.claim: tidally_locked = true (cfg adopts a hard 1:1 spin–orbit resonance), Basis 'Hut 1981' locking timescale, on an orbit with curated eccentricity e = 0.062.
findings.source_says: Per the audit's physical-plausibility rule, a forced 1:1 lock on an eccentric (e>0.01) orbit is flagged: e=0.062 > 0.01, and the Decisions table commits to 1:1. Mitigating: the report does NOT assert this reflexively — the Rotation synthesis (lines 139–145) explicitly raises the 3:2 (Mercury-analog) possibility and preserves it as a cfg variant in Open items (lines 237–240), arguing 1:1 is the likely endpoint via eccentricity damping in a compact chain. Hut 1981 has no arXiv/cache (pre-print era) so the timescale itself is uncacheable. Low severity given the explicit justification and preserved variant.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-c.md:32 (table), 139–145 (3:2 discussion), 237–240 (3:2 variant in Open items); curated e=0.062 from db/planets_curated.json HD 219134 c orbital block.

alpha-centauri-a.coverage_note: 17 Decisions rows. Cacheable+confirmed sources: mass_msun (Kervella 2016, 1610.06079), radius_rsun + limb_darkening_alpha_h (Kervella 2017, 1610.06185), age_gyr (Joyce 2018, 1806.07567), activity_cycle_years + the X-ray rows (Robrade 2016, 1612.06570), rotation + log_rhk (DeWarf 2010, 1009.1652 — present in cache). 3 rows cite Porto de Mello 2008 (spectral_type, teff_k, metallicity_fe_h_dex): the report prints bibcode 2008A&A...488..653P and the bib YAML maps it to arXiv:0804.3712, but NO 0804.3712.md exists in _papers/ — so the cited paper's full text is NOT in the offline cache and these three rows are UNVERIFIABLE against source (values do match db/stellar_props_curated.json recommended entries). 4 rows are pure tie-break/derived with no external source (hex tint, stellar_color_temp_k, apparent_diameter, corona event). DeWarf 2010 IS cached but is fundamentally about alpha Cen B; its A-specific claims (rotation, log R'HK) are absent or contradicted. Counted 9 rows fully verified; remaining 8 are either source-absent (Porto de Mello x3), contradicted by the cached paper (rotation, both X-ray rows, log_rhk), or internally inconsistent (apparent_diameter). A separate high-severity prose/Open-Items claim of a DB '4.81 Gyr erratum' is fabricated — db/stellar_props_curated.json stores 5.3 Gyr.

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_max = 27.6 (cgs), attributed to 'Robrade 2016 cycle maximum'; report further states alpha Cen A X-ray cycles by a factor of ~4 over the 27.0-27.6 range.
findings.source_says: Robrade 2016 (1612.06570) gives alpha Cen A's only quoted X-ray luminosity as L_X ~ 6e26 erg/s (log L_X ~ 26.78) at the 2003 detection, then a dimming to XMM non-detection; it explicitly states 'a clear maximum has not been covered yet' for A and the cycle amplitude L_Xmax/L_Xmin is 'nearly one order of magnitude'. The 4e27 erg/s (log 27.6) value in the paper belongs to alpha Cen B (2012 maximum), not A.
findings.evidence: 1612.06570.md line 31: 'alpha Cen A was clearly detected in the first XMM observation in 2003 with L_X ~ 6 x 10^26 erg s-1'; line 35: 'cycle likely has a period of about 15-20 years ... an X-ray luminosity ratio L_Xmax/L_Xmin of nearly one order of magnitude ... a clear maximum has not been covered yet'; line 51: 'we derive for alpha Cen B an X-ray luminosity of L_X ~ 4 x 10^27 erg s-1'. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:58.

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_min = 27.0 (cgs), attributed to 'Robrade 2016 cycle minimum'.
findings.source_says: Robrade 2016 reports alpha Cen A faded to extreme X-ray faintness with XMM NON-DETECTIONS at minimum (2005-2012), i.e. log L_X well below 27.0; the highest A value the paper states is ~6e26 (log ~26.78). A floor of log L_X = 27.0 is not supported and is inconsistent with the paper's 'nearly one order of magnitude' amplitude from a ~6e26 peak.
findings.evidence: 1612.06570.md line 31: 'remained extremely X-ray faint from about 2005-2012 ... even non-detections with XMM are present'. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:57 and prose line 102-103 ('log L_X = 27.0-27.6 ... cycles by a factor of ~4').

findings.claim: rotation_period_days = 22 +/- 3, Basis 'DeWarf 2010 - photometric variability'.
findings.source_says: DeWarf et al. 2010 (1009.1652) explicitly gives alpha Cen A's rotation period as ~15-20 days (the paper is primarily about alpha Cen B, whose rotation it determines as 36.2 +/- 1.4 d). 22 d lies above the paper's stated 15-20 d range for A. (Note: db/stellar_props_curated.json stores 22.0+/-3.0 attributed to DeWarf 2010, so report+DB agree but both diverge from the cited paper's A value.)
findings.evidence: 1009.1652.md line 29 (Sec.1): 'the rotation periods of alpha Cen A, B, C are observed to be ~15-20, 36.2 +/- 1.4, and 83.1 +/- 0.6 days, respectively.' Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:54.

findings.claim: activity_log_rhk = -4.95, Basis 'DeWarf 2010 - chromospheric Ca II H&K'.
findings.source_says: The cached DeWarf 2010 paper contains NO log R'HK value for alpha Cen A. It studies alpha Cen B via Mg II h+k and C IV (not Ca II H&K for A); R'HK appears only in a referee comment about B's Saar & Osten estimate. No '-4.95' string appears anywhere in the cached text. The value cannot be confirmed from the cited paper. (db stores -4.95 attributed to DeWarf 2010, but the paper does not support it.)
findings.evidence: 1009.1652.md: grep for '4.95' returns no match; R'HK discussed only at line 131 in the context of alpha Cen B. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:55.

findings.claim: visual_in_planet_sky_apparent_diameter_arcmin = 0.5, labeled '(from alpha Cen B at 23 AU mean)', Basis 'derived: 2 R*/a x (180*60/pi)'.
findings.source_says: The stated formula with the stated 23 AU distance does not yield 0.5 arcmin. For companion B (R=0.8632 Rsun) at 23 AU: 2*0.8632*0.004650/23 rad x 3437.75 = ~1.2 arcmin (for A, ~1.7 arcmin). The value 0.5 is actually the DEGREE figure for the host star seen from a 1.25 AU HZ planet (Visual styling line 151: 'fills 0.5 deg angular diameter'). The Decisions row also contradicts the report's own prose, which puts B's disk at ~0.04 deg (=2.4 arcmin) at periastron - i.e. ~1.2 arcmin at the 23 AU mean, not 0.5 arcmin. Unit (arcmin vs deg) and body are conflated.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:62 (row, unit arcmin, 23 AU, value 0.5) vs :151 ('the star fills 0.5 deg angular diameter') and :157 ('apparent stellar disk of ~0.04 deg at periastron').

findings.claim: Report prose (lines 21-24) and Open items (266-270) assert the DB stores age value_gyr = 4.81 +/- 0.5 for Joyce & Chaboyer 2018 (a 'less-correct' value / 'DB erratum') and that this synthesis corrects it to the 5.3 +/- 0.3 paper headline.
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json -> 'Alpha Centauri A'.age_measurements: the Joyce & Chaboyer 2018 entry is {value_gyr: 5.3, uncertainty_gyr: 0.3, recommended: true}. No 4.81 +/- 0.5 entry exists anywhere in the age_measurements list (others are Bazot 6.52, Bazot 4.4+/-1.1, Thevenin 6.41). The asserted DB erratum is fabricated; the DB already stores 5.3.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json age_measurements[0] = Joyce & Chaboyer 2018 value_gyr 5.3 +/- 0.3 recommended. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:21-24 and :266-270. (The Decisions age_gyr=5.3 row itself is correct and verified against 1806.07567.)

findings.claim: teff_k = 5847 +/- 27, Basis 'Porto de Mello 2008 - high-resolution spectroscopy, differential to Sun'.
findings.source_says: Porto de Mello et al. 2008 is NOT in the _papers cache (bibcode 2008A&A...488..653P maps to arXiv:0804.3712 in the bib YAML, but no 0804.3712.md exists; the bib marks it status: skipped). Full text is absent offline, so the 5847+/-27 value cannot be confirmed against the source. It does match db/stellar_props_curated.json teff_measurements recommended (5847, Porto de Mello 2008). Decision-driving (feeds stellar_color_temp_k).
findings.evidence: ls docs/phase3/_papers/0804.3712* -> no matches; bib YAML id068 arxiv_id 0804.3712 status: skipped (below_score_threshold). Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:50.

findings.source_says: Porto de Mello 2008 is not in the _papers cache, so the citation itself is unverifiable offline. However G2V for alpha Cen A is independently corroborated in cached Kervella 2017 (1610.06185) and matches db spectype 'G2 V', so the value is not in doubt - only the specific citation is uncheckable.

findings.claim: metallicity_fe_h_dex = +0.24 +/- 0.03, Basis 'Porto de Mello 2008 - adopted in Joyce 2018'.
findings.source_says: Porto de Mello 2008 not in _papers cache -> value not checkable against the primary source. Matches db metallicity_measurements recommended (0.24 +/- 0.03, Porto de Mello 2008); cached DeWarf 2010 corroborates [Fe/H] ~ 0.25 for the system citing Porto de Mello 2008. findings.evidence: 0804.3712 absent from _papers/; corroboration 1009.1652.md line 27 '[Fe/H] ~ 0.25 ... Porto de Mello et al. 2008'. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:52.

findings.claim: luminosity_lsun = 1.521, Basis 'derived from R and Teff via Stefan-Boltzmann; Bigot 2008 agrees'.
findings.source_says: The value 1.521 is correct (db luminosity_measurements: 1.521 +/- 0.013, method bolometric_flux, Kervella et al. 2017) - it is a measured bolometric-flux luminosity, not a Stefan-Boltzmann derivation, and the corroborating reference cited as 'Bigot 2008' is listed in the report's own bibliography as 'Bigot L. et al. 2006' (no Bigot 2008 entry exists). Minor source/method labeling error; the number is right.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json luminosity_measurements[0] = 1.521 Kervella 2017 bolometric_flux. Report Basis docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:51 vs bibliography :228 ('Bigot L. et al. 2006').

findings.source_says: Cached Bedding 2004 (astro-ph_0406471) abstract: 'we extract 42 oscillation frequencies with l=0-3'. The figure of 28 modes belongs to Bouchy & Carrier 2002, not Bedding 2004. (Not a Decisions row; methodology/context citation only.)
findings.evidence: astro-ph_0406471.md line 7 'we extract 42 oscillation frequencies'; line 11 'Bouchy & Carrier (2002) published frequencies for 28 modes'. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:222-224.

findings.claim: Intro (lines 32-33) describes the MIRI candidate point source 'at ~1.5 AU separation'.
findings.source_says: Beichman 2025 (2508.03814) reports S1 at a separation of 1.5 ARCSEC (1.51" radial) from alpha Cen A, not 1.5 AU; at 1.33 pc that projects to ~2.0 AU. The 1.5 figure is an angular separation, not a physical AU separation. (Prose/Open-items only; not a Decisions row.)
findings.evidence: 2508.03814.md line 7 'a F_nu(15.5um)=3.5 mJy point source, called S1, at a separation of 1.5" from alpha Cen A'; line 82 table '(1.51 +/- 0.13, 83.5 ...)' arcsec. Report docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-a.md:32-33.

eps-ind-a.coverage_note: 18 Decisions rows total. 16 fully verified against cached sources. Cacheable/decision-driving sources all present and matched: Lundkvist 2024 (2403.04509.md) verifies spectral_type K5 V (title), mass 0.782±0.023 + Demory 0.762±0.038, radius 0.713±0.006 (θ_LD 1.817 mas, d=3.648 pc), Teff 4700±65 (median 4649), [Fe/H] −0.17, activity cycle ~2600 d (=7.12 yr), ν_max 5265±110; Feng 2019 (1910.06804.md) verifies luminosity 0.239±0.001 (Table 2) and planet a=11.55 AU/3.25 M_Jup; Feng 2018 (1803.08163.md) verifies rotation ~35 d verbatim + supersession of 22 d (Saar&Osten); Chen 2022 (2205.08077.md) verifies log R'HK=−4.72 (Pace 2013), R_X=−5.62, age 3.48(+0.78/−1.03) Gyr, BD masses 66.92/53.25 M_Jup (T1–1.5/T6), 'star lacks a published photometric rotation period'; Matthews 2026 (2603.08780.md) verifies planet b 7.63 M_Jup, a=20.9 AU, e=0.244, ~275 K, ammonia + water-ice clouds. DB cross-check: db/stellar_props_curated.json 'eps Ind A' and db/planets_curated.json 'eps Ind A' (planet b recommended = Matthews 2026, 2425 M_Earth=7.63 M_Jup) match the report exactly — no PHASE2_DESYNC. Matthews 2024 (2503.01599.md) is a 1-byte stub (context-only, report acknowledges); not decision-driving, so not counted against coverage. Only 2 rows not fully verified: apparent_magnitude_v_from_earth (no V/G→V source in any cached paper — UNVERIFIABLE, low) and a minor distance label inconsistency (3.638 vs 3.648 pc, both correctly sourced — INTERNAL_CONTRADICTION, low). Tie-break rows (visual_surface_tint_hex, visual_spot_coverage_max) are properly labeled tie-breaks, not divergences — no POLICY_VIOLATION. No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE issues (stellar synthesis; SB check L≈0.22 vs measured 0.239 is consistent within Teff sensitivity; no lava/lock/aurora claims).

findings.claim: apparent_magnitude_v_from_earth = 4.66, Basis: 'Gaia DR3 V (converted); a naked-eye star in Indus' (docs/phase3/eps-ind-a.md:69)
findings.source_says: not in cache — no cited paper reports a Johnson V or a Gaia G→V conversion for eps Ind A. The only photometric magnitude in any cached paper is Tycho V_T = 4.826 ± 0.009 mag (Chen 2022, 2205.08077.md:27), which is not the claimed 4.66 and is not a converted Johnson V.
findings.evidence: 2205.08077.md:27 'V_T = 4.826 ± 0.009 mag from the Tycho-2 catalog'; the report's Basis cites only 'Gaia DR3 V (converted)', and no Gaia G/BP/RP photometry or G→V transform appears in any cached paper (2403.04509, 1910.06804, 1803.08163, 2205.08077, 2603.08780). Value is plausible (≈true Johnson V ~4.69) but cannot be confirmed offline.

findings.claim: distance_pc = 3.638 (intro+row both say '3.638 pc, Gaia DR3 parallax 274.84 ± 0.10 mas', docs/phase3/eps-ind-a.md:5,70), while the radius_rsun row states 'Gaia DR3 d = 3.648 pc' (docs/phase3/eps-ind-a.md:55)
findings.source_says: Both distances are individually correct to their sources: Chen 2022 gives Gaia EDR3 trig distance 3.638 ± 0.001 pc (2205.08077.md:13); Lundkvist 2024 uses Gaia DR3 distance_gspphot d = 3.648 (+0.022/−0.009) pc for the radius (2403.04509.md:88). The report labels both 'Gaia DR3' and never reconciles the 0.3% difference, so the same body carries two distances in one report.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/eps-ind-a.md:55 'Gaia DR3 d = 3.648 pc' vs docs/phase3/eps-ind-a.md:70 'distance_pc 3.638'; sources 2403.04509.md:88 (d=3.648 gspphot) and 2205.08077.md:13 (3.638±0.001 pc EDR3). Arithmetic on the distance row is self-consistent (1/274.84 mas = 3.6385 pc), so this is a labeling/reconciliation gap, not a wrong number.

gj-9066.coverage_note: 17 Decisions rows; 15 fully verified against an in-cache source. All 5 papers cited in the report's Bibliography are listed in docs/phase3/_bib/gj-9066.yaml with status:fetched, but only 4 have correct full text in the _papers cache. Quirrenbach 2022 (2203.16504, primary anchor), Feng 2020 (2008.07998), Cifuentes 2020 (2007.15077), and Shan 2024 (2401.09550) are all correctly cached and verified: Quirrenbach Table 1 / Table 4 / Sect 5-6 confirm distance 4.470±0.001 pc (line 50), parallax 223.732±0.070 mas (line 49), Gaia G=10.6811 (line 46), Teff 3154±54 Passegger / [Fe/H] -0.13±0.19 Pa19 / -0.25±0.14 Ma21 (lines 59-62), v sin i <2 km/s R18 (line 63), L 0.00254±0.00002 Schw19 (line 67), Halpha pEW' -1.906±0.025 Schf19 (line 69), log R'HK -4.79±0.42 AD17 / -4.16 BS18 / -4.46 Pe21 (lines 72-74), P_rot 1.96±0.02 d (lines 76, 169), planet c P=771.36 d e=0.46 omega=321.79 a=0.88 AU msini=0.21 MJup (Table 4, lines 245-250), age >=Hyades ~750 Myr (line 31), white-light flares dV=0.87 ASAS-SN Rodriguez Martinez 2020 (line 173), 'significantly structured photosphere' (line 173). Feng confirms M4.5V spectype and the 30.9/71.6 Mearth b/c candidates (lines 32,105,107). Cifuentes confirms 3100 K for the M4.5 V class (line 331). Shan confirms TZ Ari as the lone RV-discovered fast-rotating (<15 d) planet host (lines 11, 307). DB cross-check (db/stellar_props_curated.json, db/planets_curated.json) shows NO Phase 2 desync: every recommended value matches and only planet c is carried (b correctly dropped as refuted). The ONLY defect: the bib maps arXiv:1904.06860 to Schweitzer et al. 2019, but the cached file 1904.06860.md is the wrong paper (COSINE-100 solar-axion search), so the two precise Schweitzer-sourced cfg-decisive values (mass 0.1478, radius 0.1646) and the Schweitzer Teff alternative (3193 K) lack their primary full-text source offline. All three are independently corroborated by the in-cache Quirrenbach 2022 Table 1 (rounded forms) and match the DB exactly, so this is a cache-integrity / regenerate-the-cache issue, not a value error or fabrication. The two adopted decision-driving rows (mass, radius) are flagged UNVERIFIABLE; the Schweitzer 3193 K Teff is a non-adopted alternative (adopted Teff 3100 is verified via Cifuentes) so the teff_k row is counted as verified.

findings.claim: mass_msun = 0.1478 ± 0.0036, Basis: Schweitzer et al. 2019 (CARMENES, M-Mks relation + SED radius), bibcode 2019A&A...625A..68S / arXiv:1904.06860; rounded to 0.150 ± 0.010 in Quirrenbach 2022 Table 1.
findings.source_says: The cached file resolved from arXiv:1904.06860 is NOT Schweitzer et al. 2019. It is 'A search for solar axion induced signals with COSINE-100' (COSINE-100 Collaboration, P. Adhikari et al.) — an entirely unrelated particle-physics paper with zero M-dwarf / CARMENES content. The precise 0.1478 ± 0.0036 value cannot be checked offline. The rounded form (0.150 ± 0.010, ref 'Schw19') IS independently confirmed in the in-cache Quirrenbach 2022 Table 1, and the precise value matches db/stellar_props_curated.json (recommended).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1904.06860.md:1 title '# A search for solar axion induced signals with COSINE-100'; grep for 'M dwarf|CARMENES|Stefan|Mks' returns nothing (file is 85 lines, all axion physics). Rounded corroboration: docs/phase3/_papers/2203.16504.md:65 'M_star [M_sun] | ... | 0.150 ± 0.010 | Schw19'. Report cites the id at docs/phase3/gj-9066.md:260 (arXiv:1904.06860).

findings.claim: radius_rsun = 0.1646 ± 0.0053, Basis: Schweitzer et al. 2019 (SED fitting / Stefan-Boltzmann), bibcode 2019A&A...625A..68S / arXiv:1904.06860; rounded to 0.164 ± 0.005 in Quirrenbach 2022 Table 1.
findings.source_says: Same cache-mapping defect: arXiv:1904.06860 in the cache is the COSINE-100 solar-axion paper, not Schweitzer et al. 2019, so the precise 0.1646 ± 0.0053 SED radius cannot be verified against the Schweitzer text offline. The rounded value (0.164 ± 0.005, ref 'Schw19') IS confirmed in the in-cache Quirrenbach 2022 Table 1, and the precise value plus its VizieR provenance (J/A+A/625/A68/tableb1) match db/stellar_props_curated.json (recommended).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1904.06860.md:1 (COSINE-100 axion paper, wrong content). Rounded corroboration: docs/phase3/_papers/2203.16504.md:66 'R_star [R_sun] | ... | 0.164 ± 0.005 | Schw19'. Report cites id at docs/phase3/gj-9066.md:260.

eps-ind-a-b.coverage_note: All 35 Decisions rows had a cacheable or DB-resolvable source. The cornerstone paper Matthews et al. 2026 (arXiv:2603.08780) is fully present in docs/phase3/_papers/2603.08780.md and confirms verbatim every measured orbital/atmosphere row: e=0.244 (+0.11/-0.083), a=20.9 (+5.8/-3.3) AU, i=102.3 (+1.9/-1.7), Omega=44.6+/-1.3, omega=62 (+48/-27), P=108 (+49/-25) yr (=39447 days), M=7.63 (+0.73/-0.70) M_Jup, '1.9sigma lower than Feng 2025', separation 3.654 arcsec, NH3 at 11sigma from F1065C-F1140C=0.88+/-0.08 mag, preferred thick water-ice clouds, best-fit 275 K / log g 4.5 / 3x solar [M/H] / 2.5x solar C/O / f_sed 6 / H2O column tau=416 reaching tau=1 near 0.7 bar, WISE 0855 near-identical mid-IR color. Feng 2019 (1910.06804) confirms L=0.239 L_sun, the superseded a~11.6 AU / 3.25 M_Jup / P~45 yr. Feng 2018 (1803.08163) and DB confirm the superseded Msini ~2.71 M_Jup. Chen 2022 (2205.08077) confirms the BD pair masses, types, separation, and 3.5 Gyr age. Host params (Teff 4700, M 0.782, R 0.713, L 0.239) verified against db/stellar_props_curated.json. Derived rows insolation (5.5e-4), equilibrium_temp (~43 K A=0), angular_diameter (0.018 deg), surface_gravity (~15 g_earth, log g 4.18), mass_mearth (7.63*317.8=2425), period yr (39447/365.25=108) all recompute correctly. Only Matthews et al. 2024 (arXiv:2503.01599) is absent from cache (1-byte/untitled HTML stub, as the report itself flags) — but every row that cites it is co-cited with Matthews 2026, so no row is left UNVERIFIABLE. No PHASE2_DESYNC: the report's recommended values match db/planets_curated.json exactly. The four findings are: a density derivation error (~5.3 vs correct ~6.7 g/cc), a scale-height value (~25 km) inconsistent with its own stated inputs (~6.6 km), three rows mis-attributing the 12.6 R⊕ radius to the DB (which has no radius for this planet), and a minor Bb-mass rounding (53.3 vs 53.25).

findings.claim: density_g_cc = ~5.3 g/cc; Basis: 'derived: 7.63 M_Jup in a 1.12 R_Jup sphere ~5.3 g/cc'.
findings.source_says: A self-consistent recomputation of 7.63 M_Jup (1.448e28 kg) in a sphere of 1.12 R_Jup gives ~6.7 g/cc, not ~5.3. (Sanity check: 1 M_Jup at 1 R_Jup with the same constants returns ~1.24 g/cc, consistent with Jupiter's true 1.33, so the method is sound and 5.3 is ~21% too low.) The stated inputs do not produce 5.3.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/eps-ind-a-b.md:63 ('7.63 M_Jup in a 1.12 R_Jup sphere ~5.3 g/cc'); my computation rho = M / (4/3 pi R^3) with M=7.63*1.898e27 kg, R=1.12*7.1492e7 m -> 6.73 g/cc.

findings.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = ~25 km; Basis: 'H = kT/(mu m_H g) with T = 275 K, mu = 2.3, g ~ 15 m/s2-equivalent (~150 m/s2) -> ~25 km'.
findings.source_says: The basis's own stated inputs do not yield 25 km. With g = 150 m/s2 (the value the basis states it uses), H = kT/(mu m_H g) = (1.381e-23 * 275)/(2.3 * 1.6726e-27 * 150) = ~6.6 km. Even with g = 15 m/s2 it would be ~66 km. No combination of the cited inputs (T=275, mu=2.3, g=150) gives 25 km; the row value is internally inconsistent with its own basis.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/eps-ind-a-b.md:71; recomputation H = 1.381e-23*275/(2.3*1.6726e-27*150) = 6.58e3 m ~ 6.6 km.

findings.claim: radius_rjup = 1.12; Basis: 'DB radius 12.6 R⊕ = 1.12 R_Jup ...'. surface_gravity_g_earth (line 62) and density_g_cc (line 63) likewise derive from 'DB M (7.63 M_Jup) and R (12.6 R⊕)'.
findings.source_says: db/planets_curated.json has NO radius field for eps Ind A b — the planet's 'physical' array contains only true_mass_mearth (2425, Matthews 2026) and the two superseded masses. The 12.6 R⊕ radius is a Phase 3 synthesis estimate (massive cold giant ~1 R_Jup), not a DB-curated value. Attributing it to 'DB radius'/'DB curated' is incorrect (note: COCONUTS-2 b, elsewhere in the same DB file, does have radius_rearth 12.576 R⊕, a likely source of confusion). The numeric radius itself (1.12 R_Jup = 12.6 R⊕) is plausible; only the DB provenance is wrong.
findings.evidence: db/planets_curated.json:1463-1494 (eps Ind A b 'physical' block: no radius key; verified programmatically has_radius=False); docs/phase3/eps-ind-a-b.md:61-63.

findings.claim: eps Ind Bb mass cited as 53.3 M_Jup (Ba 66.9 M_Jup).
findings.source_says: Chen et al. 2022 reports Mass_Bb = 53.25 +/- 0.29 M_Jup (and Mass_Ba = 66.92 +/- 0.36). 66.9 rounds correctly from 66.92; 53.3 is a slight over-round of 53.25 (53.2 would be the conventional 3-sig-fig round). Cosmetic only; ~1459 AU separation, spectral types T1-1.5 / T6, and 3.5 Gyr age all match the paper.

55-cnc-f.coverage_note: 26 Decisions rows. Three cited papers are decision-relevant and all three are present in the offline cache: Moutou 2025 (2510.11523.md), von Braun 2011 (1107.1936.md), Bourrier 2018 (1807.04301.md, context only); Fischer 2008 is context-only with no arXiv id and is not decision-driving. Cacheable sources: von Braun Teff=5196 (Table 1) and L=0.582/R=0.943 fully confirmed; Moutou e=0.063±0.048 and Msini~48.5 confirmed (line 204); Bourrier alternatives P=259.88/a=0.7708/e=0.08/Msini=47.8 confirmed (Table at lines 102-111). All derived rows (Teq A=0 271, A=0.3 248, peri 280, apo 263, insolation 0.905, mass_mjup 0.153, angular diam 0.627) recomputed and match. The two orbital headline numbers attributed to Moutou (period 260.58 and a=0.802) are NOT present in the cached Moutou markdown because Table 8 (inner-planet orbits) was referenced but never rendered into the cache, so those two decision-driving rows are unverifiable offline against the cited paper despite being DB-consistent (no PHASE2_DESYNC). Physical/policy checks: tidally_locked=false on a 260-d/0.802-AU eccentric orbit is correct (no forced lock), no lava/molten-surface or reflexive-aurora claims, no cfg divergence-without-section. Bourrier superseded values are inlined in Basis cells but as documented recommended-vs-alternative measurements (tie-break/supersession), not a cfg-vs-canonical divergence, so not flagged POLICY_VIOLATION.

findings.claim: sidereal_period_days = 260.58, high confidence, Basis 'Moutou 2025 (Phase 2 recommended; Bourrier 2018 259.88 agrees)'
findings.source_says: Cached Moutou 2025 (2510.11523.md) does NOT contain the value 260.58 or any inner-planet orbital period table. findings.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = 130, derived as 'H = kT / (μ m_H g) with T = 411 K, μ ≈ 2.6, g ≈ 27 m/s² ≈ 130 km'.
findings.source_says: The report's own stated inputs give ~48-49 km, not 130 km. H = (1.381e-23 × 411) / (2.6 × 1.6736e-27 × 26.5 m/s²) ≈ 49 km. To get 130 km you need g ≈ 10 m/s² (1.0 g⊕), which contradicts the report's own derived surface gravity of 2.7 g⊕ = ~27 m/s² (row surface_gravity_g_earth, line 43). The stated g=27 m/s² and the stated result 130 km are mutually inconsistent.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-d.md:52 vs :43. Recomputed: with T=411, μ=2.6, g=26.5-27 m/s² -> H ≈ 48-49 km; 130 km requires g≈10 m/s². Low-confidence, non-cfg-decisive (rendering-only) field, but a clear self-contradiction in the shown derivation.

hd-219134-g.coverage_note: 26 Decisions rows total. The inherited stellar values (stellar_illumination_color_temp_k = 4858, and L=0.264 used in the derived insolation/T_eq rows) trace to Ligi et al. 2019 = arXiv 1909.10058, also cached and confirmed (Table 4: Teff 4858+/-50, L 0.264+/-0.004, R 0.726+/-0.014). All derived rows recompute correctly EXCEPT atmosphere_scale_height_km (130 km claimed; ~54 km from stated inputs). NON-cacheable rows: radius_rearth (Lopez & Fortney 2014 / Wolfgang 2016) and atmosphere_present (Owen & Wu 2017) cite papers that are absent from the _papers cache AND absent from the HD 219134 bib YAML (these arXiv ids appear only in TRAPPIST-1 bibs); they print resolvable arXiv ids in the report's Bibliography but cannot be verified offline -> UNVERIFIABLE. The remaining tie-break / convention rows (obliquity, bond_albedo, atmosphere composition/pressure/tints, cloud rows) require no external source and are labeled as tie-breaks; no row mislabels a tie-break as a divergence and no '## Canonical alternatives' section is needed (report explicitly states 'No documented divergence'). No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE issues (e=0 so the non-locked rotation is consistent; T_eq=326 K with no lava/aurora claims). No PHASE2_DESYNC: report matches db/planets_curated.json for g (P, a, e, M, omega, Msini, recommended=Vogt 2015).

findings.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = 130, derived as H = kT/(mu m_H g) with T = 326 K, mu approx 2.6, g approx 19 m/s^2 => approx 130 km
findings.source_says: The stated formula with the stated inputs does not yield 130 km. H = kT/(mu m_H g) = (1.38e-23 * 326)/(2.6 * 1.673e-27 * 19) = 5.45e4 m approx 54 km (55.6 km if g = 18.6 m/s^2 = 1.9 g_earth). 130 km would require g approx 8 m/s^2 or mu approx 1.1, neither of which matches the report's own derived surface_gravity (1.9 g_earth = 18.6 m/s^2) or stated mu = 2.6.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-g.md:54 ('atmosphere_scale_height_km | 130 | ... H = kT / (mu m_H g) with T = 326 K, mu approx 2.6, g approx 19 m/s^2 approx 130 km'). Recomputation with these exact inputs gives ~54 km; off by factor ~2.4. The g = 19 m/s^2 is itself consistent with the report's surface_gravity row (1.9 g_earth), so the discrepancy is in the stated result, not the inputs.

61-vir-b.coverage_note: Of 32 Decisions rows, only 7 are fully verified-by-construction (the purely derived internal-consistency rows: insolation_s_earth 326 [computed 325.4], equilibrium_temp_k A=0 1180 [1183], A=0.3 1080 [1082], surface_gravity_g_earth 1.87 [1.87], density_g_cc 6.27 [6.26], plus the trivial no-atmosphere consequence rows cloud_cover_fraction/cloud_morphology/cloud_tint/atmosphere_scale_height/atmosphere_tint/ocean_present which follow tautologically from atmosphere_present=false). CACHEABLE-SOURCE COVERAGE IS NEAR ZERO: of the 7 papers cited as decision-driving, only Bolmont 2020 (arXiv:2002.02015) is present in docs/phase3/_papers/ — and it is TRAPPIST-1-specific, not a 61-Vir-b scaling source. The discovery/anchor paper Vogt et al. 2010 (which underpins eccentricity, omega, period, semi-major-axis, mass — 5 of the highest-confidence rows) is in NEITHER the _papers cache NOR the bib YAML; no planet-level bib docs/phase3/_bib/61-vir-b.yaml exists (only the host docs/phase3/_bib/61-vir.yaml, which lists none of the planet sources). Leger 2009 (0908.0241), Schaefer & Fegley 2009 (0905.4045), Zeng 2016 (1512.08827), Owen & Wu 2017 (1705.10810), Hut 1981 (no arXiv) are all absent. Mamajek 2008 (0807.1686) IS cached and corroborates the context-only age claim (61 Vir activity ages 6.6/6.1 Gyr; report's '6.1 +/- 1.7 Gyr' consistent) but it is not a Decisions-table source. Net: 5 high-severity UNSOURCED orbital/mass rows (no verifiable provenance at all), 6 UNVERIFIABLE rows (paper named but absent), 1 UNVERIFIABLE mis-scoped citation (Bolmont), and 1 hard PHYSICAL/INTERNAL contradiction (angular diameter off by ~10x, propagated to prose 3 places). The derived rows are arithmetically sound except angular diameter.

findings.claim: 1.02 deg, derived as '2 x R*/a x (180/pi); 0.963 Rsun at 0.0502 AU'. Prose (lines 164, 246) repeats '~2x the Sun's apparent size from Earth'.
findings.source_says: The stated formula 2*R*/a*(180/pi) with R*=0.963 Rsun and a=0.0502 AU evaluates to 10.22 deg (exact 2*atan(R/a)=10.20 deg), about 19x the Sun's 0.53 deg apparent diameter from Earth — not 1.02 deg / '~2x the Sun'. The value is off by a factor of ~10; the report's own arithmetic does not reproduce its quoted number.
findings.evidence: Computed: 2*(0.963*6.957e8)/(0.0502*1.496e11)*180/pi = 10.22 deg. Report docs/phase3/61-vir-b.md:64 ('1.02' with the 2xR*/a formula), :164 ('angular diameter 1.02 (about 2x the Sun's apparent size from Earth)'), :246 ('subtends 1.02 deg ... about 2x the Sun's angular size'). A factor-10 derivation error propagated into all three locations.

61-vir.coverage_note: Decisions table has 23 rows. CACHEABLE/VERIFIED sources: the 7 stellar rows anchored on von Braun 2014 (arXiv:1312.1792, cached) fully verify - spectral_type 'G7 V' (1312.1792.md:184), mass 0.93 (line 184/206), radius 0.9867+/-0.0048, Teff 5538+/-13, L 0.8222+/-0.0033 with F_bol 36.06 (lines 88,145,184), stellar_color_temp_k 5538, and visual_surface_tint (tie-break derived from the verified Teff). The age-divergence ALTERNATIVES also verify: von Braun 8.6 Gyr (cache) and Mamajek 2008 6.1-6.6 Gyr + log R'HK -5.001 (0807.1686.md:803). Pure-synthesis/tie-break rows with no external number (activity_cycle_years=null, visual_spot_coverage_max=0.003, disk_opacity=0.10) are policy-consistent and not faulted. NOT CACHEABLE: 14 rows depend on sources that are absent from the cache or bib. (a) GdS21 (2012.10199, cached) lacks 61 Vir's per-star log R'HK -5.013 and isochrone age 7.69+/-1.44 in its body - those are VizieR-table values; the only cached 61 Vir row shows N=227 spectra, contradicting the report's '1251 spectra'. (b) Rotation 29 d cites Baliunas 1996 (no arXiv, status:skipped, not cached) compiled by Wright 2011 (cached but no 61 Vir body row). (c) X-ray -6.9 cites Wright 2011 VizieR (not in cached body). (d) Metallicity cites SWEET-Cat 2013 (2013A&A...556A.150S) which is in neither bib nor cache; Sousa 2008 corroboration (0805.4826, cached) is a 451-star catalogue with no 61 Vir body row. (e) ALL 10 disk rows cite Wyatt et al. 2012 (arXiv:1204.6063 / 2012MNRAS.424.1206W), which is in NEITHER the bib YAML (lists only 6 papers) NOR the _papers cache - classified UNSOURCED. disk_mass_mearth additionally has an internal arithmetic error (7 x 5e-3 = 0.035, not 0.07). The von Braun bibliography entry also mis-titles the paper as volume 'VI' when the cache shows volume 'V' (data still matches, so cosmetic). Bottom line: 9 rows verified, 14 problem rows (10 high/medium UNSOURCED disk rows hinging on an uncacheable anchor, 4 UNVERIFIABLE VizieR-table stellar rows, plus 1 UNSOURCED metallicity and cosmetic title/spectral notes).

gj-896-a-b.coverage_note: All 32 Decisions rows reviewed. 30 fully verified. Source coverage: every paper-cited row (eccentricity, argument_of_periastron, sidereal_period, semi_major_axis, inclination, longitude_of_ascending_node, mass_mjup, mass_mearth, tidally_locked host mass 0.44 M☉, aurora host-activity grounding, obliquity 148-deg mutual-inclination hook) was checkable offline against the cached full text docs/phase3/_papers/2208.14553.md (Curiel et al. 2022, arXiv:2208.14553) and cross-confirmed by db/planets_curated.json (GJ 896 A b, recommended astrometric fit) and db/stellar_props_curated.json (m_A=0.436 M☉; no Teff/L curated, confirming the report's 'no Phase 2 L / synthesized L≈0.02 L☉' caveat is legitimate, not a desync). Paper values all match: e=0.35±0.19, P=284.39±1.47 d, a=0.63965±0.00067 au, i=69.2°25.61, Ω=45.62, ω=353.11, m_b=2.26±0.57 M_Jup (=718.27 M⊕), single-companion alt (a=0.6352, P=281.56, e=0.30) = DB recommended:false; HZ 0.18-0.26 au, snow line ~0.51 au, perihelion/aphelion 0.42/0.86 au, Φ=148-deg mutual inclination, M4.5 companion GJ 896 B, P_AB=229.06 yr, a_AB=31.6 au all verbatim in cache. Derived rows independently recomputed and confirmed: insolation ~0.05 (0.0488), T_eq ~130 K (130.7), T_eq range ~110-165 K (112.7-161.3), T_eq A=0.3 ~120 K (119.5), angular diameter 0.29/0.44 deg, density ~2.1 (actual 2.25, minor rounding within tolerance). The TWO non-verified rows are NOT source/attribution problems but a derived-physics error: surface_gravity is ~12× too high (570 m/s²/58 g⊕ vs correct ~48 m/s²/~4.9 g⊕), and the scale-height row's stated derivation propagates that wrong g (though its 9 km result coincidentally matches the correct g). No fabricated features, no policy/Phase2 desync detected; tie-breaks are correctly labelled and no 'Canonical alternatives' section is required (no cfg-vs-canonical divergence asserted).

findings.claim: surface_gravity_g_earth = ~58, derived: g = G M / R² with M = 2.26 M_Jup, R = 1.1 R_Jup → ~570 m/s² ≈ 58 g⊕ (gj-896-a-b.md:47)
findings.source_says: Computing g = GM/R² with M = 2.26 M_Jup (4.29e27 kg) and R = 1.1 R_Jup (7.69e7 m) gives g ≈ 48.4 m/s² ≈ 4.9 g⊕, NOT 570 m/s² / 58 g⊕. Cross-check: Jupiter's own surface gravity is ~24.8 m/s² (~2.5 g); scaling by M/R² = 2.26/1.21 = 1.87× gives ~46 m/s² (~4.7 g). The report's value is too high by a factor of ~12.
findings.evidence: Self-computed from the row's own stated inputs (M=2.26 M_Jup, R=1.1 R_Jup): g=GM/R²=48.4 m/s²=4.9 g_earth. gj-896-a-b.md:47 states '~570 m/s² ≈ 58 g⊕'. The m/s²→g conversion within the cell is internally self-consistent (570/9.81≈58) but both absolute numbers are ~12× too large.

findings.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = ~9, derived: H = kT/(μ m_H g) with T ≈ 130 K, μ = 2.3, g ≈ 570 m/s² ≈ 9 km (gj-896-a-b.md:56)
findings.source_says: H = kT/(μ m_H g). With the row's stated g ≈ 570 m/s², H = 0.82 km, not 9 km. The value 9 km is only recovered with the CORRECT g ≈ 48 m/s² (gives 9.64 km). So the scale-height value (~9 km) is right, but its stated derivation cites g ≈ 570 m/s² — the same wrong gravity from the surface_gravity row — which is inconsistent both internally (570 m/s² → 0.82 km) and with the surface_gravity row.
findings.evidence: Self-computed: with g=570, H=kT/(μ m_H g)=0.82 km; with g=48.4, H=9.64 km. gj-896-a-b.md:56 cites 'g ≈ 570 m/s² ≈ 9 km'. The correct scale-height value (~9 km) silently assumes g≈48 m/s², contradicting the surface_gravity row (gj-896-a-b.md:47) which asserts ~570 m/s²/58 g⊕.

40-eridani-b.coverage_note: 23 Decisions rows total. 21 fully verified against the offline _papers cache: spectral_type (DA2.9, confirmed via Bond 2017 cache 1709.00478.md:13 attributing to GBR11, and GBR11 itself cached as 1109.3171.md with matching authors Gianninas/Bergeron/Ruiz; DB stellar_props_curated.json '40 Eridani B' matches exactly — no Phase2 desync); mass/radius/teff/luminosity/log_g/gravitational_redshift/cooling_age/progenitor_mass/pre_wd_lifetime/total_age/h_envelope_qh/core_composition/metal_pollution all confirmed in Bond 2017 cache (1709.00478.md lines 7,35,41,43,45,49,55,63,65,71,81,100); companion_c orbit period/eccentricity/a confirmed in Mason 2017 cache Table 4 (1707.03635.md:283-288); visual_apparent_diameter (25.09 recomputed = 25.0934), stellar_color_temp_k, visual_surface_tint_hex (tie-break, correctly labeled, no canonical-alternatives section needed) all sound. 2 rows cite sources absent from BOTH the bib YAML and the _papers cache: magnetic_field_upper_limit_gauss (Landstreet & Bagnulo 2015 — decision-driving, value not independently in cache, medium) and companion_a_separation_arcsec (Tokovinin 2018 — value corroborated by Bond/Mason cache, low). The b-component bib YAML pins only 4 papers; the host slug 40-eridani has no separate host bib (component report). Note: bib YAML line 23 mislabels arXiv 1109.3171 as 'Giammichele et al. 2012' but the actual cached paper is Gianninas/Bergeron/Ruiz 2011 matching the report's attribution. Intro prose says 'All 20 measurement rows' but the table has 23 rows (cosmetic, not flagged as a finding).

findings.claim: <250 G upper limit, high confidence, Basis: 'Landstreet & Bagnulo 2015 — ESPaDOnS spectropolarimetry, sigma<Bz> approx 85 G, no detection'
findings.source_says: not in cache
findings.evidence: Landstreet & Bagnulo 2015 (bibcode 2015A&A...580A.120L) is cited in the Bibliography (docs/phase3/40-eridani-b.md:238-248) with a DOI but NO arXiv id. It is NOT listed in docs/phase3/_bib/40-eridani-b.yaml (which contains only 4 papers: 1709.00478, 1707.03635, 1109.3171, astro-ph/0006050) nor in any 40-eridani-{a,c}.yaml. No file in docs/phase3/_papers/ matches the bibcode, title ('A novel and sensitive method...'), or distinctive phrase ('smallest standard error of field measurement'). The only cached magnetic paper, astro-ph_0006050.md (Fabrika 2003), covers only the REFUTED ~4-5 kG variability claim and its 2h25m/5h17m periods (astro-ph_0006050.md abstract), not the <250 G / sigma~85 G figures. The decision-driving 250 G upper limit could never be verified offline.

findings.claim: ~83 arcsec, high confidence, Basis: 'Tokovinin MSC 2018 — wide-binary 40 Eri A<->BC geometry'
findings.source_says: not in cache (Tokovinin 2018); value ~83 arcsec is independently corroborated by Bond 2017 and Mason 2017 which ARE cached
findings.evidence: Tokovinin 2018 (bibcode 2018ApJS..235....6T) is cited only in the Bibliography (docs/phase3/40-eridani-b.md:295-298), has no arXiv id, is absent from all 40-eridani bib YAMLs, and has no matching file in docs/phase3/_papers/. The cited source itself is unverifiable offline. However the value is corroborated: Bond 2017 cache (1709.00478.md:11) 'The primary lies 83 arcsec from the companion BC binary' and Mason 2017 cache (1707.03635.md:11) 'rho ~ 83.7 arcsec'. Decision value is sound; only the attribution is uncacheable.

findings.claim: Basis cites 'Bond et al. 2017 §6.1 — atmosphere purely radiative at 17,200 K, well above the DA H-convection onset (~13,000 K)'
findings.source_says: Bond 2017 §6.1 confirms the atmosphere is 'purely radiative' (1709.00478.md:41) but does NOT state a ~13,000 K H-convection onset; §6.1 instead says a thin-H qH=10^-10 DA would mix at Teff~10,000 K and discusses DB-gap edges at 45,000/30,000 K (1709.00478.md:63-65)
findings.evidence: 1709.00478.md:41 'In the case of 40 Eri B, the atmosphere is purely radiative'; 1709.00478.md:65 'A DA star with a thin H layer of qH=10^-10 would mix at Teff~10,000 K'. The core decision (none / purely radiative) is correct and cited; only the parenthetical ~13,000 K onset number is not attributable to the cited Bond section.

CACHEABLE + FULLY VERIFIED (8): radius_rsun, teff_k, luminosity_lsun, stellar_color_temp_k (all anchored on Rains 2020 = 2004.02343.md line 293, exact match θ_LD 1.828/R 1.197/Teff 5571/L 1.24); mass_msun adopted value 1.07±0.13 and rotation_period_days vsini-input both verified in Bruntt 2010 = 1002.4268.md lines 82/281; spectral_type G8IV cross-confirmed in Rains Table 1 (line 44); metallicity Bruntt +0.33 corroboration verified (line 82). NOT OFFLINE-VERIFIABLE (per-star data in CDS electronic tables or paper absent): age_gyr (Holmberg/Bensby per-star rows absent from cached 0811.3982/1309.2631), activity_log_rhk and metallicity recommended value (Gomes da Silva 2012.10199.md cached but contains no per-star δ Pav row — '5.13'/'6002'/'190248' all 0 hits), x_ray (Hünsch 1998 uncached, no arXiv), disk_present (Eiroa 2013 uncached, no arXiv — but DB-corroborated), limb_darkening (Claret 2011 uncached theory tables), spectral_type Gray 2006 source (astro-ph/0603770 uncached, though G8IV is cross-confirmed by Rains). Tie-break/derived rows (visual_surface_tint, visual_corona_extent, activity_cycle=unknown) are synthesis picks needing no paper. POLICY CHECK: the age 'divergence' is policy-compliant — the report adopts the Holmberg recommended value (= DB recommended), so the cfg does NOT outrank canonical and no '## Canonical alternatives' section is required (per conflict-resolution.md lines 216-221); both literature readings appear only as a Basis-column cross-check, not inlined in the Value cell. DB PARITY: all adopted values match db/stellar_props_curated.json recommended measurements; no confirmed planet (planets_curated empty) and no disk (disks_curated empty) for δ Pav — no PHASE2_DESYNC. Distance 6.10 pc / parallax 163.95 mas is internally consistent (1000/163.95 = 6.099 pc).

findings.claim: Rotation synthesis (line 140) cites 'The Spada & Lanzafame 2020 subgiant rotation models predict 30–45 d at this evolutionary stage.' Bibliography (line 237) lists this as 'Spada F. et al. 2011 — Stellar evolutionary models (2011MNRAS.416..447S, arXiv:1105.3125).'
findings.source_says: The cached paper 1105.3125 is Spada, Lanzafame, Lanza, Messina & Collier Cameron 2011, titled 'Modelling the rotational evolution of solar-like stars: the rotational coupling timescale' — about pre-main-sequence/cluster angular-momentum coupling, NOT a 2020 paper, NOT titled 'Stellar evolutionary models', and it does not contain a '30–45 d subgiant' prediction. The bibliography title is wrong, and the prose 'Spada & Lanzafame 2020' is an uncatalogued source not in the bib or cache. The rotation_period_days Decisions value (~35 d) itself rests on Bruntt vsini+R (verified), so the cfg cell is not affected — this is a prose/bibliography attribution error.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1105.3125.md:1 title 'Modelling the rotational evolution of solar-like stars: the rotational coupling timescale'; abstract (lines 7-9) discusses τ_c core-envelope coupling and α Per fast rotators, no subgiant 30–45 d. grep 'subgiant|30-45|2020' -> 0. Report bib at docs/phase3/delta-pavonis.md:237-239; prose at :139-141.

This is a theory-table interpolation (Confidence=medium) rather than a per-star measurement; low severity as it is a visual-only parameter.

alpha-centauri-b.coverage_note: Decisions table has 17 rows (intro miscounts as 16). 8 rows fully verified against cached papers: mass_msun (Kervella 2016, 1610.06079.md:87 m_B=0.9373+/-0.0033), radius_rsun (Kervella 2017, 1610.06185.md:7 R_B=0.8632+/-0.0037), age_gyr (Joyce 2018, 1806.07567.md '5.3 +/- 0.3 Gyr'), x_ray_log_lx_cgs_max (Robrade 2016, 1612.06570.md:51 L_X~4e27=log27.6, rounds to 27.5), has_planet_b (Rajpaul 2016, 1510.05598.md title 'no planet for Alpha Cen B' + 3.236-d window-function artifact), limb_darkening_alpha_h (Kervella 2017, 1610.06185.md:7 alpha(B)=0.1545+/-0.0044), stellar_color_temp_k (derived, consistent with teff row), and the Pourbaix&Boffin 0.972 bib value (1601.01636.md:13). CACHEABLE-SOURCE STATUS: 4 rows cite papers with NO cacheable full text -> teff_k, metallicity_fe_h_dex, and spectral_type cite Porto de Mello 2008 (arXiv:0804.3712 EXISTS in bib YAML + DB but is NOT in _papers cache; report's bib wrongly says 'no arXiv'); rotation_period_days, activity_log_rhk, activity_cycle_years cite DeWarf 2010 / Henry 1996 which genuinely have no arXiv and are not cached. x_ray_log_lx_cgs_min (26.5) is an inference not explicitly in Robrade. luminosity_lsun and the apparent-diameter row are derivations: luminosity's stated 'derived from R and Teff' basis is internally inconsistent (R+Teff give 0.534, value 0.503 is the DB bolometric_flux number at old 5236 K Teff), and the apparent-diameter row is a hard geometry error (1.1 arcmin vs correct ~39 arcmin). PHASE2 check: db/stellar_props_curated.json corroborates mass/radius/teff(5316)/metallicity(0.25)/age/luminosity(0.503) recommended values -> no Phase 2 desync; no confirmed planet for B in db/planets_curated.json (only Proxima Cen b), consistent with has_planet_b:false. Prose-level (non-table) issues also flagged: unsupported Robrade 'section 4' pi/2 chromospheric-coronal phase claim, and Kjeldsen delta-nu 160.1 vs paper's 161.1.

findings.claim: Value = 1.1 arcmin, Basis 'derived: 2 R*/a' (apparent diameter of alpha Cen B seen from a planet at 0.7 AU). Visual styling prose: 'B fills 1.1 arcmin angular diameter (roughly twice the Sun seen from Earth)'.
findings.source_says: Geometry: 2*R/a with R=0.8632 Rsun (Kervella 2017, verified) and a=0.7 AU gives 0.657 deg = 39.4 arcmin = 2366 arcsec, NOT 1.1 arcmin. The value is wrong by a factor of ~36. The prose comparator 'roughly twice the Sun seen from Earth' (Sun = ~32 arcmin) implies ~39-64 arcmin, which contradicts the 1.1 arcmin number in the same sentence and the table.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:70 (table row) and :150-151 (prose). Recomputed: math.degrees(2*0.8632*0.00465047/0.7)*60 = 39.43 arcmin. Radius R_B=0.8632 confirmed in 1610.06185.md:7 abstract.

findings.claim: teff_k = 5316 +/- 28 K, high confidence, Basis 'Porto de Mello 2008'.
findings.source_says: Porto de Mello et al. 2008 (arXiv:0804.3712, 'The Alpha Centauri Binary System: Atmospheric Parameters and Element Abundances', bibcode 2008A&A...488..653P) is present in the bib YAML and is the DB-cited source, but its full text is NOT in docs/phase3/_papers/ (0804.3712.md absent). The 5316 K value cannot be confirmed offline. Additionally the report's Bibliography wrongly states this paper has 'no arXiv preprint' (it is arXiv:0804.3712).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/alpha-centauri-a.yaml:2548-2556 (arxiv_id 0804.3712, Porto de Mello authors); db/stellar_props_curated.json teff_measurements value_k 5316 reference 'Porto de Mello et al. 2008' bibcode 2008A&A...488..653P; `ls _papers/0804.3712.md` -> not cached.

findings.claim: metallicity_fe_h_dex = +0.25 +/- 0.04, high confidence, Basis 'Porto de Mello 2008'.
findings.source_says: Same source as teff (Porto de Mello et al. 2008, arXiv:0804.3712) is not in the _papers cache, so +0.25 cannot be verified against full text offline. DB corroborates the attribution (metallicity_measurements value_dex 0.25, reference Porto de Mello et al. 2008) and Kervella 2017 independently uses [Fe/H]=+0.25 for the 3D model, but the cited Porto de Mello paper itself is uncacheable here.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json metallicity_measurements value_dex 0.25 reference 'Porto de Mello et al. 2008'; 1610.06185.md:139 '[Fe/H]=+0.25 dex for alpha Cen B' (Kervella's 3D model input, corroborating but not the cited source); 0804.3712.md not in cache.

findings.claim: luminosity_lsun = 0.503, Basis 'derived from R and Teff'.
findings.source_says: Stefan-Boltzmann with the report's own table values R=0.8632 Rsun and Teff=5316 K (Tsun=5772 K) gives L = 0.534 Lsun, not 0.503 (~6% off). The 0.503 value actually matches the DB bolometric_flux measurement (Kervella et al. 2017), and is what R+Teff yields only at the SUPERSEDED 5236 K Teff (0.8632^2*(5236/5772)^4 = 0.505). So the Basis label 'derived from R and Teff' is inconsistent with the table's current 5316 K Teff; the value is a measured/DB number, not a fresh derivation.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:58; recompute (0.8632^2)*((5316/5772)^4)=0.536; db/stellar_props_curated.json luminosity_measurements value_lsun 0.503 method bolometric_flux reference 'Kervella et al. 2017'.

findings.claim: rotation_period_days = 38 +/- 2 (range 36-40), high confidence, Basis 'DeWarf 2010'.
findings.source_says: DeWarf et al. 2010 (2010ApJ...722..343D) has no arXiv preprint and is not in the _papers cache (the report's own Bibliography labels it 'no arXiv preprint, Tier A manual followup'). The 36-40 d rotation period cannot be verified offline against the cited paper's full text. This is a decision-driving row.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:178-180 (Bibliography: DeWarf 2010 'no arXiv preprint'); no DeWarf file in docs/phase3/_papers/.

findings.claim: activity_log_rhk = -5.0, high confidence, Basis 'Henry 1996; DeWarf 2010'.
findings.source_says: Both cited sources (Henry et al. 1996 = 1996AJ....111..439H, and DeWarf et al. 2010) are 'no arXiv' per the report's own Bibliography and are not in the _papers cache. The log R'HK = -5.0 value cannot be verified offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:181-182 (Henry 1996 '1996AJ....111..439H, no arXiv'); :178-180 (DeWarf 2010 no arXiv); neither file present in _papers/.

findings.claim: activity_cycle_years = 8.1, high confidence, Basis 'DeWarf 2010 chromospheric cycle'.
findings.source_says: DeWarf 2010 (chromospheric cycle source) is not in the cache. The closest cacheable corroboration is Robrade 2016 (1612.06570.md:41), which states the CORONAL cycle period is P_act ~= 8-9 yr for B -- broadly consistent with 8.1 yr but it is the coronal, not chromospheric, value and not an exact 8.1. The cited 8.1-yr chromospheric value itself cannot be confirmed offline.
findings.evidence: 1612.06570.md:41 'the corresponding period is P_act ~= 8-9 yr'; DeWarf 2010 not cached (docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:178-180).

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_min = 26.5, medium confidence, Basis 'Robrade 2016 cycle minimum'.
findings.source_says: Robrade 2016 (1612.06570.md) gives the B coronal cycle amplitude as L_Xmax/L_Xmin ~= 6-9 (line 41) and the 2012 maximum as L_X ~= 4e27 erg/s (line 51, = log 27.6). A discrete cycle-minimum log L_X of 26.5 is not stated explicitly in the cached text; it is an inference (27.6 minus ~factor 6-9 -> ~26.7-26.8). The exact 26.5 minimum cannot be confirmed from the cache, though it is in the right order of magnitude.
findings.evidence: 1612.06570.md:41 'L_Xmax/L_Xmin is about 6-9'; :51 'L_X ~= 4 x 10^27 erg s-1 ... brightest quasi-quiescent state'; no explicit 26.5 minimum value found.

findings.claim: spectral_type = K1V, high confidence, Basis 'Porto de Mello 2008; IAU MK'.
findings.source_says: K1V for alpha Cen B is independently confirmed in cached papers (e.g. Kervella 2017 1610.06185.md:8 'K1V', Kervella 2016 1610.06079.md:11 'K1V (B, HD 128621)'), so the value is sound. However the specifically cited source Porto de Mello 2008 (arXiv:0804.3712) is not in the cache, so the attribution itself cannot be checked against that paper's text. Low severity since the value is corroborated elsewhere.
findings.evidence: 1610.06185.md:8 and 1610.06079.md:11 both state K1V for component B; cited Porto de Mello 2008 (0804.3712) not in _papers cache.

findings.field: prose: Robrade 2016 chromospheric/coronal cycle phase offset
findings.claim: Atmosphere/Visual/Open-items prose: 'phase-shifted from B's chromospheric cycle by roughly pi/2 (Robrade 2016 section 4)' and 'Robrade 2016 section 4 shows that B's chromospheric and coronal cycles are ~pi/2 out of phase'. This drives a proposed coronal-cycle-phase-offset cfg variant.
findings.source_says: Robrade & Schmitt 2016 (1612.06570.md) has NO section 4 (headers are 1, 2, 2.1, 2.2, and an unnumbered final acknowledgements section) and contains NO mention of a chromospheric cycle, Ca II / S-index, or any chromospheric-vs-coronal phase offset. The only '90' occurrences are 'B contributes >~90% of X-ray events' (line 47) and an observation-ID date string (line 55). The pi/2 phase-offset claim and 'section 4' citation are unsupported by the cached paper.
findings.evidence: 1612.06570.md section headers via grep '^## /^### ' -> 5,11,17,29,39 (##) and 45 (###) only (no section 4); grep 'chromospher|Ca II|phase shift|out of phase' returns only 'active phase'/'inactive phase' hits. docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:106-108, 161-163, 246-249.

findings.claim: Rotation & spin synthesis: 'Kjeldsen 2005 detected 37 modes with frequency separation delta-nu = 160.1 +/- 0.1 microHz and nu_max near 4000 microHz'.
findings.source_says: Kjeldsen et al. 2005 (astro-ph_0508609.md) abstract confirms '37 oscillation modes' (line 7) and excess power centred at ~4 mHz (~4000 microHz, line 11). But the large separation reported is delta-nu = 161.1 microHz (line 11 'a large separation of 161.1 microHz'; line 63 Eggenberger models give 161.7 and 161.1), not 160.1. The report's 160.1 is ~1 microHz low. Non-decisive prose context.
findings.evidence: astro-ph_0508609.md:7 '37 oscillation modes'; :11 'large separation of 161.1 microHz'; :63 'large separations delta-nu_0 of 161.7 and 161.1 microHz'. docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:136-137.

findings.field: prose: 'All 16 cfg picks' vs 17 Decisions rows
findings.claim: Intro (Scenario choice) states 'All 16 cfg picks are canonical-aligned'.
findings.source_says: The Decisions table actually contains 17 data rows (spectral_type through visual_in_planet_sky_apparent_diameter_arcmin), not 16. Minor count mismatch.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/alpha-centauri-b.md:46 ('All 16 cfg picks'); table rows 54-70 = 17 rows (counted via awk).

eps-eri-b.coverage_note: The report has NO pinned b-bibliography (docs/phase3/_bib/eps-eri-b.yaml does not exist; the report itself admits this at lines 481-485, though it wrongly claims the host bib has 'zero planet-b entries' — the host eps-eri.yaml in fact pins Mawet 2019=1810.03794 and Llop-Sayson 2021=2108.02305). Of 35 Decisions rows: 11 verified against cached primaries or correct derivations (eccentricity, arg_periastron, sidereal_period, semi_major_axis, insolation, both equilibrium_temp rows, stellar_illumination_color_temp, and 2 belt/ring rows corroborated). 2 cornerstone observational papers ARE cacheable and were read in full: Mawet 2019 (1810.03794) and Llop-Sayson 2021 (2108.02305). 4 cited decision-relevant papers are NOT cacheable (Roettenbacher 2022/2110.10643, Booth 2017/1705.05868, Su 2017/1703.10330, MacGregor 2015/1505.03879 — none in cache or any bib). The remaining ~17 rows are self-labeled low/medium tie-break or theoretical picks citing uncached secondary papers (Burrows, Fortney, France 2018, Wood 2002, Khare, Hörst, Clarke, Bonfond) or pure aesthetic defaults — UNVERIFIABLE offline but flagged as non-cfg-decisive in the report. The high-severity cluster is the mass row: the report's 0.78 M_Jup (high confidence) misattributes Mawet 2019's value to Llop-Sayson, contradicts Llop-Sayson's actual 0.66 M_Jup result, AND desyncs from the DB's curated 209.77 M_Earth — and that wrong mass propagates into the derived gravity/density/scale-height rows.

findings.claim: mass_mjup = 0.78 ± 0.12, confidence high, Basis: 'Llop-Sayson 2021 — deprojected from M sin i ≈ 0.66 M_Jup using disk-aligned i = 34°'
findings.source_says: Llop-Sayson 2021's headline combined-fit mass is M_b = 0.66 +0.12/−0.09 M_Jup (true/deprojected mass), NOT 0.78. The 0.66 figure is the deprojected combined-fit true mass, not an 'M sin i'. There is no deprojection-at-i=34° giving 0.78 in the paper.
findings.evidence: 2108.02305.md line 7 (abstract): 'We find a new mass, M_b = 0.66_{-0.09}^{+0.12} M_Jup'; line 180 Table 4 RV+Astrometry+DI 800 Myr = 0.66_{-0.09}^{+0.12}; line 260 (conclusion) repeats 0.66. Report claim at docs/phase3/eps-eri-b.md:46.

findings.claim: 0.78 ± 0.12 M_Jup attributed to Llop-Sayson 2021
findings.source_says: 0.78 +0.38/−0.12 M_Jup is the MAWET 2019 800-Myr model value (with a geometric i=89°±42° prior), not Llop-Sayson's. Mawet's RV M sin i is 0.72 ± 0.07 M_Jup, also not 0.66.
findings.evidence: 1810.03794.md (Mawet 2019) line 354: 'M_b = 0.78_{-0.12}^{+0.38} M_Jup, and an orbital plane inclination of i = 89° ± 42°'; line 297: 'M_b sin i = 0.72 ± 0.07 M_Jup'. Report attributes 0.78 to Llop-Sayson at eps-eri-b.md:46.

findings.claim: mass_mjup = 0.78 (= 248 M_Earth) as the recommended/high-confidence mass for eps Eri b
findings.source_says: db/planets_curated.json stores eps Eri b true_mass_mearth = 209.77 ± 38.14 (= 0.66 M_Jup), mass_type 'true mass', source 'Llop Sayson et al. 2021'. The report's 0.78 contradicts the DB's own Llop-Sayson value.
findings.evidence: db/planets_curated.json eps Eri[0].physical: true_mass_mearth=209.76674862, uncertainty 38.139, source 'Llop Sayson et al. 2021'. 209.77/317.8 = 0.660 M_Jup. Report eps-eri-b.md:46-47.

findings.claim: mass_mearth = 248, derived from 0.78 M_Jup × 317.8
findings.source_says: DB stores 209.77 M_Earth (from Llop-Sayson 0.66 M_Jup). The report's 248 is derived from the erroneous 0.78 and is ~18% higher than the DB-curated true mass. This wrong mass propagates into surface_gravity (1.8 g), density (0.85), and scale_height (23 km) downstream rows.
findings.evidence: db/planets_curated.json true_mass_mearth=209.77; report eps-eri-b.md:47 (248) and :49-50,:59 (g, density, H all derived using M=0.78). 0.66×317.8=209.7.

findings.claim: Canonical alternative 78.81° ± 29.34° described as 'Llop-Sayson 2021 RV-only fit', and DB value 'from Llop-Sayson RV-only fit'
findings.source_says: 78.81 +29.34/−22.41° is from Llop-Sayson's COMBINED RV + Hipparcos/Gaia astrometry + direct-imaging fit. The paper is explicit that RV ALONE does not constrain inclination (a sine/uniform prior is assumed); the astrometry is precisely what breaks the i degeneracy. Labeling it 'RV-only' is a method error.
findings.evidence: 2108.02305.md line 164: 'although the RV fits do not constrain the inclination, i, we assume a sine distribution'; line 170: '78.81° ... thanks to the inclusion of the astrometry data'; line 185 Table 4 (RV+Astrometry+DI 800 Myr) i=78.81. Report eps-eri-b.md:45 and :314.

findings.claim: cfg picks i = 34°; 'Roettenbacher 2022 ... favors i ≈ 34°' is treated as positive astrometric support for the coplanar reading
findings.source_says: not in cache — Roettenbacher 2022 (arXiv:2110.10643) is in NEITHER the b-bib (no eps-eri-b.yaml exists) NOR the host bib NOR the _papers cache, so the 'favors 34°' claim cannot be verified offline. The report's own title for it ('No Reliable Astrometric Detection of ε Eridani b') is a non-detection paper, making the 'favors 34°' framing unconfirmable. Booth 2017 disk i = 34±2° IS independently corroborated by cached papers, but the planet-to-disk coplanarity is the contested step.
findings.evidence: No docs/phase3/_papers/2110.10643.md (verified absent). 2108.02305.md line 208/260 instead says the 78.81° planet inclination is ~2σ from the 34° disk and 'indicate[s] that the planet orbit is not co-planar with the main ring'. Report eps-eri-b.md:45,314.

findings.claim: high confidence, Basis 'Su 2017 §4 — gap-clearing requires a planet of M ≳ 0.5 M_Jup between the two belts'
findings.source_says: not in cache — Su 2017 (arXiv:1703.10330) is not in the _papers cache or any bib YAML. The substance (planet b sits between the ~3 AU inner belt and ~20 AU intermediate belt) IS independently corroborated by the cached Llop-Sayson paper, but the specific Su 2017 §4 mass-threshold claim cannot be checked offline.
findings.evidence: No docs/phase3/_papers/1703.10330.md (verified absent). Corroborating: 2108.02305.md line 202 'an inner belt at ~3 AU, an intermediate belt at ~20 AU. Planet b sits between the two inner belts'. Report eps-eri-b.md:78.

yz-cet.coverage_note: Host (star) report; Decisions table has 23 rows. All 6 cited papers have a cache .md file present, but coverage is uneven: (a) Stock 2020 (2002.01772) and Astudillo-Defru 2017 (1708.03336) are the correct full-text papers and verify cleanly — rotation 68.40±0.05 d, spectral type M4.5 V, log R'HK −4.705±0.208, alt Teff 3056/mass 0.13/radius 0.168, age 5.0 Gyr, 83 d superseded, planet b 2.02 d all confirmed. (b) Pineda & Villadsen 2023 (2304.00031) and Trigilio 2023 (2305.00809) are correct full-text and verify the entire SPI block strongly: L_X/L_bol −4.13 (Stelzer), L_Hα/L_bol −4.32 (Reiners 2018), ⟨Bf⟩ 2.2 kG (Moutou), Rossby 0.5, ~100% circular polarization (paper text), 2.02087 d, B_p ≲2.4 kG (s=2), B_min≈90 G at ν_min≈500 MHz, B_planet≥0.4 G, η★≈10^8, R_Alf≈100 R★, a/R★=21.9, v_orb=87.6 km/s, Ṁ≤0.2 Ṁ⊙, 4.37σ/99.992% — ALL confirmed verbatim. (c) The two catalog sources are problematic: Schweitzer 2019's mapped cache (arXiv:1904.06860) is the WRONG paper entirely (a COSINE-100 solar-axion paper), so the decision-driving mass (0.1368) and radius (0.1571) rows cannot be verified against Schweitzer offline — both are corroborated by the frozen DB and by Stock 2020's adopted values (R=0.157, M=0.142); Cifuentes 2020's cache is the right paper but carries no per-star YZ Cet row (values are in VizieR), so teff (3100) and luminosity (0.0022) are only corroborated at the spectral-type-mean/DB level. Gaia-derived rows (parallax 269.057 mas, distance 3.717 pc, V 12.156, source_id) verify against db/astrometry_raw.json and db/photometry_raw.json. disk_present false confirmed (no disk in db/disks_curated.json, no fabrication). Tie-break rows (visual tint #cf5630, spot coverage 0.1, SPI viz) are correctly labeled tie-breaks, no 'divergence' language, no POLICY_VIOLATION. No PHASE2_DESYNC, INTERNAL_CONTRADICTION, or PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE found. Summary: 19 rows fully verified, 4 flagged UNVERIFIABLE (2 decision-driving but DB+Stock-corroborated, 2 low-severity catalog/VizieR), 0 outright VALUE_MISMATCH or fabrication.

findings.claim: mass_msun = 0.1368 ± 0.0033, high confidence, Basis: Schweitzer et al. 2019 — CARMENES spectroscopic calibration (Phase 2 recommended).
findings.source_says: The cache file mapped to Schweitzer 2019 (arXiv:1904.06860) is the WRONG paper — it is 'A search for solar axion induced signals with COSINE-100' (COSINE-100 Collaboration), not the CARMENES masses/radii paper. The Schweitzer mass value cannot be checked offline. It matches the frozen DB exactly (db/stellar_props_curated.json: 0.1368 recommended). Stock 2020 (verifiable) adopts the Schweitzer params as M = 0.142 ± 0.010 M☉, which the report lists as the 0.1418 'alternative'.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1904.06860.md:1 '# A search for solar axion induced signals with COSINE-100'; 1904.06860.html <title>[1904.06860] A search for solar axion induced signals with COSINE-100</title>. Cross-check: 2002.01772.md:47 'we adopted the stellar parameters of Schweitzer et al. (2019) ... 0.142 ± 0.010 M☉ for the stellar mass'. Bib map: docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet.yaml lines 30-35 (arxiv_id '1904.06860' -> 'Schweitzer et al. 2019').

findings.claim: radius_rsun = 0.1571 ± 0.0052, high confidence, Basis: Schweitzer et al. 2019 — SED fitting (Phase 2 recommended).

The value is corroborated: it matches db/stellar_props_curated.json (0.1571 recommended) and Stock 2020 states the adopted Schweitzer radius is 0.157 ± 0.005 R☉.

findings.claim: teff_k = 3100, high confidence, Basis: Cifuentes et al. 2020 — SED fitting (Phase 2 recommended).
findings.source_says: The Cifuentes 2020 cache (arXiv:2007.15077) is the correct paper but contains NO YZ-Cet-specific Teff row — the per-star values live in the VizieR table J/A+A/642/A115, which the bib YAML explicitly flags ('value-check by Karmn/object ID, not the fetched text'). The value is only corroborated indirectly: Cifuentes Table 5 gives the M4.5 V spectral-type MEAN Teff = 3100 ± 88 K, and db/stellar_props_curated.json carries 3100 K (Cifuentes recommended). So 3100 K is plausible/consistent but not directly verifiable as the per-star value offline.
