trappist-1-b.coverage_note: All papers cited by the Decisions table are present in the _papers cache (verified by file existence for every arXiv id, incl. Agol 2010.01074, Greene 2303.14849, Ducrot 2412.11627/2509.02128, Ih 2305.10414, Grayver 2211.06140, Bolmont 2601.03408/2002.02015/1605.00616, Kislyakova 1710.08761, Garraffo 1706.04617, RM22 2203.01065, Atri 1910.09871, Turbet 1911.08878, Chatterjee 2412.05188, Gialluca 2405.02401, Revol 2409.12065). Caveat on cache depth: the Agol 2010.01074 cache is abstract + a PDF-extracted Table 6 ONLY (R, M, rho, g, S, a). It contains NO eccentricity, argument-of-periastron, orbital-period, or Teff entries, and the cache itself states 'Agol 2021 reports NO equilibrium-temperature column.' So the Agol-attributed rows that ARE in Table 6 (mass 1.374, radius 1.116, density 5.43, a 0.01154, gravity ~1.10) and S=4.153 are fully verifiable; the Agol-attributed rows that are NOT in Table 6 (eccentricity 0.00622, sidereal_period 1.5109 d, omega 337) are not checkable from the cache. Three of those non-Table-6 Agol rows were partially backstopped by db/stellar_props_curated.json (Teff 2566 K = Agol recommended) and db/planets_curated.json (a 0.01154, mass 1.374, radius 1.116, density 5.43, equilibrium_temp 398 K, bond_albedo 0.0 all recommended/Agol), but eccentricity / sidereal_period / omega appear in neither the Agol cache NOR the DB orbital block (which holds only semi_major_axis + inclination). Greene 2303.14849 cache is abstract + early methods only, but its 503 K and 490 K decision values are independently confirmed (Greene's 503+/-27 K is quoted verbatim in the Ducrot 2412.11627 cache; Ducrot 2509.02128 abstract gives 490+/-17 K). 34/40 rows fully verified; 6 rows flagged below. No PHASE2_DESYNC found: report matches DB recommended Agol-2021 mass/radius/density/a/Teq/albedo and DB atmosphere non-detection. No POLICY_VIOLATION: the dipole-tilt row is correctly labeled a tie-break (not a divergence) and no Decisions cell inlines canonical/variant pairs.

findings.claim: insolation_s_earth = 4.25 (confidence high; Basis: Agol 2021)
findings.source_says: Agol 2021 Table 6 gives S = 4.153 +0.161/-0.159 S_earth for planet b. The report's own equilibrium_temp_k row (line 43) cites 'S_b = 4.153 S_earth at A_B=0' and the intro (line 5) says '4.3x'. The eq_temp 398 K only reproduces from S=4.153 (278.5*4.153^0.25=397.6), not from 4.25 (which gives ~400 K). DB equilibrium_temperature_k=398 also implies 4.153.
findings.evidence: 2010.01074.md Table 6: 'S [S_earth] | 4.153 +0.161/-0.159'. Contradicting cell docs/phase3/trappist-1-b.md:42 (value 4.25) vs :43 ('S_b = 4.153 S_earth'). DB db/planets_curated.json:2076 equilibrium_temperature_k 398.

findings.claim: 'magnetized Earth-dynamo branch gives up to ~200 W/m2 (139 TW over surface area)'
findings.source_says: Grayver 2022 Table 1 gives planet b's magnetized layered-conductivity ohmic heating as 139.2 TW (TOTAL power; the paper compares it to Earth's ~20 TW radiogenic). 139.2 TW spread over b's surface (4*pi*(1.116 Rearth)^2 = 6.35e14 m^2) is ~0.22 W/m^2, NOT ~200 W/m^2. The stated '200 W/m2' is ~900x the value its own parenthetical (139 TW over surface area) implies.
findings.evidence: 2211.06140.md Table 1 line 63: 'b | 2.9 | 139.2 | 15.5 | 379.3 | 42.9 | 304.5'; line 72 frames these vs Earth '~20 TW' radiogenic (units = TW). Cell docs/phase3/trappist-1-b.md:58.

trappist-1-h.coverage_note: All 25 distinct arXiv-cited papers in the report resolve to a _papers cache file. Of the 49 Decisions rows, 42 are fully verified against their cited source (or, where the named paper's cache lacks the field, against another cited cache that carries the same value). Caveats by source: (1) Agol 2021 (2010.01074) is cached as abstract + a manually-extracted Table 6 ONLY; it has no eccentricity, argument-of-periastron, period, T_eq, or stellar-Teff columns, so the five Agol-attributed rows for those fields are not confirmable from the Agol cache itself - of these, sidereal_period (Luger 18.766/18.77 d), stellar color temp 2566 K (Gillon 2024 cache, source Agol), and T_eq 169 K (Luger 2017) were cross-confirmed in OTHER cited caches, while eccentricity/omega trace to the uncited Grimm 2018 (1802.01377). (2) Grayver 2022 (induction_heating row) is wholly UNSOURCED - not in either bib YAML, the report bibliography, or the cache. (3) Krissansen-Totton 2022 (2207.04164) cache is effectively empty (single line, the arXiv subject category only) so its quoted retention claims in the Atmosphere-synthesis prose are unverifiable - but that is prose, not a Decisions row, so it is excluded from the row tally. (4) Author-name attribution errors: 2504.19872 is Ji et al. 2025 (report calls it 'Castan-Lopez 2025') and 2510.12794 is Chen et al. 2025 (report calls it 'Pearce 2025'); both appear in the Bibliography/synthesis prose rather than as a Decisions-row Basis, so they are noted here but not counted as failed Decisions rows. Verified high-value anchors include: Agol Table 6 a/M/R/S (0.06189/0.326/0.755/0.144), Turbet 2017 (CO2 4 mbar at 145 K + gravitational burial of CO2 ice under H2O), Bolmont 2018 (10^2-10^3 ppm CO2 cap, verbatim), Dong 2018 (h ion escape 1.29e26 s^-1, ~10^10 yr, 'most stable'), Makarov 2018 (h 3:2 capture prob 0.017, 5.3e13 W, tau_M 0.20 d), Lincowski 2018 (desiccated 10-100 bar habitable-h), and Gressier 2022 (>3sigma rejection of clear H2/He atmosphere). No PHASE2_DESYNC: the DB recommended values (mass 0.326, radius 0.755, a 0.06189, source Agol 2021) match the report exactly.

trappist-1-f.coverage_note: 51 Decisions rows. Of the cited sources, all paper full texts needed are present in the _papers cache EXCEPT Wolf 2017 (arXiv 1703.05815), which is a 1-line stub — however the Wolf 2017 abstract IS available in docs/phase3/_bib/trappist-1-f.yaml and is unambiguous (f is a snowball even at 30 bar CO2), so the central Wolf-attribution error is verifiable from the bib. Verified against full cache: Agol 2021 (2010.01074, Table 6 only) for mass/radius/g/S/a; Acuna 2021 (2101.08172) wmf 3.7+/-2.6%; Rice 2025 (2504.16201) wmf 16.2/6.9; Barr 2018 (1712.05641) tidal 0.14 W/m2 + 1621 K; Kislyakova 2017 (1710.08761) 1.1e18 erg/s; Turbet 2018 (1707.06927) ~1 bar warming + cold-trap; Garraffo 2017 (1706.04617) field/standoff; Atri 2019 (1910.09871) dose tables; RM22 (2203.01065); Zhang&Rogers (2208.06523). Two context-only refs (2605.06964, 2506.21351) are 1-line stubs but drive no Decisions rows. Agol-attributed eccentricity/arg-periastron/period/color-temp could not be confirmed because the cached Agol extract is Table-6-only (those tables are absent) — flagged UNVERIFIABLE. The dominant problem is a systemic WRONG_ATTRIBUTION: the entire canonical 1-bar-CO2 open-water 'eye-ball aquaplanet' scenario is credited to Wolf 2017, whose own abstract concludes the opposite (f snowball at up to 30 bar CO2); the supporting paper is actually Turbet 2018, which explicitly disagrees with Wolf 2017.

findings.field: atmosphere_surface_pressure_pa / bond_albedo / surface_temp_substellar_k / surface_ice_caps / surface_morphology / ocean_extent (all 'Wolf 2017 1 bar branch' rows) + Surface/Atmosphere prose
findings.claim: Report repeatedly states 'Wolf 2017 explicitly shows that f at 1 bar CO2 supports a substellar open-water disk' / 'Wolf 2017 finds 1 bar CO2 is needed for a fully open ocean' and credits the canonical eye-ball-aquaplanet scenario, bond albedo 0.30, substellar open water, and ice-line geometry to the 'Wolf 2017 1 bar branch' (lines 20, 45-46, 49, 51, 59, 62, 88, 129-139, 185, 192, 290).
findings.source_says: Wolf 2017 (arXiv 1703.05815, ApJ 839 L1) abstract: 'the outer three planets (f, g, and h) fall beyond the maximum CO2 greenhouse outer edge of the habitable zone. Model results indicate that the outer planets cannot be warmed, despite having as much as 30 bar CO2 atmospheres, instead entering a snowball state.' Wolf 2017 has NO 1-bar open-water branch for f; it concludes f is a snowball at all pressures up to 30 bar. The open-water-at-~1-bar result is Turbet 2018's, and Turbet 2018 (1707.06927 line 402) explicitly states Wolf 2017 'is at odd with our results.'
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/trappist-1-f.yaml lines ~818-832 (Wolf 2017 abstract, &id022, arxiv_id 1703.05815); docs/phase3/_papers/1707.06927.md line 402 ('Wolf (2017), which finds that CO2-dominated atmospheres as thick as 30 bars cannot warm the surface of TRAPPIST-1f and g ... is at odd with our results'); docs/phase3/_papers/1703.05815.md is only a 1-line stub (full text absent).

findings.claim: 7000 mSv/yr, confidence 'high', Basis: 'Atri 2019 (1910.09871) for f at 0.037 AU + 1 bar shielding + B-field'.
findings.source_says: Atri 2019 Table 4 (with Earth B-field), TRAPPIST-1 f row at 0.037 AU, 1000 g/cm2 (=~1 bar) column = 2.25E-03 Gy ~= 2.25 mSv PER single hard-spectrum event. Without B-field (Table 5) it is 6.66E-03 Gy. The paper reports per-event Gy doses, not an annual mSv/yr rate, and the cited '1 bar + B-field' cell is ~2 mSv, roughly 3000x smaller than 7000. No annual mSv/yr figure for f exists in the paper.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1910.09871.md line 71 ('| TRAPPIST-1 f | 0.037 | 4.28E+00 | 2.92E+00 | 7.76E-01 | 2.25E-03 |', columns are 30/100/300/1000 g/cm2 in Gy/event) and line 84 (no-B-field f row); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:72.

findings.field: water_mass_fraction (and intro line 8-9, Surface line 93, Bibliography 'Read' entry)

findings.source_says: 2504.16201 is authored by David R. Rice, Chenliang Huang, Jason H. Steffen, Allona Vazan (Rice et al. 2025), title 'Uncertainties in the Inference of Internal Structure: The Case of TRAPPIST-1 f'. The numerical values (16.2% +/- 9.9% free CMF, 6.9% +/- 2.0% Earth-like mantle/core, MAGRATHEA solver) are correct, but the author 'Acuna' is wrong — Acuna is the author of the DIFFERENT paper 2101.08172 ('Characterisation of the hydrospheres of TRAPPIST-1 planets', Lorena Acuna et al. 2021).

Allona Vazan'; abstract 'TRAPPIST-1 f likely has a water mass fraction of 16.2% +/- 9.9% ... and requires 6.9% +/- 2.0% water at an Earth-like mantle to core ratio'); docs/phase3/_papers/2101.08172.md line 3 (Acuna author of the 2021 hydrosphere paper); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:41.

findings.claim: 3.5, confidence 'high', Basis: 'Garraffo 2017 Fig. 4 bottom panel — 3-4 R_p for f under super-Alfvenic conditions'.
findings.source_says: Garraffo 2017 (1706.04617) Section on TRAPPIST-1 f states the headline result is that f has NO magnetopause: 'The extreme wind pressure ... opens the planetary field all the way to the planetary surface ... there is no magnetopause at which the planetary field pressure balances the wind pressure ... The concept of atmospheric protection by a planetary magnetic field does not hold here.' The simple pressure-balance R_mp in Fig. 4 is explicitly described as not applicable to f. Asserting standoff = 3.5 R_p at 'high' confidence contradicts the paper's actual conclusion.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1706.04617.md line 65 ('there is no magnetopause at which the planetary field pressure balances the wind pressure. Instead, stellar wind particles can constantly precipitate directly down open field'); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:70.

findings.claim: 0.3, Basis: 'Garraffo 2017 (1706.04617) test case for f at 0.3 G; supports active dynamo' / 'Garraffo 2017 explicitly adopts f as the representative habitable-zone case with 0.3 G surface field'.
findings.source_says: Garraffo 2017 examines planetary fields of 0.1 G and 0.5 G ('we examine planetary magnetic fields with equatorial strengths of 0.1 G and 0.5 G that bracket the present day terrestrial value of 0.3 G'). 0.3 G is only the cited Earth reference value the runs bracket; Garraffo did NOT run a 0.3 G test case for f. The magnetosphere section uses 'planetary field is similar to that of the Earth' as an assumption, not a 0.3 G run.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1706.04617.md line 45 ('planetary magnetic fields with equatorial strengths of 0.1 G and 0.5 G that bracket the present day terrestrial value of 0.3 G'); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:68.

findings.claim: 215 K, confidence 'high', Basis: 'Agol 2021'.
findings.source_says: Agol 2021 reports NO equilibrium-temperature column anywhere in the paper — only insolation S [S_earth] (Table 6) and a Figure 12 colorbar; any T_eq attributed to Agol is a derived value, not a table entry. A blackbody A=0 T_eq from S=0.373 is ~218 K (Acuna 2021 Table lists f T_eq=218); 215 is a slightly-off derived value mislabeled as an Agol measurement at 'high' confidence.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md lines 27-29 ('Agol 2021 reports NO equilibrium-temperature column anywhere in the paper ... Any T_eq value attributed to Agol 2021 is a derived value'); docs/phase3/_papers/2101.08172.md line 111 (f T_eq column = 218); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:43.

findings.claim: Canonical scenario asserts a substellar open-water lens (ocean_present true, ocean_extent 40 deg) under the 1 bar pick, while the substellar surface temperature row = 260 K, nightside 165 K, global mean 220 K — all below the 273 K freezing point. Prose (line 113) even calls 260 K 'the marginal case for ice/water equilibrium.'
findings.source_says: Open liquid water requires T >= 273 K at the substellar point. 260 K is 13 K below freezing, so an open-water lens cannot coexist with the stated substellar temperature. The temperature rows (basis 'Wolf 2017 + 0.1 bar CO2 greenhouse', i.e. the snowball state) describe the 0.1 bar snowball, contradicting the canonical 1-bar open-water scenario the table commits to. Borderline PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE (open water asserted below the water freezing point with no melting mechanism).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:46 (surface_temp_substellar_k 260, basis cites '0.1 bar CO2 greenhouse'), :48 (global mean 220), :56-57 (ocean_present true / ocean_extent 40), :113 ('Surface T ~ 260 K is the marginal case for ice/water equilibrium').

findings.claim: 0.001-0.005 W/m2, Basis: 'Kislyakova 2017 (1710.08761) — total induction heating 1.1x10^18 W ~= 0.0012 W/m2 normalized to f's surface'.
findings.source_says: Kislyakova 2017 Table 1 gives f Q_m = 1.1x10^18 erg/s (not W). 1.1x10^18 erg/s = 1.1x10^11 W; normalized over f's surface (~5.6x10^14 m2) that is ~2x10^-4 W/m2, not the 0.0012 W/m2 stated. The unit is mislabeled (erg/s reported as W) and the per-area normalization is ~6x too high, though the qualitative conclusion (well below molten-mantle threshold) is correct.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1710.08761.md line 55 (table header 'Q_m [erg/s]') and line 62 ('| f | ... | 1.1 x 10^18 ... |'); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:64.

findings.claim: 4.92 g/cc, confidence 'high', Basis: 'Agol 2021' (also intro line 8).
findings.source_says: Agol 2021 Table 6 gives f density = 5.009 +0.138/-0.158 g/cm3, not 4.92. Difference ~1.8% (within rounding-class, not material), but the reported value does not match the cited table value.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md line 25 ('| rho [g cm-3] | ... | 5.009 +0.138/-0.158 | ... |' for column f); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:40 and :8.

findings.claim: eccentricity 0.01007 (high, Agol 2021 TTV), argument_of_periastron 8.81 (medium, Agol 2021), sidereal_period 9.2075 (high, Agol 2021), stellar color temp 2566 K (high, Agol 2021 SED fit).
findings.source_says: The cached Agol extract (2010.01074.md) contains only the abstract header + Table 6 (R, M, rho, g, S, a, M[g], rho[g/cc]). It does NOT include eccentricity, argument of periastron, sidereal period, or stellar effective/color temperature, so these specific Agol-attributed values cannot be confirmed from the offline cache. (Mass 1.039, radius 1.045, a 0.03849, S 0.373, g 0.951 ARE confirmed from Table 6.)
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md is 29 lines total (header + Table 6 only); no orbital-element or stellar-Teff table is present. docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:33,34,35,81.

findings.claim: Bibliography says Fauchez 2019 'Confirms f's snowball state at all plausible CO2 pressures <=10 bar.'
findings.source_says: Fauchez 2019 (1911.08596) states 'a few bars of CO2 are needed to maintain ice-free surfaces on TRAPPIST-1f and g' (echoing Turbet), i.e. f CAN become ice-free at a few bars of CO2; it does not confirm a snowball 'at all plausible CO2 pressures <=10 bar.' The annotation mischaracterizes the paper's conclusion.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1911.08596.md line 19 ('a few bars of CO2 are needed to maintain ice-free surfaces on TRAPPIST-1f and g'); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:324-326.

findings.claim: 'f's Earth-mass core supports an active dynamo (Driscoll & Olson scaling in 2208.06523 finds Earth-mass dynamos extend lifetime via core solidification timing)'.
findings.source_says: 2208.06523 is authored by Jisheng Zhang & Leslie A. Rogers (2022), 'Thermal Evolution and magnetic history of rocky planets' — not Driscoll & Olson. The paper does support that liquid-core dynamo lifetime increases with planet mass for ~1 M_earth, but the author attribution is wrong.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2208.06523.md line 3 (authors 'Jisheng Zhang ... Leslie A. Rogers') and line 7 (abstract: 'the liquid core last longer with increasing planet mass'); docs/phase3/trappist-1-f.md:246-247.

tau-cet.coverage_note: 24 Decisions rows total. Cacheable + confirmed (11): mass_msun (Teixeira 0811.3989.md:7,105,119 = 0.783+/-0.012); radius_rsun (Korolik 2307.10394.md:77 = 0.793+/-0.004, theta_LD 2.019 line 72); teff_k (Korolik 2307.10394.md:122 = 5370+/-20 via Boyajian Fbol; spectroscopic 5320+/-40 line 79); luminosity_lsun (Teixeira 0811.3989.md:107 = 0.488+/-0.010, SB-consistent at 0.471); age_gyr=10 (Di Folco has no arXiv but Korolik 2307.10394.md:84,138 confirms 10 Gyr + range 4.4-12.4); rotation_period_days=46+/-4 (Korolik 2307.10394.md:85,146); limb_darkening_alpha_h=0.14+/-0.03 (Korolik 2307.10394.md:74,104); spin_axis_inclination_deg=7+/-7 (Korolik 2307.10394.md:86,148); stellar_color_temp_k=5370 (derived from verified Teff); visual_surface_tint_hex (declared tie-break, derived); spectral_type G8.5V (Gray 2006 not cached but is non-numeric context, corroborated by Mamajek 0807.1686.md:765 'G8.5V' and DB). The activity_log_rhk=-4.977 and metallicity=-0.52 rows have a cited source whose per-star value is NOT reproduced in the cached markdown (machine-readable tables only) -> UNVERIFIABLE though level-corroborated. The 11 disk_* rows depend on MacGregor 2016 (1607.06900), Lawler 2014 (1409.0023) and Greaves 2004 (no arXiv) -- ALL three absent from the _papers cache -> entirely UNVERIFIABLE, with secondhand tension on disk extent (Korolik says 10-50 AU vs report 6-55) and disk_mass attribution (cache credits Greaves 2004, report credits MacGregor 2016). Also: one INTERNAL_CONTRADICTION (Open items '7.0+/-1.5 Gyr adopted' vs 10 Gyr everywhere else), one activity-cycle WRONG_ATTRIBUTION (~11 yr traces to the Sun, not tau Cet, in GdS), one PHASE2_DESYNC (report frames tau Cet e absence as open question while DB records a sourced NASA False-Positive reclassification), and one cosmetic Baliunas bibcode/year mismatch. The anchor stellar rows (Korolik + Teixeira) are clean; the largest exposure is that 11 of 24 rows -- the entire disk block, the report's stated defining visual element -- rest on papers that are not in the offline cache.

findings.claim: `activity_cycle_years` = ~11, Basis: "Gomes da Silva 2021 — a ~11 yr cyclic variability with a superimposed long-term declining trend".
findings.source_says: Gomes da Silva 2021 gives no cycle period for tau Ceti; its only 11 yr figure is the Sun's cycle used as a comparison.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2012.10199.md:156 ("the relative peak-to-peak variation of the S_MW of the Sun during its 11 yr activity cycle is ~25%") and :428 (tau Ceti 'long-term decreasing trend ... together with cyclic variability', no period). DB stellar_props_curated.json:1964 repeats the same '~11 yr cycle' label, so the report is internally consistent with the curator but the figure traces to the Sun, not GdS's tau Cet result.

findings.claim: `activity_log_rhk` = -4.977, Basis: "Gomes da Silva 2021 — AMBRE-HARPS (Phase 2 recommended)".
findings.source_says: The cached GdS markdown does not contain the value -4.977 anywhere. The reproduced per-star table (Table 1, line 125) lists only S_CaII = 0.140 and S_MW = 0.171 for tau Cet; the log R'HK values live in a separate machine-readable table (columns log_rhk_med / log_rhk_wmean / log_rhk_err described at lines 234-236) that is NOT reproduced in the cache. The value cannot be confirmed or refuted offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2012.10199.md:125 (tau Cet row, S-indices only) and :234-236 (log_rhk columns named but table contents absent). grep for '4.977' returns no hit in the cache.

findings.claim: `metallicity_fe_h_dex` = -0.52 +/- 0.01, Basis: "Santos 2013 (SWEET-Cat; Phase 2 recommended)".
findings.source_says: The cached Santos 2013 markdown does not contain tau Ceti / HD 10700 or the value -0.52; the per-star SWEET-Cat Table 1 is not reproduced. The paper states the 48-star sample spans [Fe/H] -0.62 to -0.5 dex with ~0.03 dex mean error (line 119), so -0.52 is plausibility-consistent and the metal-poor level is corroborated by Teixeira (-0.5 +/- 0.03, Soubiran) and by Bruntt's rejected -0.18 outlier — but the specific Santos value cannot be confirmed from the cache.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1307.0354.md:119 (range '-0.62 to -0.5 dex'); grep for '10700' / '0.52' returns no hit. Corroboration: 0811.3989.md:13 ([Fe/H]=-0.5+/-0.03); 1002.4268.md:29 (Bruntt -0.18).

findings.claim: `disk_inner_radius_au` = 6 and `disk_outer_radius_au` = 55, Basis: "MacGregor 2016 — ALMA resolved fit, inner edge ~6 AU / outer edge ~55 AU".
findings.source_says: MacGregor 2016 (arXiv 1607.06900) is NOT in the _papers cache, so the 6 and 55 AU fits cannot be verified offline. The only cached cross-reference, Korolik 2023, summarizes MacGregor's disk as spanning 'approximately 10 to 50 AU' — in tension with the report's 6-55 AU, though Korolik is secondhand and may round/mis-summarize.
findings.evidence: Cache miss: docs/phase3/_papers/1607.06900.md absent. Secondhand: docs/phase3/_papers/2307.10394.md:15 ("a debris disk that spans approximately 10 to 50 AU (MacGregor et al., 2016)").

findings.claim: `disk_mass_mearth` = 1.2, Basis: "MacGregor 2016 fit — ~10-20x Sol KBO dust mass"; Bibliography also credits MacGregor 2016 with 'dust mass ~1.2 M_earth'.
findings.source_says: Both cached secondhand sources attribute the 1.2 M_earth dust mass to GREAVES 2004, not MacGregor 2016. Korolik 2023: 'a dust mass of around 1.2 M_earth (Greaves et al., 2004)'. Di Folco 2007: 'the inferred mass ... amounts to ... 1.2 M_earth around tau Cet' citing Greaves 2004. MacGregor 2016 itself is uncached so the original cannot be checked, but the cached evidence points to Greaves, not MacGregor, as the 1.2 source.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2307.10394.md:15 (1.2 M_earth -> Greaves 2004); docs/phase3/_papers/0710.1731.md:13 (1.2 M_earth around tau Cet, Greaves 2004). MacGregor cache absent.

findings.field: disk_present / disk_dust_temperature_k / disk_tint_rgb_hex / disk_opacity / disk_morphology / disk_resolved_imaging / disk_imaging_observatory / disk_planetesimal_belt_inferred
findings.claim: All remaining disk_* rows (present=true; dust_temp=60 K from MacGregor SED + Greaves 60-80 K; tint hex from Lawler 2014 grain composition; morphology 'broad single ring' from MacGregor 2016 sec3; resolved_imaging=true; observatory=ALMA; planetesimal_belt_inferred from MacGregor 2016 sec5) are sourced to MacGregor 2016, Greaves 2004, and/or Lawler 2014.
findings.source_says: MacGregor 2016 (1607.06900), Lawler 2014 (1409.0023) and Greaves 2004 (no arXiv) are NONE present in the _papers cache, so none of these disk decisions can be verified offline. They cannot be checked for value, method, or section citation (e.g. MacGregor sec3/sec5 claims). The disk drives 11 of the 24 Decisions rows and is the report's stated 'defining visual element', so the uncacheable basis is decision-driving.
findings.evidence: Cache misses confirmed: docs/phase3/_papers/{1607.06900,1409.0023}.md absent (ls + grep return nothing); Greaves 2004 has no arXiv and no cache file. bib YAML lists no fetched entries for these three.

findings.claim: Open items (line 407) states 'The adopted 7.0 +/- 1.5 Gyr spans both Pavlenko 2012's super-population kinematic estimate and Mamajek 2008's gyrochronological estimate.'
findings.source_says: The report everywhere else adopts age_gyr = 10 Gyr (Di Folco 2004): Decisions row (line 67), intro (line 19), and the Canonical alternatives section (lines 203-209, 'whether it is ~6 or ~10 Gyr'). There is no 7.0 +/- 1.5 Gyr value anywhere else, so the Open item describes an 'adopted' value that contradicts the actual adopted 10 Gyr. It also mislabels Mamajek 2008's 5.8 Gyr as 'gyrochronological' (the report elsewhere calls it 'activity-age') and attributes a kinematic age to Pavlenko 2012 (the bib, line 332, records Pavlenko's age as 7 Gyr). Reads as boilerplate leaked from another star's report.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet.md:407-410 ('adopted 7.0 +/- 1.5 Gyr ... Pavlenko 2012's super-population kinematic ... Mamajek 2008's gyrochronological') vs :67 / :19 / :203 (age = 10 Gyr, Di Folco) and :332 (Pavlenko age 7 Gyr).

findings.field: spectral_type / planet f,g,h omission of tau Cet e
findings.claim: Report prose (lines 39-46, 388-398) frames tau Cet e's absence from the DB as an unresolved 'curation question' — 'Either deliberately dropped ... or an oversight in the Phase 2 ingest' — citing only the Feng 2017 dispute flag and rex-data-comparison, and logs it as an open item to 'decide whether to include e or formally drop it'.
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json:1964 meta_notes definitively records that tau Cet e was reclassified to 'False Positive Planet' by the NASA Exoplanet Archive on 2026-04-09, citing Figueira et al. 2025 ESPRESSO sub-10 cm/s RV non-detection (A&A 700, A174); 'DB retrieval 2026-05-22 correctly inherited e's absence.' The DB thus treats e's omission as a resolved, sourced retraction, not an oversight/open question. The report ignores the False-Positive reclassification and the Figueira 2025 source the DB carries.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json:1964 ('Tau Cet e ... reclassified to False Positive Planet by NASA Exoplanet Archive on 2026-04-09, citing Figueira et al. 2025 ...; DB retrieval 2026-05-22 correctly inherited e's absence') vs docs/phase3/tau-cet.md:393-398 ('Either deliberately dropped ... or an oversight in the Phase 2 ingest').

findings.claim: Bibliography (line 334-335) labels 'Baliunas S. L. et al. 1996 ... ApJ 438, 269' with bibcode `1995ApJ...438..269B`; the divergence alternative is cited as 'Baliunas 1996 Ca II HK ~34 d'.
findings.source_says: The bibcode `1995ApJ...438..269B` is a 1995 paper (Baliunas et al. 1995, Chromospheric variations II), not 1996, and the bib YAML instead lists `1996ApJ...457L..99B` for the same rotation alternative — an internal year/bibcode mismatch. The ~34 d value itself is corroborated secondhand by Korolik (cache 2307.10394.md:156: '34 days (Baliunas et al., 1996)'), so the number is fine; only the citation handle is inconsistent. Baliunas is not in the _papers cache (no arXiv) and is a non-cfg-decisive divergence alternative.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet.md:334-335 ('Baliunas ... 1996 ... (`1995ApJ...438..269B`)') vs docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml:70 (ads_bibcode 1996ApJ...457L..99B). Value corroborated: docs/phase3/_papers/2307.10394.md:156.

trappist-1-d.coverage_note: Decisions table has 42 rows. Every paper cited in the Decisions table is present in the _papers cache and was opened: Agol 2021 (2010.01074, abstract+Table 6 only), Piaulet-Ghorayeb 2025 (2508.08416), Way 2025 (2502.00132), Dobos 2019 (1902.03867), Kislyakova 2018 (1710.08761), RM22=Rodriguez-Mozos&Moya 2022 (2203.01065), Atri 2019 (1910.09871), Garraffo 2017 (1706.04617), Acuna 2021 (2101.08172), Perez 2025 (2511.10801, mislabeled 'Salman 2024'), plus context papers de Wit 2018, Moran 2018, Welbanks 2021. STRONGLY VERIFIED rows: semi_major_axis_au 0.02227, mass_mearth 0.388, radius_rearth 0.788, surface_gravity 0.624, density 4.35 (all match Agol Table 6 exactly and db/planets_curated.json recommended=Agol); equilibrium_temp_k A=0.3 262 (Piaulet abstract verbatim) and A=0 287 (re-derived 286.3 K from R*=0.1192 Rsun, Teff 2566); star_apparent_angular_diameter_deg 2.85 (re-derived 2.85), stellar_illumination_color_temp_k 2566 (confirmed via Way 2025 line 111 '2566 K for TRAPPIST-1'); atmosphere_surface_pressure_pa 1000 / 0.01 bar 3-sigma H2 ceiling (Piaulet Fig 3 / sec 7); ocean_present false at 0.772 S-earth condensation limit (Piaulet line 149, Turbet 2023); terminator_water_ice_clouds mbar stratospheric (Piaulet line 149/183); surface_morphology magma-ocean <=500 Myr relict (Piaulet line 163); dayside_surface_temp_k 390 vs Way Sim 01 364 K (Way line 58/103); induction_heating fraction 0.56 (Kislyakova 55.75%, line 53 — ROUNDING-OK); scale_height 12 km (re-derived 12.7). Physical consistency: e=0.00638 < 0.01 so the forced 1:1 tidal lock is NOT on an eccentric orbit (no PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE); equilibrium-temp/insolation/L/a all internally consistent; relict-magma morphology has cited interior-heating mechanisms (Dobos tidal T_eq 1645 K, Kislyakova induction) so not implausible; weak-field aurora is grounded in Garraffo 2017 open-field precipitation, not a reflexive default. No POLICY_VIOLATION: the cfg-variant / canonical alternatives (3:2 spin-orbit, wet A_B>=0.3, cooling-condensing) are flagged as variants in prose and Open-items, none inlined into a single Decisions cell. No PHASE2_DESYNC: report values match db/planets_curated.json recommended Agol 2021 measurement. The only NOT-fully-verified rows are the three orbital values (e, P, omega) whose specific numbers are absent from the cached Agol excerpt and from the DB (UNVERIFIABLE, not VALUE_MISMATCH — values are plausible literature numbers), plus the Salman->Perez WRONG_ATTRIBUTION and one unsupported prose figure (Acuna '33 W/m2'). 38 of 42 rows verified against a cacheable source.

findings.field: atmosphere_composition (Basis citation via Atmosphere-synthesis prose + Bibliography)
findings.claim: Report cites the outgassing/fO2 paper arXiv:2511.10801 as 'Salman 2024' (Atmosphere synthesis line 149 'supported by outgassing models (Liggins 2021, Salman 2024)'; Bibliography line 242-244 '2511.10801 Salman 2024 — Atmosphere-interior model with carbon cycle'). Used to support the trace-CO2 + H2O composition decision.
findings.source_says: arXiv:2511.10801 is 'What's Inside Matters: The Effect of Oxygen Fugacity and Initial Volatile Abundance on the Atmospheres of the TRAPPIST-1 Planets', first author Junellie Perez, year 2025 (bibcode 2025arXiv251110801P, pubdate 2025-11-13). There is no author named Salman; second author is Schaefer. Year is 2025, not 2024.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2511.10801.md line 1 title 'What's Inside Matters...'; docs/phase3/_bib/trappist-1-d.yaml:1116 'ads_bibcode: 2025arXiv251110801P', :1119-1126 authors '- Junellie Perez / - Laura K. Schaefer / ...' year '2025'. The science (fO2 controls CO2/H2/CO/H2O outgassing for T-1 d) IS supported by the paper abstract; only the author name and year are wrong.

findings.claim: eccentricity = 0.00638, confidence 'high', Basis 'Agol 2021 TTV'. This specific value drives the report's e<0.01 argument for stable 1:1 tidal lock and 'no seasons / insolation variation <0.4%'.
findings.source_says: The cached Agol 2021 excerpt contains only the abstract + Table 6 (R, M, rho, g, S, a, M-cgs, rho-cgs). The abstract states only 'the eccentricities of the planets are very small'. No per-planet eccentricity value (0.00638) appears in the cache, and db/planets_curated.json TRAPPIST-1 d 'orbital' block carries no eccentricity field. Value cannot be confirmed offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md line 7 ('eccentricities of the planets are very small') and Table 6 (lines 16-25, no e column); db/planets_curated.json TRAPPIST-1 d orbital = {semi_major_axis_au 0.02227, inclination_deg 89.896, ...} no eccentricity. docs/phase3/trappist-1-d.md:30

findings.claim: sidereal_period_days = 4.0508, confidence 'high', Basis 'Agol 2021'. Drives the Kopernicus rotationPeriod (349,989 s) cfg value (report line 184).
findings.source_says: The cached Agol 2021 excerpt (abstract + Table 6) contains no orbital-period column or value for d; 4.0508 d is not present in the cache. db/planets_curated.json TRAPPIST-1 d orbital block has no period field. Cannot be confirmed offline (4.0508 d * 86400 = 349989 s is internally consistent, but the source number is unverifiable).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md (Table 6 lines 16-25 list a, M, R, rho, g, S only — no period); db/planets_curated.json TRAPPIST-1 d orbital has no period. docs/phase3/trappist-1-d.md:32

findings.claim: argument_of_periastron_deg = 234, confidence 'medium', Basis 'Agol 2021 (low ecc -> weak constraint)'.
findings.source_says: The cached Agol 2021 excerpt contains no argument-of-periastron / longitude-of-periastron value for d (only Table 6 + abstract). db/planets_curated.json TRAPPIST-1 d orbital block has no periastron field. The value 234 deg cannot be confirmed offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md (no omega/periastron content); db/planets_curated.json TRAPPIST-1 d orbital block. docs/phase3/trappist-1-d.md:31

findings.field: Interior-atmosphere coupling prose (Acuna 2021, not a Decisions row)
findings.claim: Atmosphere-synthesis prose (line 156) states Acuna 2021 characterizes d's hydrosphere as 'not in global radiative balance (absorbed flux ~ 33 W/m2 below OLR), implying d is gradually cooling.' Used to justify the preserved 'cooling-and-condensing' cfg variant.
findings.source_says: Acuna 2021 explicitly does NOT compute OLR / radiative-balance for temperate planets like d: 'we only calculate the atmospheric parameters (OLR, surface temperature, albedo and thickness) for planets that present their hydrospheres in steam phase' (i.e., b and c only); for temperate planets it 'set[s] the surface temperature in our interior model to their equilibrium temperatures assuming an albedo zero'. The '33 W/m2 below OLR' figure for d is not present in the paper. (The WMF 0.036-0.084 part of the citation IS correct: Table 3 WMF=0.084+/-0.071, Table 4 WMF=0.036+/-0.028.)
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2101.08172.md line 103 ('we only calculate the atmospheric parameters... for planets that present their hydrospheres in steam phase') and Tables 3-4 lines 123,133 (WMF d = 0.084 / 0.036). docs/phase3/trappist-1-d.md:156

proxima-cen-d.coverage_note: 33 Decisions rows total. Core orbital/bulk rows (tidally_locked, obliquity, eccentricity, sidereal_period, semi_major_axis, mass) are cacheable AND DB-aligned: Faria 2022 (2202.05188) and SM25 (2507.21751) are both in the planet/host bib and fully cached, and db/planets_curated.json 'Proxima Cen d' confirms period 5.12338, a 0.02881, Msini 0.26, recommended SM25 e=0 — no PHASE2_DESYNC. Derived rows (radius 0.65 / gravity 0.62 / density 5.2 / insolation 1.9) are mutually self-consistent. Decision-driving citations with NO cacheable source: Killen 2007 (atmosphere_surface_pressure_pa basis) — absent from all bibs and cache (UNSOURCED); Makarov 2012 (rotation resonance reasoning) — no such paper in bib/cache (UNSOURCED); Lee 2021 (2109.06963, escape-rate basis) — present as a 1-line stub only, no body text and not in any bib (UNVERIFIABLE). Author/header checks PASSED for Atri 2020, Walterova & Behounkova 2020, Vida 2019 (Vida confirms Teff 2980 K, superflare 10^33 erg ~3/yr, L 0.00151 L☉). WRONG_ATTRIBUTION issues: the SM25 'high inclination precluding transits' claim (SM25 actually says inclination unknown) and the Boyajian 2012 'Proxima R=0.1542 R☉' claim (Boyajian explicitly excludes GJ 551 from its radius relation). Two derived-value errors in the equilibrium-temperature rows (357 K and 326 K) are internally/physically inconsistent with the stated S=1.9. Report claims '32 cfg picks' but the table has 33 rows (cosmetic). Of the 33 rows, ~30 had a cacheable source path; 3 distinct decision/context citations (Killen, Makarov, Lee) had no usable cached text.

findings.claim: equilibrium_temp_k (A=0) = 357 K, confidence high, 'derived'
findings.source_says: With the report's own insolation S=1.9 (and L=0.00155 L☉, a=0.029 AU), the A=0 equilibrium temperature is T = 278.5 K x S^0.25 = 327 K, not 357 K. To obtain 357 K would require S ~ 2.7. The 357 K value is inconsistent with the stated S/L/a.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-d.md:46 (row) and :45 (insolation 1.9) / :10-11 (L=0.00155, S~1.9). Recompute: 278.5*1.9**0.25 = 327 K.

findings.claim: equilibrium_temp_k (A=0.3) = 326 K, confidence high, 'derived; rocky bond albedo'
findings.source_says: For S=1.9 and A=0.3 the correct value is T = 278.5*(0.7)^0.25*1.9^0.25 = 299 K, not 326 K. The quoted 326 K is actually the A=0 result (327 K). The two equilibrium-temp rows are mutually inconsistent: the A=0.3 cell carries the A=0 number while the A=0 cell (357 K) matches no consistent S. Intro line 11-12 repeats '326 K (A=0.3)', propagating the error.

findings.claim: atmosphere_surface_pressure_pa = 10^-9, Basis cites 'Killen 2007 Mercury exosphere review for analogy'
findings.source_says: not in cache; 'Killen 2007' appears in NEITHER docs/phase3/_bib/proxima-cen-d.yaml NOR docs/phase3/_bib/proxima-cen.yaml NOR docs/phase3/_papers/. The cited source could never have been verified offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-d.md:52 (and :121 prose). grep 'Killen' across _bib/proxima-cen-d.yaml, _bib/proxima-cen.yaml returns nothing; not present in _papers/.

findings.claim: Intro (line 14-16) and SM25 Bibliography annotation (line 190-192): 'Suarez Mascareno 2025 §4 confirms a high orbital inclination that places transit geometry outside the photometric monitoring envelope' / 'constrains inclination to high values precluding transits'.
findings.source_says: SM25 (2507.21751) does NOT confirm a high orbital inclination. Line 428: 'we do not know the inclination of the system. A pole-on stellar alignment might hide the variations...'. The only inclination SM25 uses is the STELLAR rotation-axis tilt 47±7 deg from Klein et al. 2021 (line 382), applied via a coplanarity ASSUMPTION to estimate true masses (0.357 M⊕) — not a planet inclination measurement and not a transit-geometry statement.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2507.21751.md:428 ('we do not know the inclination of the system'); :382 (47±7 deg stellar axis from Klein 2021 + coplanarity assumption). Report claim at docs/phase3/proxima-cen-d.md:14-16, 190-192.

findings.claim: Bibliography line 186-187: 'Faria et al. 2022 ... Orbital eccentricity is consistent with circular at the precision of the fit.'
findings.source_says: Faria 2022 (2202.05188) line 126 reports the 5-day signal eccentricity posterior is 'quite wide, with a peak at 0.45' and a median of 0.33 (+0.13/-0.23); circular is only 'not excluded at the 2-sigma level' (line 128). The report's annotation overstates Faria's constraint as 'consistent with circular'. (SM25, not Faria, is the source that statistically adopts a circular model with e<0.25.) The cfg value eccentricity=0 itself matches the DB-recommended SM25 e=0, so the row value is acceptable; the attribution/prose is what is wrong.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2202.05188.md:126 ('peak at 0.45 ... estimate for the eccentricity of 0.33+0.13-0.23'), :128. SM25 e<0.25 at 2507.21751.md:307,374. Report at proxima-cen-d.md:186-187, :38.

findings.field: stellar radius (Boyajian 2012 Bibliography annotation feeding insolation)
findings.claim: Bibliography line 200-202: 'Boyajian T. S. et al. 2012 ... Proxima R = 0.1542 R☉; sets the insolation calculation.'
findings.source_says: 1208.2431 does NOT report a Proxima/GJ 551 radius of 0.1542 R☉. The paper explicitly EXCLUDES GJ 551 from its color-metallicity-radius relation ('we do not include GJ 551 in the analysis because the adopted functional form ... is not appropriate', line 83) and warns its results are 'unreliable for such red colors' (line 107); GJ 551 also yields a null radius result in the model comparison (line 187). No 0.1542 R☉ value is present. (Independently, the Vida cache gives Proxima R=0.146 R☉ from Ribas 2017.)
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1208.2431.md:83, :107, :187 (GJ 551 excluded / unreliable / null). Cross-ref Proxima R=0.146 R☉ in _papers/1907.12580.md table. Report at proxima-cen-d.md:200-202.

findings.claim: Atmosphere section line 111-114: 'Lee 2021 (2109.06963) found that even Proxima b at 0.05 AU experiences Venus-analog escape rates, and at d's 0.029 AU the loss rates scale up by another order of magnitude.'
findings.source_says: not in cache (full text absent). docs/phase3/_papers/2109.06963.md is a 1-line stub containing only '# Astrophysics > Earth and Planetary Astrophysics' — no body, no escape-rate numbers. Paper also absent from both bib YAMLs. Cannot verify the Venus-analog escape-rate claim offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2109.06963.md (1 line, category header only). Report at proxima-cen-d.md:111-114, :216-217. Drives the 'atmosphere_present=false' tie-break rationale only as context.

findings.claim: Rotation section line 144-145: 'only modest e is required to drive a higher-order resonance trap (Makarov 2012)'.
findings.source_says: not in cache; no 'Makarov 2012' paper exists in any Proxima bib YAML or _papers cache. The only Makarov references found are 'Efroimsky & Makarov 2014' / 'Makarov 2014' cited inside the Walterova paper's text (proxima-cen-b.yaml:4450,4487) — not a standalone 2012 source. Context-only (supports the 1:1 vs 3:2 reasoning), not a Decisions row value.
findings.evidence: grep 'Makarov' in _bib/proxima-cen-b.yaml:4450,4487 (only 2014, inside Walterova abstract). Report at proxima-cen-d.md:144-145.

findings.claim: star_apparent_angular_diameter_deg = 2.5, 'derived: 2 R★ / a x (180/pi)'
findings.source_says: Applying the stated formula with R★=0.1542 R☉ (the report's own stellar radius) and a=0.029 AU gives 2.83 deg; with R★=0.146 R☉ gives 2.68 deg. Either way ~7-12% above the quoted 2.5 deg. The '5x the Sun' prose (line 167-168) is closer to 4.7x (2.5/0.53).

findings.claim: surface_radiation_dose_msv_yr = 10^5 and flare_dose_event_msv = 10^4, both confidence 'high', attributed to 'Atri 2020 scaling'.
findings.source_says: Atri 2020 (1910.09871) reports doses in Gray (Gy), not mSv, and never computes a value for Proxima d: it gives ~1-10 Gy lethal hard-spectrum events, ~100 Gy for extremophiles, '1000s of Gy in extreme cases' for Proxima b (lines 31-33). The report's mSv/yr and mSv/event figures are unstated scaling extrapolations (100 Gy ~= 10^5 mSv, 10 Gy ~= 10^4 mSv are order-of-magnitude consistent), so the magnitudes are plausible but the 'high' confidence and direct Atri attribution overstate paper support; the specific numbers are not in the paper.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1910.09871.md:31-33 (doses in Gy; no mSv, no Proxima d number). Report at proxima-cen-d.md:63, :66.

yz-cet-d.coverage_note: All 28 Decisions rows had a resolvable source: every cited paper (Stock et al. 2020 = 2002.01772.md, Pineda & Villadsen 2023 = 2304.00031.md, Trigilio et al. 2023 = 2305.00809.md, Astudillo-Defru et al. 2017 = 1708.03336.md, Cifuentes et al. 2020 = 2007.15077.md) is present in both the host bib YAML and the _papers cache. Note: the planet-level bib docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet-d.yaml does NOT exist; only the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet.yaml was available (it covers all five papers). Verified cleanly against the cache: orbital P=4.65626 d, a=0.02851 AU, e=0.07±0.05, ω=200 deg, M sin i=1.09±0.12 (all from Stock 2020 'Stable 3 Planet + GP (run c)' table, 2002.01772.md:21-25,281-282); R_d≈1.04 R⊕ semi-empirical estimate (originates in Stock 2020 2002.01772.md:265, and Trigilio 2305.00809.md:37 does cite R_d=1.04 R⊕ as the report claims); Teff=3100 K (Cifuentes 2007.15077.md:331); discovery-paper context P=4.65627/e=0.129/Msini=1.14 (Astudillo-Defru 1708.03336.md:87-90). DB cross-check (db/planets_curated.json) matches the report exactly for YZ Cet d, including recommended=Stock 2020 and recommended:false=Astudillo-Defru 2017 — no PHASE2_DESYNC. All six derived rows (insolation 2.7, T_eq 357/327, surface gravity 1.03, angular diameter 2.94) reproduce arithmetically from L=0.0022 L☉, a=0.02851 AU, R★=0.1571 R☉. Internal sibling-temperature consistency holds (d 357 K vs c 410 K vs b 471 K; '~50 K cooler than c, 110 K cooler than b' checks out). Aesthetic tie-break rows (density, atmosphere presence/pressure/composition, tints, morphology, day/night temps, bond albedo) are correctly low-confidence within-window choices with the airless variant preserved in Open items — no POLICY_VIOLATION, no molten/lava claim, no reflexive aurora (SPI explicitly attributed away from d). Only 2 rows had defects, both low-severity: an over-ranked 'worst of the three' SPI characterization and the uncached Vinson 2017 / Makarov 2018 spin-orbit theory citations (non-cfg-decisive).

findings.claim: Basis: 'the SPI bursts fold to b's orbit, not d's (worst phase agreement of the three)' — and prose (lines 19-20, 105-106) states 'd shows the worst radio phase agreement of the three' / 'Trigilio 2023 find the worst agreement of the three for d'.
findings.source_says: Neither paper ranks d as the single worst of the three. Pineda & Villadsen (2304.00031.md:41): 'We also applied a similar analysis to planets c and d, finding worse burst agreement in both time and phase' — c and d grouped, not d singled out. Trigilio (2305.00809.md:83): 'While for planet c and d, detections and non detections are randomly mixed around the orbits, for planet b the phases ... appear in two groups' — c and d grouped together as randomly mixed.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2304.00031.md:41 and docs/phase3/_papers/2305.00809.md:83; vs docs/phase3/yz-cet-d.md:19-20,55,106

findings.source_says: not in cache. Vinson 2017 and Makarov 2018 are absent from both the host bib YAML (docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet.yaml — no vinson/makarov entry) and the _papers cache (the only grep hits, e.g. 1712.05641.md, 1902.03867.md, are TRAPPIST-1 papers that merely cite Vinson/Makarov in their reference lists, not the papers themselves). findings.evidence: docs/phase3/yz-cet-d.md:132-133; grep of docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet.yaml (no match) and docs/phase3/_papers/*.md (only incidental citations inside unrelated TRAPPIST-1 papers). Note: not cfg-decisive — the tidally_locked=true / obliquity_deg=0 rows cite the standard tidal-locking-timescale argument as Basis, not Vinson/Makarov; the e=0.07 (>0.01) value tensions with a forced 1:1 lock, but the report explicitly argues e is poorly constrained (σ≈0.05, near-circular) rather than asserting the lock reflexively.

trappist-1-g.coverage_note: 53 Decisions rows. Every paper cited in the report's Bibliography is present in _papers/ (all 26 checked arXiv IDs cached). The separate host bib docs/phase3/_bib/trappist-1.yaml does NOT exist; the planet bib docs/phase3/_bib/trappist-1-g.yaml (76k lines) covers the system. Sources with FULLY usable cached full text that verified the cfg-decisive claims: Unterborn 2018 (>=50 wt% water for f/g), Turbet 2018 (150+/-1 mbar, 174+/-0.1 K CO2 equilibrium for g - exact), Berardo 2025 (L_XUV=1.83e28 erg/s, ~30x 2017, ~45 min / 1e29 erg microflares, 1e30 erg / 6 hr JWST flares - exact), Van Looveren 2024 (1 bar per 5 Myr at 14 F_EUV, g=120 F_EUV - exact), Hay & Matsuyama 2019 (g 2-20% planet-planet tides, others <1% - exact), Acuna 2021 (WMF>=0.14 uninhabitable / >=0.10 <50% habitable - exact), Fauchez 2019 (almost-fully-ice-covered snowball quote + 0.1 kg/m2 clouds - exact), Wakeford 2019 (2400 K phot / ~35% 3000 K spot / <3% ~5800 K facula; report's '1% facula 5825 K' is a minor restatement). Agol 2021 (2010.01074): cache holds ONLY abstract + Table 6, which verifies mass=1.321, radius=1.129, a=0.04683, g=1.035, density=5.042, S=0.252 - but eccentricity (0.00208), sidereal_period (12.3524), argument_of_periastron (191), equilibrium_temp, and stellar color temp (2566 K) are NOT in the Agol cache; the cache explicitly notes Agol has no T_eq column. Stellar color temp 2566 K and mass/radius/a were independently confirmed against db/stellar_props_curated.json and db/planets_curated.json (recommended source Agol et al. 2021, bibcode 2021PSJ.....2....1A) - no PHASE2_DESYNC. Sources present but with the report's specific g-value NOT recoverable from cached text (UNVERIFIABLE-as-stated): Wolf 2017 (full text missing, abstract only), Grayver 2022, RM22 (2203.01065), Zhang&Rogers (2208.06523), Garraffo 2017 (cache contradicts the report's 'well-organized 4 R_p' framing). No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE triggers: e=0.00208 (<0.01) so 1:1 tidal lock is fine; surface is a 175 K snowball (no lava claim); T_eq(A=0)=194 K and angular diameter 1.36 deg are arithmetically consistent with S=0.252, a=0.04683, R*~0.117-0.119 Rsun. No POLICY_VIOLATION: cryovolcanism promotion and atmosphere/pressure variants are documented as preserved cfg alternatives in prose and Open items (interesting-first rule applied transparently).

Any T_eq value attributed to Agol 2021 is a derived value, not a table entry.' 194 K is a derived value (computed 197 K from S=0.252, A=0); it should not be attributed to Agol as a sourced number, and is marked 'high' confidence.

findings.claim: bond_albedo 0.55, surface_temp_substellar 200 K, nightside 130 K, global_mean 175 K, all cited 'Wolf 2017 cold-snowball / GCM'; intro 'Wolf 2017 §5 finds complete snowball below ~0.3 S⊕'.

The abstract is recoverable but contains NO albedo, surface-temperature, or '§5 / 0.3 S⊕' numbers. Abstract does state outer planets f/g/h 'cannot be warmed despite as much as 30 bar CO2 ... entering a snowball state' (report's prose says '10 bar' / '~0.3 S⊕', neither of which is in the abstract).

findings.claim: magnetosphere_standoff_planet_radii = 4 | medium | 'Garraffo 2017 Fig. 4 - outer planets less compressed than b/c; 4-5 R_p plausible at g's orbit'; prose 'g a relatively well-organized magnetosphere with standoff at ~4 R_p'; '50-80% of each orbit sub-Alfvenic per Garraffo 2017'.
findings.source_says: Garraffo 2017 (1706.04617) gives no ~4 R_p standoff for g and reaches the opposite qualitative conclusion: 'all magnetospheres are greatly compressed'; 'If these planets have magnetospheres, they would be much smaller than that of the Earth' (~10 R_earth); for close-in planets 'there is no magnetopause at which the planetary field pressure balances the wind pressure ... The concept of atmospheric protection by a planetary magnetic field does not hold.' No 50-80% sub-Alfvenic figure is stated (only 'large fraction of orbital period').
findings.evidence: _papers/1706.04617.md:7 (abstract), :59-61 (standoff much smaller than Earth), :65 ('no magnetopause...protection does not hold'). docs/phase3/trappist-1-g.md:67,262-263,290.

The paper models flare/ICME ohmic heating and explicitly distinguishes itself from the classic motional 'induction heating' (Kislyakova 2017) that the row label implies. The specific number cannot be confirmed offline.

teegardens-star.coverage_note: All 15 Decisions rows in docs/phase3/teegardens-star.md (lines 77-91) were verified against cached sources; every cited decision-driving paper is present in docs/phase3/_papers/ (Schweitzer 2019 = 1904.03231, Zechmeister 2019 = 1906.07196, Dreizler 2024 = 2402.00923, Fuhrmeister 2025 = 2504.02338, Marfil 2021 = 2110.07329, Cifuentes 2020 = 2007.15077 — all status:fetched in the bib YAML), so coverage was complete and no row was UNVERIFIABLE. Three secondary attributions (Alonso-Floriano 2015 spectral type, Rojas-Ayala 2012 metallicity/Teff, Lafarga 2021 rotation) are NOT in the bib papers list nor cached as primaries, but the report explicitly cites them 'via Zechmeister 2019 Table 1' / 'via Dreizler 2024 Table 2', and the exact values are confirmed in those cached secondary tables — so they are properly sourced, not UNSOURCED. Verification anchors: Schweitzer 1904.03231.md line 214 (R=0.107+/-0.004, M=0.089+/-0.009 for the M7 V Teegarden) and Zechmeister 1906.07196.md Table 1 lines 45-65 (d=3.831+/-0.004, V=15.08, J=8.39, Sp M7.0 V Alo15, Teff 2904+/-51 Sch19, L 0.00073 Sch19, [Fe/H] -0.19 Sch19 / -0.55 Roj12, Teff 2637 Roj12, age 7+/-3 / >8, W=-58.68, logLHa/Lbol=-5.25, X-ray <-4.23) cover the fundamental + age rows; Dreizler 2402.00923.md Table 2 lines 101-107 (Prot 96.2 Laf21; alt M 0.097+/-0.010, R 0.120+/-0.012; Teff 3034 Marf21) plus lines 93/227/257 (Terrien ~100 d, Kemmer 98 d; 172 d kept as activity, 96 d as rotation; ~2500 d possible cycle) cover rotation + the radius/mass alternative; Fuhrmeister 2504.02338.md line 137/157 (logLx/Lbol=-4.9 Chandra), Table 1 line 32 (XMM -5.0..-4.81), Table 3 lines 120/124 (the two TESS flares 1.3e32 and 4.3e31 erg), line 129 (2.6+/-1.8 flares/100 d), abstract line 6 (He I IR triplet absent, Ha filled-in, Neupert, erupting-prominence/CME) cover the activity + flare rows. DB cross-check (db/stellar_props_curated.json, db/planets_curated.json) matches the report's recommended/alternative picks exactly — no PHASE2_DESYNC. The '## Canonical alternatives' section is present and correctly used for the only two documented divergences (radius/mass; metallicity); the visual-tint row is correctly labeled a tie-break, not a divergence. Two non-findings worth noting for transparency: (a) the Decisions flare_state range '~10^31-10^32 erg' matches the two large TESS flares' per-flare table energies (4.3e31, 1.3e32) rather than the abstract's broader fluence span (10^29-10^32) — defensible, the report is describing the two large flares specifically; (b) the DB rotation record labels method 'photometric_variability'/Dreizler 2024, whereas the report (correctly, per the cached Dreizler Table 2 'Laf21' and line 93) attributes it to Lafarga 2021 spectroscopic activity indices — the report is more accurate than the DB label, so no finding against the report. The report's '~3 sigma' radius-divergence figure is the report's own characterization (0.013/0.004 ~ 3.25 sigma vs the recommended value's error bar); the cached papers state no explicit sigma, but the framing is reasonable and not cfg-decisive. No VALUE_MISMATCH, WRONG_ATTRIBUTION, ROUNDING, UNSOURCED, UNVERIFIABLE, INTERNAL_CONTRADICTION, PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE, or POLICY_VIOLATION findings.

Derived rows recomputed correct: insolation 0.274, T_eq(A=0) 202 K, T_eq(A=0.3) 184 K, surface_gravity 1.20, density 3.66, angular diameter 0.317 deg. Of 43 rows, ~12 are directly source-cited measured/derived rows (all verified against the cache), ~28 are explicitly-flagged low-confidence theoretical defaults / tie-breaks (Wordsworth 2015, Pierrehumbert 2011, Kopparapu 2014, Vinson 2017, Bolmont, Reiners-Christensen RM22) whose framework references are mostly NOT pinned in the bib YAML but drive only low-confidence non-cfg-decisive rows (Kopparapu 1404.5292.md and Vinson 1705.09685.md happen to be in cache; Wordsworth/Pierrehumbert/Bolmont specific papers are not pinned). The one cacheable-source FAILURE is stellar_illumination_color_temp_k, mis-attributed to a non-existent \"Pavlenko 2012\" (the recommended Teff 5370 belongs to Korolik 2023 per the host report and DB). Feng 2018 (1801.05415) is referenced in prose/bib but NOT cached (UNVERIFIABLE), though it drives no Decisions row. rows_verified counts the 39 rows with a confirmed or correctly-derived source; the 4 not fully verified are the stellar_illumination_color_temp_k attribution and the three low-severity internal/unverifiable items above (some findings span the same row).

findings.claim: stellar_illumination_color_temp_k = 5370, Basis: "host Teff (Pavlenko 2012)". The Bibliography also lists "Pavlenko Y. V. et al. 2012 — host star [Fe/H] = -0.55, log R'HK = -4.95, Teff = 5344 K".

No Pavlenko 2012 paper exists in the host bib YAML or the _papers cache.

Note: the planet bib docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet-b.yaml does NOT exist; only the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet.yaml exists, which supplied all bibcode<->arxiv_id mappings. So 28/28 Decisions rows had a cacheable/checkable source (no UNVERIFIABLE or UNSOURCED rows). 27 rows fully verified. Orbit/mass rows (P=2.02087 d, a=0.01634 AU, e=0.06±0.06, ω=197°, Msini=0.70±0.09) match Stock 2020 Table 8 stable solution AND db/planets_curated.json exactly (no PHASE2_DESYNC; the 1.96876 d Astudillo-Defru solution is recommended:false in both). All derived quantities recompute correctly (g=0.84, density=5.07, S=8.24 vs Stock 8.21, T_eq A=0 = 471.5 vs Stock 471.2, T_eq A=0.3 = 431.3, angular diameter = 5.12°). Magnetic-field rows (B_planet≥0.4 G, R_MP≥1.6-2.0 R_pl, magnetosphere required, a/R★=21.9, R_Alf≈100 R★, sub-Alfvénic) all match Trigilio 2023 §6.2/6.5. Synodic 2.082 d and P_rot 68.46 d match Pineda. Teff 3100 K matches Cifuentes 2020. SPI 'confirmed' framing is correctly attributed to Trigilio (4.37σ/99.992%); Pineda only calls YZ Cet an 'SPI candidate', which the report correctly assigns to Pineda as the earlier candidate-detection. No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE (report explicitly argues warm-not-molten at 471 K; magnetosphere is sourced to Trigilio, not a reflexive default; tidal lock is on a near-circular e=0.06 orbit which the report justifies vs 3:2). No POLICY_VIOLATION (divergences are labeled 'tie-break', airless variant preserved in Open items). The only deviation is the radius value 0.913 vs the cited paper's 0.93 (~1.8% off), a low-severity rounding/restatement issue on an explicitly low-confidence ESTIMATE row.

trappist-1-c.coverage_note: 50 Decisions rows audited. Cacheable & verified against full text: Agol 2021 / 2010.01074 (a, M, R, g, density confirmed via Table 6); Barr 2018 / 1712.05641 (tidal 1.32 W/m2, T_eq mantle 1666 K, density 7642 kg/m3, ~50% Fe, highest density — all confirmed); Dobos 2019 / 1902.03867 (0.62 +0.42/-0.53 W/m2, T_eq 1659 K confirmed); Acuña 2021 / 2101.08172 (WMF 4.4e-6, CMF values confirmed; scenario mislabel noted); Brasser 2019 / 1905.00512 (k2/Q 0.4-2e-4 + dynamical 1e-3, MoI C=0.286 range 0.235-0.4 — exact match); Lincowski 2023 / 2308.05899 (0.1 bar O2 within 1σ, Venus 2.6σ, 1-10 bar 2.0-2.9σ, up to 10% H2O — all confirmed); Ducrot 2025 / 2509.02128 (c dayside 369±23 K, no nightside detection, T_eq 340 K — confirmed); Kislyakova 2018 / 1710.08761 (68.28% induction fraction, highest in system, 17%/56% for b/d — confirmed); RM22 / 2203.01065 (0.01-0.1 M_earth dynamo cluster supported, low-conf scaling). NOT cacheable / unverifiable: Zieba 2023 / 2306.10150 cache file is EMPTY (1 byte) — the 380 K dayside brightness temp could not be checked (though its 421 ppm depth is corroborated by Lincowski 2023); Garraffo 2017 standoff is figure-based (text lacks the number); Agol 2021 cache is abstract+Table 6 only, so eccentricity/period/argument-of-periastron rows attributed to Agol are unverifiable. UNSOURCED (in neither bib YAML nor cache): Grayver 2022 (induction_heating_w_m2 row + prose) and Vidotto 2013 (aurora_oval row) — 0 hits in the c bib. Prose-only refs Luger & Barnes 2015 (1411.7412) and Lincowski 2018 are also absent from the cache. Roughly 42/50 rows trace to a cacheable source; ~8 rest on figure-only, empty-cache, or unsourced references.

hd-219134-h.coverage_note: 35 Decisions rows total. The decisive anchor source — Vogt et al. 2015 (arXiv 1509.07912) — IS in the _papers cache and the host bib YAML, and every Vogt-cited row (eccentricity 0.06[0.04], omega 215[50], mean anomaly 209[56], P 2247[43] d, a 3.11 AU, Msini 0.34±0.02 M_Jup = 108 M⊕, 'similar to the mass of Saturn') verifies exactly against Table 10 of the cached paper AND db/planets_curated.json (recommended Vogt entry, identical values). The inherited stellar values (L=0.264±0.004 L⊙, Teff=4858 K, R=0.726 R⊙) verify against the cached Ligi 2019 paper (1909.10058, Table 4) and db/stellar_props_curated.json. All project-formula derivations recompute correctly: insolation 0.027 (=0.264/3.11²=0.0273), T_eq A=0 113 K (=278.3·0.264^0.25/√3.11=113.1), T_eq A=0.3 104 K (103.5), angular diameter 0.124° (0.1244), peri/apo 0.024→0.030 S⊕. The ONLY cacheable decision sources are Vogt and (via host) Ligi — both present. Four cited supporting papers (Fortney 2007, Lodders 2003, Li 2018, Hanel 1983) are in NEITHER the hd-219134 bib YAML NOR the _papers cache → UNSOURCED, but each drives only low/medium-confidence aesthetic-or-estimate rows. Two computational errors are independent of source availability: surface_gravity (4x too high, high severity, contradicts Jupiter/Saturn calibration of its own formula) and scale_height (35 km matches neither g=37 nor g=9.34). DB parity: no PHASE2_DESYNC — DB has exactly one recommended Vogt orbital + Msini entry for h, matching the report; no disk entry for HD 219134 (consistent with ring flagged unobserved/artistic).

findings.claim: g = G M / R² with M = 0.34 M_Jup, R = 0.95 R_Jup ≈ 37 m/s² ≈ 3.8 g⊕ (value 3.8)
findings.source_says: Self-derivation is arithmetically wrong by ~4x. G·(0.34 M_Jup)/(0.95 R_Jup)² = 9.34 m/s² ≈ 0.95 g⊕, not 37 m/s²/3.8 g⊕. Sanity checks: same formula reproduces Jupiter (24.78 vs known 24.79 m/s²) and Saturn (10.43 vs known ~10.4 m/s²). A Saturn-mass body at 0.95 R_Jup must have Saturn-like ~10 m/s² gravity. 37 m/s² would require R≈0.48 R_Jup, contradicting the report's own 0.95 R_Jup.

findings.claim: H = kT/(μ m_H g) with T = 113 K, μ = 2.3, g ≈ 37 m/s² ≈ 35 km (value 35)
findings.source_says: The stated value matches neither input. With the report's own (wrong) g=37 m/s², H = 11.0 km, not 35 km. With the physically correct g=9.34 m/s², H = 43.4 km. The 35 km figure is inconsistent with its own cited formula and with the corrected gravity; it inherits and compounds the surface_gravity error.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-h.md:56. Recomputed H=kT/(mu*mH*g)/1000: g=37 -> 11.0 km; g=9.34 -> 43.4 km. Neither equals 35.

findings.claim: 0.95 R_Jup, citing 'Fortney 2007 evolutionary tracks for 0.34 M_Jup at Gyr ages give ~0.9–1.0 R_Jup'

The ~0.9–1.0 R_Jup claim cannot be verified offline. Decision-driving: this radius propagates into surface_gravity, density, scale_height, and both ring-edge rows.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-219134-h.md:45,187-191; bib docs/phase3/_bib/hd-219134.yaml lists no Fortney; ls docs/phase3/_papers/ has no 0612671/fortney.

The 5 papers the report relies on are ALL present in both docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet.yaml and the _papers cache: Stock et al. 2020 (bibcode 2020A&A...636A.119S -> arxiv 2002.01772, cached), Pineda & Villadsen 2023 (2304.00031, cached), Trigilio et al. 2023 (2305.00809, cached), Astudillo-Defru et al. 2017 (2017A&A...605L..11A -> 1708.03336, cached), Cifuentes et al. 2020 (2020A&A...642A.115C -> 2007.15077, cached; per-star Teff/L is a VizieR catalog value inherited from the host synthesis yz-cet.md, not in the ar5iv body, consistent with the bib note). Note: the report itself cites no bib file at docs/phase3/_bib/yz-cet-c.yaml (no per-planet bib exists); the host bib yz-cet.yaml covers all c citations. Decisive numbers all confirmed exact against the cache: P=3.05989 d (2002.01772:219, 'run c' column), a=0.02156 AU (=2.156e-2 au, :282), M sin i=1.14+0.11 M⊕ (:281), e=0 fixed (:222), R_c≈1.05 R⊕ (:265), T_eq=410.3 K (:283), S=4.72 (:284). SPI rows confirmed: bursts fold to planet b not c, c/d 'randomly mixed' (2305.00809:83) and 'worse burst agreement' for c/d (2304.00031:41); planet b polar field >=0.4 G (2305.00809 abstract). DB (db/planets_curated.json:5196-5248) is fully in sync: recommended Stock 2020 orbit+mass, Astudillo-Defru 2017 retained recommended:false (P=3.06008 d, M sin i=0.98 M⊕) matching report context row. Derived values recomputed and verified (g≈1.034, angular diameter 3.88°, 264374 s, T_eq A=0.3=375.5). No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE (warm-not-molten at 410 K well below solidus; tidal lock on circular e=0 orbit; no reflexive aurora — magnetic field left 'unconstrained', visual section explicitly 'No SPI aurora'). No POLICY_VIOLATION (aesthetic choices consistently labeled 'tie-break' not 'divergence'; airless variant preserved in Open items; no canonical measured value overridden). Only one low-severity attribution nuance found: report calls T_eq/insolation 'computed here'/'derived' when Stock 2020 already tabulates the same values — numbers correct, attribution slightly under-credits the source.

findings.claim: Report presents T_eq (A=0) = 410 K and insolation S = 4.7 as values 'computed here'/'derived' by the report author (lines 11-13, 42-43, 72): 'T_eq ≈ 410 K ... (computed here; T_eq = 278.3·L^0.25/√a)' and 'insolation_s_earth | 4.7 | high | derived = L / a²'.
findings.source_says: Stock et al. 2020 (2002.01772) Table 9 already reports these for planet c directly: T_eq = 410.3 +2.0/-1.9 K and S = 4.72 +0.09/-0.09 S⊕. The report's self-computed numbers (410.5 K, 4.73) reproduce the cited paper's published values almost exactly.
findings.evidence: 2002.01772.md:283 'T eq (K) ... 410.3' and :284 'S (S⊕) ... 4.72'; vs docs/phase3/yz-cet-c.md:11-13,42-43. Values agree to <0.5%; only the attribution ('computed here'/'derived') under-credits the cited paper which already tabulates them.

trappist-1-e.coverage_note: 52 Decisions rows total. Cacheable/verifiable sources: most rows resolve to papers present with full text in docs/phase3/_papers/ - Agol 2021 (2010.01074, abstract+Table 6 only), Glidden 2025 (2509.05407/05414), THAI II (2109.11459), Cohen 2022 (2211.11887), Boldog 2023 (2312.01893), Fraschetti 2019 (1902.03732), Wang 2025 (2504.16662), Atri 2019 (1910.09871), Grayver 2022 (2211.06140), Bolmont 2020 (2010.04587), Acuna 2021 (2101.08172), Vidotto 2013 (1306.4789), Turbet 2018 (1707.06927) - all readable. THREE cited sources are NOT verifiable offline: (1) Wolf 2017 (1703.05815) is in the cache but is a 1-line category-header stub with no body - this drives the surface_temp_substellar/nightside/global_mean rows and ocean_extent/ice_caps (UNVERIFIABLE); (2) 'Hapi 2024' (bond_albedo) does not exist in any bib or cache (UNSOURCED/fabricated); (3) Bourgeois 2025 (2510.18704) and 2310.15992 are also body-less stubs but do not directly drive any Decisions row. Agol 2021 is present but its cache is abstract+Table 6 only (M,R,rho,S,a) - it has NO eccentricity, argument-of-periastron, sidereal-period, obliquity, or equilibrium-temperature values, and explicitly notes it reports no T_eq; rows citing Agol for eccentricity(0.00510)/arg_periastron(108)/sidereal_period(6.1010) rest on uncacheable detail (treated as verified-by-corroboration where the system period 6.10 d is well established, but the exact ecc/omega digits are not in cache). Net: 44 rows fully verified, 8 flagged (1 high VALUE_MISMATCH, 1 medium UNSOURCED, 2 medium WRONG_ATTRIBUTION, 2 medium UNVERIFIABLE, 2 low ROUNDING). The Glidden/THAI/Cohen/Boldog/Fraschetti/Wang atmospheric, cloud, ocean-probability, proton-flux and magnetic-field claims all check out cleanly against the cached papers.

findings.claim: 12000 mSv/yr (12 Sv/yr), 'high' confidence; Basis: 'Atri 2019 (1910.09871) Table 6 for e at 0.028 AU + 1 bar column + Earth-like B-field; 5000x Earth's 2.4'.
findings.source_says: Atri 2019 Table 6 is an ENHANCEMENT FACTOR over GCR background (dimensionless), not a Sv/yr dose. The e row (1910.09871.md:70) reads '| TRAPPIST-1 e | 0.028 | 7.42E+00 | 5.07E+00 | 1.35E+00 | 3.90E-03 |' (enhancement factors 7.42..0.0039 for decreasing column; the 1000 g/cm2 / 1-bar column value is ~3.9E-3). The paper's only absolute e doses are per-event: '1.23 Gy' (30 g/cm2) and '1.3E-3 Gy' (1000 g/cm2) for a single 10 GeV hard event (1910.09871.md:35). There is NO '12 Sv/yr baseline', no '5000x Earth 2.4 mSv' computation anywhere in the cache.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1910.09871.md:35 ('the surface radiation dose for a planet with ... 1000 g cm-2 atmospheric depth to be 1.3 x 10-3 Gy') and 1910.09871.md:70 (Table 6 enhancement-factor row for TRAPPIST-1 e); vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:73

findings.claim: 0.30, 'medium'; Basis: 'Earth + Hapi 2024 aqua-planet GCM range 0.25-0.35'.
findings.source_says: No author 'Hapi' (any year) exists in the e bib YAML or in any TRAPPIST bib YAML, and no such paper is in the _papers cache. grep for 'Hapi,' / 'Hapi ' as an author returns nothing across all docs/phase3/_bib/*.yaml. The citation appears fabricated. (The 0.30 value itself is plausible: THAI II planetary albedos are 0.22-0.28 per 2109.11459.md:147, but the cited source does not exist.)
findings.evidence: grep across docs/phase3/_bib/*.yaml returns 'NO Hapi, author in any bib'; no docs/phase3/_papers/ file for any Hapi paper; vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:45

findings.claim: Surface synthesis prose (and bibliography) attribute the 0-0.23 water-mass-fraction range for e to 'Bourgeois et al. 2024 (2008.09599) Magma ocean evolution', central estimate 0.05-0.10.
findings.source_says: arXiv 2008.09599 is authored by Patrick Barth, Ludmila Carone, Rory Barnes, Lena Noack, Paul Molliere, Thomas Henning (first author BARTH), year 2021 - title 'Magma Ocean Evolution of the TRAPPIST-1 Planets'. The 0-0.23 WMF value is CORRECT (abstract: 'water mass fractions of 0-0.23 ... for planets e'), but the author ('Bourgeois') and year (2024 vs 2021) are both wrong. The bib YAML itself lists this id as Barth 2021.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2008.09599.md:3 ('Patrick Barth 1,2,3 Ludmila Carone 3 Rory Barnes 4...') and docs/phase3/_bib/trappist-1-e.yaml:837-845 (arxiv_id 2008.09599, authors: - Barth, Patrick ... year: '2021'); vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:97-100 and :284

findings.claim: 251 K, 'high' confidence, Basis: 'Agol 2021'.
findings.source_says: The Agol 2021 cache explicitly states it has NO equilibrium-temperature column: 'Agol 2021 reports NO equilibrium-temperature column anywhere in the paper - only insolation S [S_earth] ... Any T_eq value attributed to Agol 2021 is a derived value, not a table entry.' The value 251 K is itself corroborated elsewhere (Boldog 2023 Table 1 gives e at 251.3 +/- 4.9 K), so the number is right but the 'Agol 2021' attribution at 'high' confidence is wrong - it should be 'derived' or attributed to Boldog 2023.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md:27-29 (explicit note: no T_eq column in Agol 2021); corroborating value docs/phase3/_papers/2312.01893.md:62 ('251.3 +/- 4.9'); vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:43

findings.claim: Substellar 290 K, nightside 230 K, global mean 270 K - all 'medium', all attributed to 'Wolf 2017 GCM Aquaplanet'.
findings.source_says: Wolf 2017 = arXiv 1703.05815 ('Assessing the Habitability of the TRAPPIST-1 System Using a 3D Climate Model'). Its cache file contains ONLY the arXiv category header ('# Astrophysics > Earth and Planetary Astrophysics') - no abstract, no body, no temperature values. The 290/230/270 K numbers cannot be checked offline. Note the global-mean 270 K is materially warmer than the offline-verifiable THAI II Hab-1 (232-246 K, 2109.11459.md:321) and Cooke WACCM6 (219-231 K), a divergence the report does acknowledge in prose.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1703.05815.md is a 1-line stub ('# Astrophysics > Earth and Planetary Astrophysics'); vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:46-48

findings.claim: Open ocean within ~35 deg of substellar point; ice caps cover ~60% of surface outside that disk - both attributed to 'Wolf 2017 Aquaplanet'.
findings.source_says: Wolf 2017 (1703.05815) full text is absent from the cache (1-line category-header stub only), so the '35 deg' ice line and '~60% ice' figures cannot be verified offline. Turbet 2018 (1707.06927, present) supports the qualitative claim 'always sustain surface liquid water at least in the substellar region' but gives no 35-deg radius.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1703.05815.md (stub); corroborating-but-non-quantitative docs/phase3/_papers/1707.06927.md:7; vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:59 and :61

findings.claim: 4.92 g/cc, Basis: 'Agol 2021'.
findings.source_says: Agol 2021 Table 6 gives rho = 4.885 (+0.168/-0.182) g/cm3 for e. 4.885 rounds to 4.89, not 4.92 (~0.7% high). Within the upper error bar, so cosmetic.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md:25 ('| rho [g cm-3] | ... 4.885 +0.168/-0.182 [e] |'); vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:40

findings.claim: 0.66 S_earth, 'high', Basis 'Agol 2021' (also used in intro and angular-size derivation).
findings.source_says: Agol 2021 Table 6 gives S = 0.646 (+/-0.025) S_earth for e. 0.646 rounds to 0.65, not 0.66 (~2% high). Minor; Turbet 2018 also quotes 0.66 S_earth so the value is in common use, but the precise Agol figure is 0.646.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2010.01074.md:22 ('| S [S_earth] | ... 0.646 +0.025/-0.025 [e] |'); vs docs/phase3/trappist-1-e.md:42

tau-cet-h.coverage_note: 42 Decisions rows total. Cacheable/verified against an offline source: 11 rows trace to a paper actually present in the cache — the orbital/physical rows (eccentricity 0.23, argument_of_periastron 7.45° = 0.13 rad, sidereal_period 49.41 d, semi_major_axis 0.243 AU, mass_mearth 1.83 Msini) all match Feng 2017 Table 5 (docs/phase3/_papers/1708.02051.md:289-297) and db/planets_curated.json; insolation_s_earth 8.26 is correctly derived from L=0.488 L☉ which is confirmed in the cached Teixeira 2009 (docs/phase3/_papers/0811.3989.md:107, '0.488 ± 0.010 L☉'). A further ~13 derived rows (radius, surface_gravity 1.29, density 5.99, equilibrium_temp 472/334, scale_height 6.9, angular_diameter 1.74) are arithmetically self-consistent and internally verifiable from the cited L/a/mass/radius. The remaining ~18 rows are explicit 'scenario choice / Venus-analog template / standard value' picks self-labeled low confidence (pressure, composition, albedo, cloud/surface tints, magnetic/aurora) — not misattributed, but unsourced by design. Two decision-driving rows cite papers that are NOT cacheable and print no arXiv id (tidally_locked → Vinson 2017 / Makarov 2018; tidal_heating → Bolmont 2020) — these are flagged UNVERIFIABLE. No tau-cet-h.yaml planet bib exists; only the host bib docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml is present, which pins arXiv ids for Feng 2017 (1708.02051, cached) and Teixeira 2009 (0811.3989, cached); the report's own Bibliography only prints arXiv ids for Feng 2017 and Feng 2018 (1801.05415, NOT cached, but Feng 2018 drives no Decisions row).

findings.source_says: DB stellar_props_curated.json gives teff_k 5370 with teff_bibcode 2023AJ....166..123K (Korolik et al. 2023, CHARA/MIRC-X interferometric Teff). Pavlenko 2012 is NOT the source of the recommended 5370 K and is not cached / prints no arXiv id in the report.

findings.source_says: The only cached paper that references Lawler 2014's disk inclination (Feng 2017) states the best-fit debris-disk inclination is 'around 30°', not 35°. Lawler 2014 and MacGregor 2016 are themselves not in the cache or bib YAML, so the 35° cannot be directly confirmed; the nearest cacheable cross-reference gives 30°.

The specific quantitative claim (3:2 capture probability >80% for e≳0.10, 'Figure 5') cannot be checked offline. This row drives the canonical spin-orbit / rotationPeriod cfg pick.

findings.source_says: not in cache — Bolmont et al. 2020 is absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ and the bib YAML, and the report prints no arXiv id for it. findings.claim: Decisions row + intro + surface + atmosphere all state 8.26 S⊕ (derived L=0.488 / 0.243²)
findings.source_says: The Kopparapu context bullet in the Bibliography states 'h at 7.74 S⊕' — contradicting the 8.26 S⊕ used in the Decisions row and four other places. 8.26 is the arithmetically correct value (0.488/0.243² = 8.264); 7.74 is internally inconsistent.
findings.evidence: report docs/phase3/tau-cet-h.md:276 'h at 7.74 S⊕ is firmly in the runaway regime' vs lines 8/45/78/155 '8.26 S⊕'.

findings.claim: true mass ≳ 2.3 M⊕ assuming sin i ≈ 0.7 (Decisions row 40)
findings.source_says: 1.83 / 0.7 = 2.61 M⊕, not 2.3 M⊕; and the report's own 'Open items' section line 313 states 'true mass ≈ 3.2 M⊕' from i≈35° (1.83/sin35° = 3.19). The two true-mass figures (2.3 vs 3.2) are mutually inconsistent and neither equals 1.83/0.7.
findings.evidence: report docs/phase3/tau-cet-h.md:40 'true mass ≳ 2.3 M⊕ assuming sin i ≈ 0.7' vs line 313 'Cfg adopts i ≈ 35° from disk → true mass ≈ 3.2 M⊕'; 1.83/0.7=2.61, 1.83/sin35°=3.19.

findings.claim: Report treats h as a confirmed body for full Venus-analog synthesis; flags it only as 'disputed (pl_controv_flag = 1)' and frames the thick-CO₂ atmosphere as 'more secure'. Intro also calls the system 'four planet candidates' (e/f/g/h).
findings.source_says: DB meta_notes record that τ Cet e was reclassified to 'False Positive Planet' by NEA (2026-04-09, Figueira et al. 2025 ESPRESSO non-detection) and that 'Figueira 2025 also reports that f/g/h are at or below ESPRESSO's detection floor; ... the system is on retraction watch.' planets_curated.json holds only f, g, h (e correctly absent). The report does not mention the Figueira 2025 ESPRESSO non-detection or the retraction-watch status of f/g/h, and the 'more secure Venus analog' framing overstates confidence in a body that the DB flags as possibly below the detection floor.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json tau Cet meta_notes: 'Figueira 2025 also reports that f/g/h are at or below ESPRESSO\'s detection floor; ... the system is on retraction watch.'; planets list = [f, g, h] only. report docs/phase3/tau-cet-h.md:12-13, 251-256 (no Figueira 2025), and the system is presented as a robust synthesis target.

hd-69830.coverage_note: 28 Decisions rows total. Cacheable + verified: the 10 stellar-layer rows (mass, radius, teff, luminosity, metallicity, age, v_sin_i, rotation_period, stellar_color_temp_k, plus the visual_surface_tint/limb_darkening/spot/corona/cycle synthesis rows whose paper anchors I confirmed) trace to cached papers: Tanner 2015 (1412.5251.md) verifies theta_LD 0.674, R 0.9058, L 0.622, Teff 5394/SME 5385, [Fe/H] -0.04, mass 0.863, age 7.5/10.6, v sin i 0.8, mu=0.352 Claret 2000, Mamajek 5.7-6.1 and Song 0.6-2 Gyr; Simpson 2010 (1006.4121.md) verifies P_rot 35.1+-0.8 d, grade Probable, 1 of 8 seasons, P_calc~35; Santos 2004 (astro-ph_0311541.md) [Fe/H] -0.03 and Sousa 2008 (0805.4826.md) [Fe/H] -0.06 corroborate. NOT cacheable: the activity row (Gomes da Silva 2021 IS cached but its full text lacks the per-star value — VizieR-only, UNVERIFIABLE); the ENTIRE 9-row disk block (Beichman 2005, Lisse 2007, Wyatt 2007, Smith/Wyatt/Haniff 2009 are all absent from both the bib YAML and the _papers cache = UNSOURCED, though db/disks_curated.json holds an audited Phase 2 disk block that the report partially desyncs from); and the X-ray + spectral_type rows (Merloni 2024, Schmitt 2004, Gray 2006 uncacheable). Roughly half the table (stellar layer) has a verifiable cached source; the disk layer and activity index do not. Most severe: disk_mass_mearth value 4e-4 contradicts its own basis (6e-5) and the DB (6e-5/1e-3); the 0.92-1.16 AU geometry is mis-attributed to Smith/Wyatt/Haniff 2009 exactly as the DB audit flagged and corrected.
