proxima-cen.coverage_note: Host-star report; 21 Decisions rows. Cacheable & verified against cached papers/DB: activity_log_rhk (-5.65+/-0.17, exact match SM2016 docs/phase3/_papers/1506.08039.md:184), flare_rate_per_day_total (1.49, exact Vida 2019 1907.12580.md:7/82/116), flare_rate_superflare (3/yr >=1e33, 0.5/yr >=1e34, exact Vida 1907.12580.md:122), mass_msun (0.1221, confirmed via Feng&Jones 1709.03560.md:11 quoting Mann 2015), teff_k (2904+/-51, confirmed via db recommended Passegger 2019), orbital_role (P~547 kyr / a=8.7 kau, confirmed via Feng&Jones 1709.03560.md:11 quoting Kervella 2017), spectral_type/luminosity-value/visual hex/derived rows (uncontroversial/DB or derived). Sources NOT in cache (uncacheable -> coverage gap): Demory 2009 (arXiv:0906.0602, radius), Benedict 1998 (astro-ph/9806276, rotation; bib status=skipped, not fetched), Kervella 2017 (arXiv:1611.03495, NOT cached - cache 1611.04857 is an unrelated condensed-matter paper), Hawley 1996 (spectral type, not in bib/cache), Claret 2018 (limb-darkening tie-break, appears only as a CARMENES co-author, no standalone paper). Two cited papers ARE cached but are the WRONG paper for the claim: 1711.06576 (Reiners 2018) is the 324-star spectral-atlas/RV paper with no Zeeman magnetography, so both magnetic-field rows are mis-attributed. Decision-relevant but value-correct-source-mismatched: metallicity (DB Passegger value is +0.05 not +0.21), age attribution (Kervella 2017 not Feng&Jones). Of 21 rows: ~13 verified, 4 wrong/mismatched values or attributions (2 high: magnetic; 2 medium: cycle 7 vs 8.0 yr, flare-peak 28.5 vs log 27.8), 4 unverifiable (radius/rotation/quiescent-Lx/limb-darkening - sources uncacheable but values DB- or literature-consistent), 1 internal-derivation contradiction (luminosity). Also noted outside the Decisions table: intro/open-items state Proxima c at '5.2 AU' but SM2025 (2507.21751.md:9) gives Proxima c at P=1900 d (~1.5 AU); not a Decisions row so not flagged as a finding.

findings.claim: magnetic_dipole_strength_kG = 0.6, high confidence, Basis: 'Reiners 2018 CARMENES Zeeman (dipole component)' (arXiv:1711.06576, 2018A&A...612A..49R).
findings.source_says: The cached paper 1711.06576 (Reiners et al. 2018, 'The CARMENES search ... High-resolution optical and near-infrared spectroscopy of 324 survey stars') is a spectral-atlas / RV-survey paper. It presents one spectrum per M dwarf and measures RV, Halpha and vsini; it contains NO Zeeman magnetic-field analysis (zero 'kG' occurrences, no mean-field value). The ~600 G field is attributed by Fuhrmeister 2022 to Reiners & Basri 2008 and Klein et al. 2021, not to this paper.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1711.06576.md:7 abstract '...we present the survey sample by publishing one CARMENES spectrum for each M dwarf ... we measure its RV, Halpha emission, and projected rotation velocity'; grep 'kG' count = 0 in 1711.06576.md. Cross-check docs/phase3/_papers/2204.09270.md:11 'Proxima Centauri exhibits only a moderate magnetic field of B_f ~ 600 G (Reiners & Basri 2008; Klein et al. 2021)'. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:67.

findings.claim: magnetic_total_field_kG_rms = 4, high confidence, Basis: 'Reiners 2018 (Zeeman RMS)' (arXiv:1711.06576). Prose (atmosphere synthesis) also says 'Reiners et al. 2018 (CARMENES) measured a Zeeman-broadened mean magnetic field of ~4 kG (RMS over the disk) with a roughly 0.6-kG dipole component'.
findings.source_says: 1711.06576 (Reiners et al. 2018, 324-star spectral atlas / RV precision paper) contains no Zeeman mean-field or RMS magnetic-field measurement for Proxima (zero 'kG' mentions; only 3 'magnetic' mentions, all instrument/context). The Proxima mean magnetic field measurement originates from Reiners & Basri 2008 / Klein et al. 2021, not this CARMENES catalog paper.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/1711.06576.md grep 'kG' = 0; line 7 abstract describes RV/Halpha/vsini survey, not magnetography. Fuhrmeister attribution at docs/phase3/_papers/2204.09270.md:11. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:68 and prose docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:102-104.

findings.claim: activity_cycle_years = 7, Basis: 'Wargelin 2024 X-ray + UV + optical cycle' (arXiv:2411.04252). Prose: '...refined by Wargelin 2024 to ~7 years'.
findings.source_says: Wargelin et al. 2024 (the cited paper) actually REFINES the cycle to 8.0 yr. Its abstract states the combined data 'now reveals clear cyclic behavior in all three wavebands with a period of 8.0 yr.' The ~7 yr figure is the older Suarez Mascareno 2016 (6.8 yr) / Wargelin 2017 (7.05 yr) result, which the 2024 paper supersedes.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2411.04252.md:7 '...now reveals clear cyclic behavior in all three wavebands with a period of 8.0 yr.' (older values quoted at line 15: SM2016 6.8+/-0.3 yr, Wargelin 2017 7.05+/-0.15 yr). Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:64; prose docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:121-122.

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_flare_peak = 28.5, Basis: 'Fuhrmeister 2022 simultaneous X-ray + FUV peak' (arXiv:2204.09270).
findings.source_says: Fuhrmeister et al. 2022 report an average flare X-ray luminosity L_X,fl = 6.7e27 erg/s (= log 27.8), with the count rate rising by an average factor of ~10 (range 5-13.5) during flare maxima above a quiescent L_X,qu = 6.6e26 erg/s. No flare X-ray luminosity as high as 10^28.5 (~3.2e28) is reported; 28.5 is ~0.7 dex (factor ~5) above the paper's flare luminosity.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2204.09270.md:127 'During flares, the photon count rate increases by factors of 5 to 13.5 ... The average flare luminosity is L_X,fl = 6.7e27 erg s-1.' and 2204.09270.md:121 quiescent L_X,qu = 6.6e26 erg/s. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:66.

findings.claim: metallicity_fe_h_dex = +0.21, medium, Basis: 'Passegger 2019 H-band'.
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json carries the Passegger et al. 2019 (2019A&A...627A.161P) metallicity as the recommended value: value_dex = 0.05, uncertainty_dex = 0.16 (method high_res_spectroscopy). The report attributes +0.21 to the same Passegger 2019 paper, contradicting the DB's curated Passegger value by 0.16 dex (sign/magnitude materially different).
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json Proxima Cen metallicity_measurements: {value_dex: 0.05, uncertainty_dex: 0.16, reference: 'Passegger et al. 2019', bibcode 2019A&A...627A.161P}. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:60. Cached Passegger paper docs/phase3/_papers/1907.00807.md has no Proxima per-star metallicity in extracted text to confirm +0.21.

findings.claim: luminosity_lsun = 0.001567, high, Basis: 'derived from R, Teff'. Surface-synthesis prose: '0.00157 L_sun - about 1/640 of the Sun'.
findings.source_says: Stefan-Boltzmann derivation from the report's own R = 0.141 R_sun and Teff = 2904 K gives L = (0.141)^2 (2904/5772)^4 = 0.00127 L_sun, ~19% lower than 0.001567. The stated value 0.001567 instead matches the measured bolometric luminosity (db recommended Boyajian 2012 = 0.00155 L_sun), so the 'derived from R, Teff' basis does not reproduce the quoted value.
findings.evidence: Computed L from report's R/Teff = 0.001274. db/stellar_props_curated.json luminosity recommended = 0.00155 (Boyajian et al. 2012). Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:59; prose docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:80-81.

findings.claim: rotation_period_days = 83.5 +/- 0.5, high, Basis: 'Benedict et al. 1998 (HST photometric)'.
findings.source_says: Benedict et al. 1998 (the primary cited source) is in the bib YAML as arXiv:astro-ph/9806276 with status: skipped (below_score_threshold) and is NOT present in the _papers cache, so the 83.5 +/- 0.5 d value cannot be checked offline against its source. The value is independently corroborated by cached SM2020 (P_rot ~83.2 d) and SM2025 (GP 83.2 +/- 1.6 d), which lowers practical risk.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_bib/proxima-cen.yaml id095 arXiv:astro-ph/9806276, year 1998, 'status: skipped, skip_reason: below_score_threshold'; no astro-ph_9806276.md in docs/phase3/_papers/. Corroboration docs/phase3/_papers/2005.12114.md:46 'P_rot ~83.2' and docs/phase3/_papers/2507.21751.md:110 '83.2 +/- 1.6'. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:62.

findings.claim: radius_rsun = 0.141 +/- 0.011, high, Basis: 'Demory et al. 2009 (VLTI/VINCI interferometry)' (bib lists arXiv:0906.0602).
findings.source_says: Demory et al. 2009 (arXiv:0906.0602 / 2009A&A...505..205D) is NOT in the _papers cache, so the value cannot be confirmed against the paper offline. The value IS consistent with db/stellar_props_curated.json recommended radius (0.141 +/- 0.011, interferometry, Demory et al. 2009), so DB-consistent though paper-uncacheable.
findings.evidence: No 0906.0602.* file in docs/phase3/_papers/. db/stellar_props_curated.json Proxima Cen radius_measurements recommended: {value_rsun: 0.141, uncertainty_rsun: 0.011, reference: 'Demory et al. 2009', bibcode 2009A&A...505..205D}. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:57.

findings.claim: x_ray_log_lx_cgs_quiescent = 27.0, medium, Basis: 'Damonte 2026 XMM time-resolved spectra' (arXiv:2512.18011).
findings.source_says: The cached Damonte 2026 paper reports time-resolved X-ray FLUXES (erg/s/cm^2) and flux thresholds, not an explicit quiescent X-ray LUMINOSITY of log L_X = 27.0; no such value was located in the cache. The quiescent log L_X ~ 27.0 is independently supported by the literature range L_X = (0.4-1.6)e27 erg/s (Haisch 1990, quoted in Fuhrmeister 2022), so the number is physically plausible but its specific attribution to Damonte 2026 is unconfirmed offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/_papers/2512.18011.md uses flux units (e.g. line 150 '1.5e-11 erg s-1 cm-2'); no log L_X=27.0 quiescent luminosity found. Literature range at docs/phase3/_papers/2204.09270.md:11 'quiescent X-ray luminosity L_X ~ (0.4-1.6)e27 erg s-1 (Haisch et al. 1990)'. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:65.

findings.claim: age_gyr = 4.85, medium, Basis: 'dynamics + activity proxy; Feng & Jones 2018 capture vs. coeval ambiguity'.
findings.source_says: The 4.85 Gyr figure is, per db/stellar_props_curated.json, the recommended age from Kervella et al. 2017 (kinematic), not from Feng & Jones 2018. The cached Feng & Jones 2018 does not state 4.85 Gyr as its own value; it cites alpha Cen A/B age 4.7-5.2 Gyr (Bazot 2016) and argues Proxima is 'probably older than 5 Gyr (Eggenberger 2004)'. The report's parenthetical '(DB Phase 2 attribution)' in prose partly acknowledges this.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json age_measurements recommended: {value_gyr: 4.85, uncertainty_gyr: 0.5, method: kinematic, reference: 'Kervella et al. 2017', bibcode 2017A&A...598L...7K}. docs/phase3/_papers/1709.03560.md:47 'age for alpha Centauri A and B (e.g., 4.7-5.2 Gyr, Bazot et al. 2016)' and 'age of Proxima is probably older than 5 Gyr (Eggenberger et al., 2004)'. Report cell: docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:61.

tau-cet-g.coverage_note: Decisions table = 41 rows. Of these, ~20 are fully verified: the 5 Feng-2017-anchored orbital/physical rows (eccentricity 0.06, arg_periastron 35.341 = 6.90 rad normalized, sidereal_period 20.00, semi_major_axis 0.133, mass_mearth 1.75 Msini) all confirmed against the cached Feng 2017 Table 5 (_papers/1708.02051.md:289-297); ~6 derived rows (surface_gravity 1.26, insolation 27.59, Teq A=0 638, Teq A=0.3 584, angular_diameter 3.17) reproduce exactly from L=0.488 (Teixeira 2009 cached, _papers/0811.3989.md:107) and a=0.133; density 5.84 is ROUNDING-fine (recompute 5.87); and ~9 consequence rows (atmosphere_scale_height, atmosphere_tint, cloud_cover, cloud_tint, ocean_present, ocean_extent, ocean_tint, surface_ice_caps, aurora_present) follow deterministically from atmosphere_present=false. CACHEABLE SOURCES: only 2 of the report's cited papers are actually offline-verifiable — Feng 2017 (1708.02051, present and used) and Teixeira 2009 (0811.3989, host bib). The two papers the report claims 'drove decisions' but that are NOT cacheable: Feng 2018 (1801.05415, absent from cache AND bib YAML), Vinson & Hansen 2017, Makarov 2018, Zahnle 2017, Lawler 2014, Kopparapu 2014, Bolmont 2020, Owen & Wu 2017, Pavlenko 2012, Schmitt 1985 — NONE of these print an arXiv ID in the report's Bibliography and NONE appear in docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml, so the tie-break rows that depend on them (inclination_deg, tidally_locked spin pick, bond_albedo, magnetic_field, tidal/induction/radiogenic heating) are not offline-verifiable. NOTE: the planet-level bib docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet-g.yaml referenced in the task does NOT exist; only the host bib tau-cet.yaml is present. DB cross-check: g's orbital+physical values match db/planets_curated.json exactly; the system is correctly treated as f/g/h (τ Cet e correctly absent, matching its NEA False-Positive reclassification).

findings.claim: Decisions row, intro, and Surface synthesis all state 27.59 S⊕ (derived 0.488 L☉ / 0.133²).
findings.source_says: The same report contradicts itself: the Kopparapu context entry in the Bibliography section says 'g at 25.85 S⊕ is far inside.' 27.59 is the arithmetically correct value (0.488/0.133² = 27.588); 25.85 is an unexplained stray value.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:282 '...for a G8V is ~1.0 S⊕; g at 25.85 S⊕' vs lines 8, 45, 77, 155 which all use 27.59.

findings.claim: Decisions row gives 5370 K 'host Teff (Pavlenko 2012)'.
findings.source_says: The value 5370 K is correct, but db/stellar_props_curated.json and docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml attribute the recommended interferometric Teff = 5370 K to Korolik et al. 2023 (2023AJ....166..123K), not Pavlenko 2012. Pavlenko 2012 has no arXiv ID printed in the report and is absent from the bib YAML; the cache file at 1112.0447.md is actually Baines & Armstrong on ε Eridani, not Pavlenko/τ Cet. The attribution cannot be supported offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:73 'host Teff (Pavlenko 2012)'; docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml lists teff rec = Korolik 2023; db/stellar_props_curated.json tau Cet teff rec = (5370, 'Korolik et al. 2023'); _papers/1112.0447.md header = 'CONFIRMING FUNDAMENTAL PROPERTIES OF THE EXOPLANET HOST STAR ε ERIDANI'.

findings.claim: inclination_deg = 35, 'Tie-break: τ Ceti debris disk inclination (Lawler et al. 2014, adopted by MacGregor et al. 2016; assumed coplanar)'. This drives the adopted true mass ≈3.0 M⊕ in Open items.
findings.source_says: Not in cache. Lawler et al. 2014 has no arXiv ID printed in the report Bibliography and is not in the host bib YAML (docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml). The cache file at the only plausible ID (1405.7707.md) is Cohen et al. on M-dwarf magnetospheres, not Lawler. Feng 2017 (cached) mentions a disk inclination of 'around 30°' / 'around 30 deg' (lines 285, 345), not 35° — so the specific 35° figure cannot be confirmed offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:39; _papers/1708.02051.md:285 'the best-fitting inclination for the debris disk of τ Ceti (around 30∘)'; _papers/1405.7707.md header = 'Magnetospheric Structure and Atmospheric Joule Heating of Habitable Planets Orbiting M-dwarf Stars' (Cohen et al.).

findings.claim: 'maybe (3:2 or 1:1; cfg picks 3:2 Mercury-analog)' on basis 'Vinson 2017 / Makarov 2018: ... 3:2-capture-permitted ... 3:2 capture probability ~50–70%'. Drives the spin-state cfg pick and rotationPeriod (13.33 d).
findings.source_says: Not in cache. Neither Vinson & Hansen 2017 nor Makarov 2018 has an arXiv ID printed in the report Bibliography, and neither is in the host bib YAML. The specific 'Makarov 2018 Figure 7 → 50–70% capture' claim cannot be checked against any cached paper.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:33 and :180; report Bibliography lines 268-273 list Vinson and Makarov with NO arXiv id; only Feng 2017/2018 carry arXiv ids (grep 'arXiv:' returns only lines 259, 265).

findings.claim: Decisions cell inlines both the cfg pick and the canonical alternative in one cell: 'maybe (3:2 or 1:1; cfg picks 3:2 Mercury-analog)'.
findings.source_says: Phase 3 policy: canonical/variant values should not be inlined in a single Decisions cell; the divergence belongs in the '## Canonical alternatives' section (which does exist and correctly documents the 3:2 vs 1:1 split). The inlined alternative in the Decisions cell duplicates and pre-empts that section.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:33 (Decisions cell) vs lines 307-314 ('## Canonical alternatives' table already holds the 3:2 vs 1:1 divergence).

findings.claim: Decisions row: '1.75 (M sin i; true mass ≳ 2.2 M⊕ assuming sin i ≈ 0.7)'.
findings.source_says: Two problems. (1) Arithmetic: 1.75 / 0.7 = 2.50 M⊕, not 2.2. (2) Inconsistent with the report's own adopted inclination: inclination_deg row and Open items adopt i ≈ 35° (sin i = 0.57) giving true mass ≈ 3.0 M⊕ (line 321), contradicting the mass row's sin i ≈ 0.7 / 2.2 M⊕. The cfg value 1.75 (Msini) itself is correct per Feng 2017 Table 5.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:40 vs :321 ('Cfg adopts i ≈ 35° from disk → true mass ≈ 3.0 M⊕'); _papers/1708.02051.md:296 'm sin i ... 1.75 [1.35, 2.00]'.

findings.claim: Open items: 'Controversial flag (pl_controv_flag = 1). As for f, monitor for retraction by Lubin 2024 or future ESPRESSO RV reanalysis.' Bibliography cites Feng 2018 (arXiv:1801.05415) as confirming 20-d signal stability.
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json tau Cet meta_notes record the curated retraction-watch source as Figueira et al. 2025 (A&A 700, A174) ESPRESSO non-detection — which reclassified τ Cet e to 'False Positive Planet' and reports f/g/h at/below ESPRESSO's floor — not 'Lubin 2024'. The report's named retraction reference does not match the DB-recorded one. Separately, Feng 2018 (1801.05415) is absent from both the _papers cache and the host bib YAML, so its 'confirms 20-day signal stability' claim is unverifiable offline.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:318-319 ('Lubin 2024') and :263-266 (Feng 2018 arXiv:1801.05415); db/stellar_props_curated.json tau Cet meta_notes ('Figueira et al. 2025 ESPRESSO ... f/g/h are at or below ESPRESSO's detection floor; ... on retraction watch'); no file _papers/1801.05415.md exists.

findings.claim: Report adopts a 3:2 spin-orbit resonance (Mercury analog) at e = 0.06 and notes the 1:1 lock as a preserved variant.
findings.source_says: This is the correct call, not an error — noted only to document that the audit checked it. A 3:2 resonance is physically appropriate (indeed required-by-nonzero-e, Mercury template) for e = 0.06; the report does NOT force a 1:1 lock on the eccentric orbit, and does NOT assert a molten/lava surface (it explicitly rules out a silicate-vapor cap at 700 K). No physical-implausibility violation found.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-g.md:33, :133-136 ('At 700 K dayside ... silicate vapor pressure is well below 10⁻⁶ bar ... no substellar silicate-vapor cloud is rendered'). Reported for transparency; severity low and effectively a non-finding.

hd-69830-d.coverage_note: 42 Decisions rows. Cacheable / offline-checkable sources are scarce: only the host stellar parameters are in the _papers cache (Tanner et al. 2015 = 1412.5251.md, plus Sousa/Mamajek/Simpson/Santos/Gomes da Silva), and only one report-cited paper is cached, Atri 2019 (1910.09871.md), which the report correctly characterizes as non-decisive (the 12 mSv/yr is self-labeled as not derived from it). The planet bib docs/phase3/_bib/hd-69830-d.yaml does NOT exist; only the host bib hd-69830.yaml is present. Every planet-decision-driving paper is absent from the cache: Lovis 2006 (in host bib but arxiv_id:null/skipped -> UNVERIFIABLE; values match db/planets_curated.json), and Beichman 2005, Lisse 2007, Wyatt 2007, Chen&Kipping 2017, Otegi 2020, Karkoschka 2011, Sromovsky 2001/1993, Atreya&Wong 2005, Encrenaz 2003, Heller 2011, Pearl 1990, Lamy 2017, Hammel 1989 (none in any bib YAML nor cache -> UNSOURCED for offline purposes). Rows I could positively verify: the orbital/mass set (a, e, omega, P, mass) against db/planets_curated.json; the derived arithmetic (insolation, Teq A=0/A=0.3, surface gravity 0.91, scale height ~112 km, peri/apo insolation) which is internally self-consistent; and the stellar Teff against cached Tanner -> ~9 verified. Principal problems: (1) the angular-diameter row 0.673 deg does not match its own stated formula+inputs (should be ~0.75 deg, ~0.766 with DB radius); (2) three PHASE2_DESYNC issues where the report uses superseded/non-recommended stellar values vs db/stellar_props_curated.json (R*=0.887 instead of Tanner 0.9058 'explicitly superseded'; L=0.60 instead of 0.622; spectral type G8V instead of curated K0 V; age 5.7 Gyr instead of recommended 7.5 Gyr); and (3) the large block of uncacheable Neptune-analog citations. No PHYSICAL_IMPLAUSIBLE or POLICY_VIOLATION found (tidal-lock=false is correct for a free rotator; the dynamo/aurora is physically expected for a Neptune-class envelope, not a reflexive default; no lava claim; equilibrium temp is consistent with stated L and a; no divergence is asserted without a section).

findings.claim: 0.673 deg, 'derived: 2 R*/a x (180/pi) with R* = 0.887 Rsun, a = 0.63 AU'
findings.source_says: The stated formula with the stated inputs does not yield 0.673. 2*0.887*Rsun_au/0.63*(180/pi) = 0.750 deg (and 0.766 deg with the DB radius 0.9058). 0.673 deg would require a ~ 0.70 AU. The value is an arithmetic/derivation error.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-69830-d.md:81; recomputation: 2*0.887*0.00465047/0.63*180/pi = 0.7503 deg. Propagated to prose 'fills a 0.67 deg disk' (md:188-189, 269).

findings.claim: Derivation uses R* = 0.887 Rsun for HD 69830.
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json recommends R* = 0.9058 +/- 0.019 Rsun (Tanner et al. 2015, CHARA interferometry, bibcode 2015ApJ...800..115T). The DB note explicitly states: 'Prior unverified values (Lovis 2006 M 0.86, Rosenthal 2021 R 0.887) superseded by Tanner.' The report uses the superseded Rosenthal radius.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json radius_measurements value_rsun 0.9058 recommended:true, meta_notes '...Rosenthal 2021 R 0.887... superseded by Tanner'; cached Tanner 1412.5251.md abstract 'R* = 0.9058 +/- 0.0190 Rsun'; report docs/phase3/hd-69830-d.md:81.

findings.claim: L = 0.60 Lsun, giving insolation 1.51 S_earth and Teq(A=0)=308 K / Teq(A=0.3)=282 K.
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json recommends L = 0.622 +/- 0.014 Lsun (Tanner 2015, bolometric_flux, recommended:true). With L=0.622: S = 1.567, Teq(A=0)=311 K, Teq(A=0.3)=284.5 K. The report's internal arithmetic is self-consistent at L=0.60 but uses a value below the recommended DB luminosity.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json luminosity_measurements value_lsun 0.622 recommended:true; cached Tanner 1412.5251.md abstract 'L* = 0.622 +/- 0.014 Lsun'; report md:8, 51, 52-53.

findings.claim: HD 69830 described as 'G8V' / 'old G8V' in intro and all derivations (e.g. tint reasoning '5385 K vs 5772 K', md:24,61,90,178,189).

Note: Tanner 2015 Table 2 lists 'G8V' from Gray and adopted the K0V SED template; the cached paper supports both labels, but the curated DB authoritative spectral type is K0 V.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json spectype 'K0 V'; cached Tanner 1412.5251.md '...preference of recent, authoritative surveys for the former spectral type... we opted to use the values derived from the K0V fit'; report md:24 etc.

findings.claim: '5.7 Gyr system age' and '5.7 +/- 1 Gyr' used as the adopted age (md:41, 200).

The report adopts the non-recommended Mamajek value. (Conclusion 'free rotator' is robust under either age, so cfg-impact is nil.)
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json age_measurements: 7.5 recommended:true, Mamajek 5.9 recommended:false; report md:41, md:200.

findings.claim: 5385 K, 'HD 69830 Teff' (high confidence).
findings.source_says: Cached Tanner 2015 gives Teff = 5394 +/- 62 K (CHARA+SED, the DB-recommended teff_k=5394, bibcode 2015ApJ...800..115T) and 5385 +/- 44 K (SME alternate). The report uses the SME alternate, not the DB-recommended 5394. Difference is 9 K (cosmetic for color-temp).
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json teff_k 5394 teff_bibcode 2015ApJ...800..115T; cached Tanner 1412.5251.md Table 2 'Effective Temperature (K) | 5394 +/- 62 | CHARA+SED fit | 5385 +/- 44 | SME'; report md:82.

findings.claim: e=0.07, omega=224 deg, P=197+/-3 d, a=0.63 AU, Msini=18.1 Mearth, all cited 'Lovis 2006'.

They DO match db/planets_curated.json exactly (a 0.63, e 0.07, omega 224, mass 18.1 Msini, same bibcode), so they are PHASE2-confirmed even though the paper text is unavailable.

findings.claim: 4.45 Rearth, tie-break citing 'Chen & Kipping 2017 (arXiv:1603.08614), Otegi 2020 (arXiv:1911.04745)'.
findings.source_says: Neither arXiv:1603.08614 nor arXiv:1911.04745 is present in the _papers cache, and no planet bib YAML exists (docs/phase3/_bib/hd-69830-d.yaml is absent; the host bib does not list these papers). The mass-radius forecast (18 Mearth -> 4.4-4.5 Rearth) therefore could never have been verified offline. This is a decision-driving (medium-confidence) row.
findings.evidence: ls docs/phase3/_papers/ : 1603.08614.md and 1911.04745.md MISSING; docs/phase3/_bib/hd-69830-d.yaml ABSENT; host bib hd-69830.yaml does not list Chen/Otegi; report md:48, 321-327.

findings.field: atmosphere_composition / cloud_cover_fraction / cloud_morphology (Atreya&Wong 2005, Karkoschka 2011, Sromovsky 2001, Encrenaz 2003, Hammel 1989)

findings.source_says: None of Atreya & Wong 2005, Karkoschka & Tomasko 2011, Sromovsky 2001, Encrenaz 2003, Hammel 1989 are in the _papers cache (all are pre-arXiv Icarus/Science/SSR classics with no arXiv id in the report's bibliography), and there is no planet bib YAML. These Neptune-analog claims cannot be verified offline. They are self-labeled tie-break/analog rows (low-medium confidence), not novel measurements.
findings.evidence: report bibliography md:328-338, 381-384 lists these with no arXiv ids; _papers cache contains none of them; docs/phase3/_bib/hd-69830-d.yaml ABSENT.

findings.claim: Blue-cyan aurora and H2 Lyman/Werner + H3+ emission cited as 'Lamy 2017 Neptune analog' (md:77) and 'Lamy 2017' (md:78).
findings.source_says: The report's own bibliography (md:361-363) titles Lamy et al. 2017 as 'Multispectral simultaneous diagnosis of Saturn's aurorae' (JGR Planets 122, 1101) - a SATURN aurora paper, not a Neptune study. The paper is not in the _papers cache and cannot be opened offline. The decision text labels it a 'Neptune analog,' so it is an analog use of a Saturn paper rather than a misquote of its number; flagged because the source is non-Neptune-specific and uncacheable. Also note 'Sromovsky 2003' cited at md:78 is not in the bibliography (only Sromovsky 1993/2001 are listed).
findings.evidence: report md:77-78 ('Lamy 2017 Neptune analog'; 'Sromovsky 2003, Lamy 2017'); bibliography md:361-363 'Lamy L. et al. 2017 - Multispectral simultaneous diagnosis of Saturn's aurorae'; 1910.09871 is the only cached aurora-related paper and it is Atri (radiation dose), unrelated.

hd-69830-c.coverage_note: 42 Decisions rows. Cacheable sources are extremely sparse: of all papers cited in the Decisions/Bibliography, only THREE are in docs/phase3/_papers — Tanner 2015 (1412.5251, host stellar params), Simpson 2010 (1006.4121, host rotation, not cited by any c row), and Atri 2019 (1910.09871, context, explicitly non-decisive for the radiation row). The anchor for all 5 orbital/mass rows (Lovis 2006) is uncacheable (bib: arxiv_id null, status skipped) but is independently confirmed by db/planets_curated.json, so those values are PHASE2-verified. Every decision-DRIVING astrophysics source is absent from the cache: Chen&Kipping 2017, Rogers 2021, Wolfgang 2016, Otegi 2020 (radius); Moses 2013, Sudarsky 2003, Karkoschka 2009, Morley 2015 (atmosphere/tint/albedo); Hut 1981, Greenberg 2009, Heller 2011, Showman&Polvani 2011 (rotation); Lamy 2017, Sromovsky 2003, Bolmont 2013, Beichman 2005, Bonfils 2007 — none in cache. Merloni 2024 (XUV) is in neither cache nor bib (UNSOURCED). rows_verified = 11 counts rows confirmable offline: the 5 Lovis orbital/mass rows (verified vs planets_curated.json), surface_gravity (0.99, math-checked), insolation 17.3 / Teq A=0 567 / Teq A=0.3 519 (self-consistent with stated L=0.60), atmospheric_shielding (internally consistent derivation), and stellar_illumination_color_temp_k 5385 (matches Tanner 2015 SME Teff in 1412.5251.md). The dominant problems are PHASE2 desync on the host (G8V vs curated K0 V; superseded R=0.887 vs Tanner 0.9058; age 5.7 vs recommended 7.5 Gyr; L=0.60 vs 0.622), one self-inconsistent derived value (angular diameter 2.27 deg contradicts its own formula -> ~2.54-2.60 deg), and broad UNVERIFIABILITY of all tie-break decision drivers.

findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json: "spectype": "K0 V"; 1412.5251.md intro: 'HD 69830, a nearby (12.5 pc, van Leeuwen 2007) K0V star'; report docs/phase3/hd-69830-c.md:30,70,99,101 say 'G8V'.

findings.claim: Row value 2.27, basis 'derived: 2 R★ / a × (180/π) with R★ = 0.887 R☉'.
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json adopts R = 0.9058 R☉ (Tanner 2015 CHARA) as recommended and explicitly states 'Rosenthal 2021 R 0.887 superseded by Tanner'. The report uses the superseded 0.887 value.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json radius_measurements value_rsun 0.9058 + meta_notes 'Rosenthal 2021 R 0.887 superseded by Tanner'; 1412.5251.md abstract 'R∗ = 0.9058 ± 0.0190 R⊙'; report docs/phase3/hd-69830-c.md:77.

findings.claim: Value 2.27 deg, basis 'derived: 2 R★ / a × (180/π) with R★ = 0.887 R☉, a = 0.186 AU'. Prose lines 162/238/239 reuse 2.27 deg ('about 4× the apparent Sun-from-Earth diameter').
findings.source_says: The cited formula does not yield 2.27. 2 x 0.887 Rsun / 0.186 AU x 180/pi = 2.54 deg; with the correct R=0.9058 it is 2.60 deg. The stated 2.27 deg is inconsistent with the report's own formula and inputs regardless of which radius is used. (4x Sun's 0.53 deg would be ~2.1 deg, not 2.27, and the true value is ~4.8x.)
findings.evidence: Recomputed: 2*0.887*0.00465047/0.186*180/pi = 2.541 deg; with 0.9058 -> 2.595 deg. report docs/phase3/hd-69830-c.md:77 (value 2.27) and :238 ('fills 2.27 deg').

vega.coverage_note: 35 Decisions rows. Cacheable/verifiable sources: the primary anchor Monnier et al. 2012 (arXiv:1211.6055) is in the cache, but only the HTML preserves Table 2 — the .md stripped the table, so the headline numbers (R_eq 2.726+/-0.006, R_pol 2.418+/-0.012, T_pol 10070+/-90, T_eq 8910+/-130, surface-mean Teff 9360+/-90, L_bol 47.2+/-2.0, L_app 58.4+/-2.2, omega/omega_crit 0.774+/-0.012, beta 0.231+/-0.028, inclination 6.2+/-0.4) were verified from 1211.6055.html Table 2 Model 3 (Concordance). Body text confirms mass 2.15(+0.10/-0.15), age 700(+150/-75) Myr, v_eq=195 km/s, i~6.5 (back-of-envelope), [Fe/H]=-0.5, Z=0.006 (Yoon recommends Z=0.009). Aufdenberg 2006 (astro-ph_0603327.md) full text verified the Canonical-alternatives values (T_pol 10150, T_eq 7900, dT 2250, omega 0.91, i 5deg, L 37); note Aufdenberg's equatorial velocity is 275 km/s not the report's 274 (cosmetic, prose-only, not a Decisions row). 22 stellar/visual rows verified against cache or DB with no problem. Petit 2010 (rotation_period 0.71+/-0.03 d) and metallicity (Yoon -0.5) source papers are absent from cache but are corroborated verbatim inside the Monnier cache, so treated as verified, not flagged. NOT cacheable: Su et al. 2013 (arXiv:1211.7298) and Sibthorpe 2010 (no arXiv) have NO _papers files — the 7 disk-quantity rows they drive are flagged UNVERIFIABLE (all are DB-recommended in disks_curated.json, so DB-backed, not fabricated). Peterson 2006 cache (astro-ph_0603520.md) is a 222-byte title-only stub; it drives no Decisions row. No Phase2 desync vs DB found (planets_curated has no Vega planet, matching 'no confirmed planets'; stellar/disk DB values match the table). The one substantive integrity problem is the internal age contradiction: the Decisions row adopts Monnier 0.7 Gyr (matching the DB recommendation) while Open items says the cfg 'adopts the Yoon headline' 0.455 Gyr and another row uses 'system age 0.45 Gyr', plus Yoon's method is described as both rotating and non-rotating in different sections.

findings.claim: Decisions row adopts age = 0.7 (+0.15/-0.075) Gyr from Monnier 2012 rotation-aware Geneva tracks as the cfg value, marked 'DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE vs Yoon 2010 non-rotating isochrone 0.455 Gyr'. Canonical alternatives (line 95-96) repeats: cfg adopts Monnier 0.7 Gyr, Yoon's 0.455 is non-rotating.
findings.source_says: DB stellar_props_curated.json recommends Monnier 700 Myr (so the Decisions value itself is correct), but the report contradicts its own Decisions row twice: Open items line 148 says 'the cfg adopts the Yoon headline' (0.455 Gyr) AND describes Yoon as using 'rotating-model isochrones' (the Decisions row + Canonical alternatives call it non-rotating); disk_planetesimal_belt_inferred row (line 48) and the Su prose use 'system age 0.45 Gyr'.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/vega.md:20 (Decisions: Monnier 0.7 Gyr, divergence vs Yoon 0.455) vs :148 ('the cfg adopts the Yoon headline'; calls Yoon 'rotating-model isochrones') vs :48 ('system age 0.45 Gyr'). Monnier cache 1211.6055.md:57 confirms 700 (+150/-75) Myr and that Yoon's age is lower; DB note: 'age 700 Myr ... DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE, recommend Monnier'.

proxima-cen-b.coverage_note: Of 48 Decisions rows, 46 cite a source that is cacheable/derivable and were checked: the orbital+mass block (SM25, arXiv:2507.21751) and the Boutle 2017 climate block (arXiv:1702.08463) are fully in cache and verified; Garraffo (2211.15697), Zuluaga (1609.00707), Walterova (2007.12459), Atri (1910.09871), Cohen (2211.11887), Braam 2024 (2410.19108), Sergeev (2004.03007) are all cached and checked. TWO rows rest on sources that are NOT offline-verifiable: water_mass_fraction cites Herath 2021 (bibcode 2021MNRAS.500..333H, no arXiv, not in _papers cache — Tier A manual followup) and atmosphere_present partly leans on Meadows 2018 (1608.08620) + Lee 2021 (2109.06963), both of which are 50-byte title-only stubs in the cache (full text absent); however atmosphere_present is co-cited to Boutle+Zuluaga (cached), so it remains partially anchored. Several DERIVED rows (insolation, equilibrium_temp x2, density, scale_height) were checked by recomputation against the report's own stated inputs and the DB host props (L=0.001567, R*=0.141 Rsun, Teff=2904 from db/stellar_props_curated.json), and the DB planet entry (db/planets_curated.json Proxima Cen b) which matches the orbital/mass rows exactly (no PHASE2_DESYNC on those).

findings.claim: equilibrium_temp_k (A=0) = 226 K, confidence high, 'derived'
findings.source_says: With the report's own inputs (S=0.646 S_earth per Boutle Table 1, or 0.668 from L=0.00157/a^2) the bare-rock A=0 equilibrium temperature is ~250 K (also ~250-256 K via Tstar*sqrt(R*/2a) with R*=0.141 Rsun, Teff=2904-2980). 226 K would require S~0.43 or a large albedo, neither of which is stated.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:81; cross-check: Teq = 278.5*(S(1-A))^0.25 with S=0.646 -> 250 K; 1702.08463.md:27 gives S=0.646; db/stellar_props_curated.json Proxima radius 0.141 Rsun, teff 2904.

findings.claim: equilibrium_temp_k (A=0.3) = 207 K, confidence high, 'derived'
findings.source_says: For S=0.646 and A=0.3 the equilibrium temperature is ~228 K (~228-234 K via the stellar-radius method). 207 K is ~21 K too low and does not follow from the stated L and a. Note the literature (Anglada-Escude 2016 context) puts Teq ~234 K for Earth-like albedo, also well above 207.

findings.claim: insolation_s_earth = 0.646, Basis 'derived from L = 0.00157 L_sun and a = 0.04848 AU'
findings.source_says: The value 0.646 is correct, but it is NOT derived from L=0.00157/a^2 (that gives 0.668). 0.646 is exactly the stellar-irradiance ratio adopted in Boutle 2017 (881.7 W/m^2 = 0.646 S_earth). The Basis attributes the number to a host-luminosity derivation it does not actually come from; the host L=0.001567/0.04848^2 = 0.667.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:80; 1702.08463.md:27 'Stellar irradiance / W m-2 (S_earth) 881.7 (0.646)'; recompute 0.00157/0.04848^2 = 0.668.

findings.claim: density_g_cc = 5.36, Basis 'derived; consistent with Earth-analog rocky composition'
findings.source_says: Density derived from the report's own mass (1.055 M_earth) and radius (1.07 R_earth) is 5.514*1.055/1.07^3 = 4.75 g/cc, not 5.36. The stated 5.36 implies R~1.03 R_earth, contradicting the report's radius_rearth=1.07 row.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:78 (density 5.36) vs :76 (radius 1.07) and :75 (mass 1.055); recompute rho = 5.514*1.055/1.07^3 = 4.75.

findings.claim: atmosphere_scale_height_km = 11, Basis 'derived: kT/mu*g with T = 260 K, mu = 30, g = 9.0 m/s^2'
findings.source_says: H = kT/(mu*m_H*g) with T=260 K, mu=30, g=9.0 m/s^2 equals 8.0 km, not 11 km. 11 km would need T~357 K or mu~22 at the stated g. The stated inputs do not produce the stated output.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:90; recompute 1.381e-23*260/(30*1.6605e-27*9.0) = 8.0 km.

findings.claim: atmosphere_composition = 'N2 95%, CO2 5%, H2O 0.1-1%, trace O2', Basis 'Boutle 2017 simplified N2+CO2'
findings.source_says: Boutle 2017 Table 2 specifies the simplified-N2 atmosphere as N2-dominated with TRACE CO2: CO2 MMR = 5.941e-4 kg/kg (~376 ppmv, present-day-Earth). 5% CO2 is ~130x higher than what Boutle uses. The report's own Surface synthesis (line 124) correctly states '1 bar N2 + 376 ppm CO2', directly contradicting the table's 5%.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:89 ('CO2 5%') and :166 ('~5% CO2') vs :124 ('376 ppm CO2'); 1702.08463.md:40 'CO2 MMR / kg kg-1 5.941e-4', :43 'nitrogen dominated atmosphere with trace amounts of CO2... values taken from present day Earth'.

findings.claim: magnetosphere_standoff_planet_radii = 1.5, confidence high, Basis 'Garraffo 2022 - stellar wind pressure compresses standoff to < 2 R_p during super-Alfvenic transits'
findings.source_says: Garraffo 2022 explicitly reports the magnetopause standoff for Proxima b 'with values ranging between approximately 3 R_p and 11 R_p, depending on the orbital phase and wind solution' (assuming 0.1 G surface field). It does not give < 2 R_p / 1.5 R_p; the minimum is ~3 R_p.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:105; 2211.15697.md:83 'magnetopause standoff distance that strongly varies with time, with values ranging between approximately 3 R_p and 11 R_p'.

findings.claim: magnetic_dipole_moment_normalized_earth = 0.1, Basis 'Tie-break: Zuluaga 2018 plausibility range 0.01-1.0; 0.1 selected for partial-shielding regime'
findings.source_says: Zuluaga 2018 abstract gives a MINIMUM dipole moment M_dip > 0.32 (x2.3/div2.9) M_earth (i.e. roughly 0.11-0.74, lower bound ~0.32), not a range '0.01-1.0'. The chosen 0.1 M_earth is below Zuluaga's stated minimum. Garraffo's '0.1 G' surface field (~0.33x Earth's 0.3 G) also corresponds to ~0.33 M_earth, not 0.1.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:103; 1609.00707.md:7 'the dynamo of Proxima b have a dipole moment M_dip > 0.32 (div2.9, x2.3) M_dip,earth'; 2211.15697.md:77 'planetary surface magnetic field of 0.1 G as estimated by Zuluaga & Bustamante (2018)'.

findings.claim: aurora_emission_species_primary = '[OI] 557.7 nm + N2+ 391.4 nm First Negative + CO2+ doublet', Basis 'Braam 2024 photochemistry framework'
findings.source_says: Braam 2024 (2410.19108) is about spin-orbit-resonance climate dynamics and 3D OZONE chemistry; it contains no aurora, airglow, or emission-line content. Grep for 'aurora', '557', '391', 'Meinel', 'green line' returns nothing in the cached full text. The emission-line identities are generic spectroscopy, not from Braam 2024. (Same attribution issue affects aurora_color_secondary_hex, which cites 'photochemistry of N2+CO2 trace atmosphere'.)
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:112 (and :111); 2410.19108.md:7 abstract describes '3D atmospheric chemistry... 3D ozone distribution', no aurora terms anywhere in cache.

findings.claim: Prose: 'Cloud-band morphology evolves on the 10-day rotation period of the substellar Rossby waves (Cohen 2023), giving in-game cloud animation a visible 1-week cadence.' (supports cloud_morphology / cloud_cover_fraction rows citing Cohen 2023)
findings.source_says: Cohen 2023 Table 3 gives the Rossby-wave cloud-oscillation period for Proxima b as 157.5 days (Control ProxB) / 120 days (Warm ProxB), NOT ~10 days. The report appears to conflate the 11.2-day orbital/rotation period with the 157-day Rossby-gyre oscillation period.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:262-263; 2211.11887.md:139 'Oscillation period (days) | 157.5 | 120 | 19.4 | 16.2', :143 '157.5 and 120 day oscillation periods in the Control and Warm Proxima Centauri b simulations'.

findings.claim: water_mass_fraction = 0.05-0.10, Basis 'Tie-break: Herath 2021 interior models allow 0.001-0.4'
findings.source_says: not in cache. Herath et al. 2021 (bibcode 2021MNRAS.500..333H) has no arXiv preprint and is absent from the _papers cache (Tier A manual followup). The specific '0.001-0.4 by mass' range cannot be confirmed offline; the bib YAML abstract snippet mentions only 'a range of possible water mass fractions' and water delivery 'between 0 and 51 Earth oceans'.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:79; docs/phase3/_bib/proxima-cen-b.yaml:1200 'ads_bibcode: 2021MNRAS.500..333H', :672/:1107 abstract; ls of _papers shows no 2021MNRAS file.

findings.claim: stellar_illumination_color_temp_k = 2980 K, confidence high, Basis 'Proxima Teff' (prose: stellar_color_temp_k = 2980)
findings.source_says: The DB recommended Proxima Teff is 2904 K (Passegger 2019, db/stellar_props_curated.json teff_k=2904), and the host report proxima-cen.md adopts teff_k = 2904 / stellar_color_temp_k = 2904. The planet report's 2980 K contradicts the host/DB recommended value by ~76 K (~2.6%).
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:116, :256; db/stellar_props_curated.json Proxima Cen teff_k=2904; docs/phase3/proxima-cen.md:58 'teff_k | 2904 +/- 51' and :75 'stellar_color_temp_k | 2904'.

findings.claim: surface_radiation_dose_msv_yr = 5000, Basis 'Atri 2020 for 1 bar atmospheric shielding + weak B-field; spikes to 10^5 during superflares' (prose cites 'Atri 2020 Table 6')
findings.source_says: Atri 2020 reports doses in Gray PER EVENT (not annual mSv) and its Table 6 is an 'enhancement factor over GCR background', not a 5000 mSv/yr quiescent figure. The paper's quantitative Proxima-b results use 30 g/cm^2 and 1000 g/cm^2 columns and quote per-event Gy (e.g. 1.3e-3 Gy at 1000 g/cm^2 for a 10 GeV hr-event; '1000s of Gy in extreme cases'). The specific 5000 mSv/yr annual value is an interpretive conversion not stated in the cached text; only the column-density basis (~1000 g/cm^2) is directly supported.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/proxima-cen-b.md:107, :201-205; 1910.09871.md:23 atmospheric depth '1036 g cm-2', :33 'dose levels can be in 1000s of Gy in extreme cases', :33 'Table 6 gives the enhancement factor over GCR background'.

tau-cet-e.coverage_note: 44 Decisions rows audited. Of the source-cited rows: the 5 RV-orbital rows (sidereal_period_days, eccentricity, argument_of_periastron_deg, semi_major_axis_au, mass_mearth) all cite Feng 2017 (arXiv:1708.02051), which IS cached and IS in tau-cet.yaml — all five verified exactly against Feng Table 5 (P 162.87[162.41,163.95]; e 0.18[0.04,0.36]; omega 0.39 rad=22.3deg[-23.5,96.3]; a 0.538; m sin i 3.93[3.29,4.76]); the report's asymmetric error brackets reproduce Table 5 correctly. 7 derived rows (surface_gravity 1.20, density 3.66, insolation 1.69, Teq A=0 317, Teq A=0.3 290, scale_height 7.0, angular_diameter 0.786) were re-computed and all check out, using Teixeira 2009 L=0.488 (cached, 0811.3989) and Kopparapu 2014 (cached, 1404.5292). The remaining rows are explicitly labeled low-confidence tie-breaks / historical-scenario synthesis with no decisive cited number. TWO rows lack a cacheable/verifiable source: disposition (Figueira 2025 + Cretignier 2021 — the cfg-decisive, only-high-confidence row; both papers absent from cache AND bib YAML) and stellar_illumination_color_temp_k (Pavlenko 2012 absent from cache AND bib YAML, and the 5370 value is actually Korolik 2023's, misattributed). NOTE (context, not a Decisions row): intro lines 9-11 claim e had 'the lowest amplitude-to-noise ratio' of the four Feng planets, but Feng Table 5 gives e the HIGHEST semi-amplitude K=0.55 m/s (g=0.49, h=0.39, e=0.55, f=0.35) — source-contradicted prose. Also the Bibliography's Kopparapu entry says e sits at '1.58 S-earth' while the rest of the document (intro/Surface/Decisions) uses 1.69 S-earth (0.488/0.538^2=1.686); internal inconsistency in prose. No PHASE2_DESYNC: db/systems/tau_cet.json correctly omits e from planets[].

findings.claim: `disposition` = False Positive Planet (NEA 2026-04-09), confidence HIGH. Basis: 'Figueira 2025 ESPRESSO refutation; Cretignier 2021 YARARA earlier non-detection. The only high-confidence row in this table.'
findings.source_says: not in cache
findings.evidence: Neither Figueira 2025 (2025A&A...700A.174F / doi:10.1051/0004-6361/202553869) nor Cretignier 2021 (2021A&A...653A..43C) exists in docs/phase3/_papers/ (ls 2025*/2021* -> no matches) and neither is listed in docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml papers[] (grep 'figueira|700A.174|202553869' and 'cretignier|653A..43' -> NOT FOUND). The two refutation papers that DRIVE the only high-confidence, cfg-decisive row cannot be checked offline. (Independent corroboration: db/systems/tau_cet.json planets[] omits e, listing only f/g/h, which is consistent with a false-positive disposition, so the conclusion is plausible — but the cited evidence itself is unverifiable.)

findings.claim: `stellar_illumination_color_temp_k` = 5370, Basis: 'host Teff (Pavlenko 2012)'.
findings.source_says: not in cache; and the report's own Bibliography attributes Teff=5344 K to Pavlenko 2012, not 5370. The 5370 K value is the Korolik 2023 CHARA/MIRC-X interferometric Teff per docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml note ('Teff = Korolik 2023 interferometric 5370 K').
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/tau-cet-e.md:80 (row) vs docs/phase3/tau-cet-e.md:319 ('Pavlenko ... Teff = 5344 K'); docs/phase3/_bib/tau-cet.yaml line 6-7 ('Teff = Korolik 2023 interferometric 5370 K'). Pavlenko 2012 is absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ (no dedicated tau Cet Pavlenko paper; cache 'Pavlenko' hits are all other stars) and absent from tau-cet.yaml papers[] (grep 'pavlenko' -> NOT FOUND). So 5370 is misattributed to Pavlenko (it is Korolik's), and Pavlenko 2012 is itself unverifiable. Non-cfg-decisive (the SED color-temp row only labels the historical scenario).

hd-69830-b.coverage_note: 42 Decisions rows. CACHEABLE/VERIFIED SOURCES: only 2 cited papers are present in docs/phase3/_papers/ — Tanner 2015 (1412.5251, host stellar params) and Atri 2019 (1910.09871). The planet-level bib docs/phase3/_bib/hd-69830-b.yaml does NOT exist; only the host bib hd-69830.yaml exists. Orbital + mass rows (eccentricity, argument_of_periastron, sidereal_period, semi_major_axis, mass_mearth) cite Lovis 2006, which has arxiv_id:null and status:skipped in the host bib (no offline full text), but every one of those values matches db/planets_curated.json exactly (a=0.0785, e=0.1, ω=340, Msini=10.2), so they are corroborated by the curated DB and counted as verified. The six decision-DRIVING tie-break papers — Owen & Wu 2017 (1705.10810), Rogers 2021 (2105.03446), Morley 2015 (1511.01492), Kawashima 2019 (1905.02963), Heller 2011 (1101.2156), Showman & Polvani 2011 (1103.3101) — are referenced but ABSENT from the cache and from any bib YAML, so all rows resting on them (radius, density, atmosphere composition/presence, cloud cover/morphology, tidal lock) are UNVERIFIABLE offline. The most material findings are NOT cache-coverage gaps but PHASE2_DESYNC against db/stellar_props_curated.json: the report's derived-quantity stellar inputs (R★=0.887 superseded by Tanner 0.9058, L=0.60 vs 0.622, Teff 5385 vs recommended 5394, age 5.7 vs recommended 7.5 Gyr) all use non-recommended/superseded values, and the star_apparent_angular_diameter_deg=5.39 row is additionally internally inconsistent — its own stated formula and inputs give 6.02°. 28 rows verified (orbital/mass via DB + arithmetically self-consistent derived chains + physically-standard qualitative picks); 14 rows carry findings (overlapping where a row is both desync and unverifiable).

findings.claim: 5.39 deg, derived: 2 R★ / a × (180/π) with R★ = 0.887 R☉, a = 0.0785 AU
findings.source_says: The cell's own stated formula and inputs do not produce 5.39°. 2·0.887·R☉(AU)/0.0785·180/π = 6.02°. With the DB-recommended R★ = 0.9058 R☉ (Tanner 2015) it is 6.15°. 5.39° only results from R★ ≈ 0.794 R☉, which is neither value.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:75; arithmetic 2*0.887*0.00465047/0.0785*180/pi = 6.021. The wrong 5.39 propagates into prose at lines 161, 214, 231-234 ('fills 5.4°', '5.39°', '~10× the Sun').

findings.claim: R★ = 0.887 R☉ used as the input radius for the angular-diameter derivation
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json recommends R★ = 0.9058 ± 0.019 R☉ (interferometry, Tanner 2015, recommended:true). The curated meta_notes explicitly state: 'Prior unverified values (Lovis 2006 M 0.86, Rosenthal 2021 R 0.887) superseded by Tanner.' The report uses the superseded 0.887.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json radius_measurements value_rsun 0.9058 recommended true; meta_notes '...Rosenthal 2021 R 0.887) superseded by Tanner.' vs docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:75.

findings.claim: Insolation 97 S⊕ and Teq derived from L = 0.60 L☉
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json recommends L★ = 0.622 ± 0.014 L☉ (Tanner 2015, bolometric_flux, recommended:true). Using 0.622 gives S = 100.9 S⊕ and Teq(A=0) = 881 K, not 97 / 873. ~3.5% low; internally self-consistent with L=0.60 but the input L is not the curated value.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json luminosity_measurements value_lsun 0.622 recommended true; vs docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:45-47. Arithmetic: 0.622/0.0785^2 = 100.9.

findings.claim: 5385 K (HD 69830 Teff), high confidence
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json records teff_k 5394 as the recommended interferometric value (Tanner 2015); 5385 K is the *non-recommended* SME spectroscopic measurement (recommended:false) in the same paper. Both are Tanner 2015 so the attribution is fine, but the recommended curated Teff is 5394, not 5385.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json teff_k 5394 teff_bibcode 2015ApJ...800..115T; teff_measurements lists 5394 recommended:true and 5385 recommended:false. vs docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:76.

findings.field: system age (used in tidally_locked, obliquity_deg, scenario choice)
findings.claim: 5.7 ± 1 Gyr system age, cited throughout (lines 10, 22, 35, 36, 100, 173) as the driver of tidal lock and photoevaporation-stripping
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json recommends age 7.5 ± 3 Gyr (Tanner SME-isochrone, recommended:true); alternatives 10.6 ± 4 (Tanner HRD) and 5.9 (Mamajek 2008). 5.7 Gyr is the bottom of the non-recommended Mamajek activity-age range, presented in the report as the point value with no documented-divergence framing for age.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json age_measurements 7.5 recommended:true, 5.9 (Mamajek) recommended:false; meta_notes 'AGE IS A DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE ... Tanner SME-isochrone 7.5 +/- 3 Gyr (recommended)'. vs docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:35,100,173.

findings.field: spectral type (G8V used in atmosphere_composition, cloud_tint, aurora, prose)
findings.claim: HD 69830 treated as G8V throughout (rows at lines 53,58,67,70,73; prose lines 156,162)
findings.source_says: db/stellar_props_curated.json records spectype 'K0 V' (Gray et al. 2006, recommended). Tanner 2015 Table 2 does list 'Spectral Type G8V (Gray et al. 2006)' and the SED text fits both, so G8V is sourced in the cited paper — but it conflicts with the curated canonical K0 V.
findings.evidence: db/stellar_props_curated.json 'spectype': 'K0 V'; _papers/1412.5251.md:73 '| Spectral Type | G8V | Gray et al. (2006)|' and line 27 'opted to use the values derived from the K0V fit'. vs docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:53,156.

findings.claim: 2.6 R⊕ (low conf), tie-break per 'Owen & Wu 2017, Rogers 2021' stripped-sub-Neptune mass-radius relation
findings.source_says: not in cache — arXiv 1705.10810 (Owen & Wu 2017) and 2105.03446 (Rogers 2021) are referenced but absent from docs/phase3/_papers/ (no .md/.html). Neither is in any bib YAML (planet bib does not exist; host bib lists neither). The mass-radius basis cannot be checked offline.
findings.evidence: ls docs/phase3/_papers/1705.10810.* and 2105.03446.* => ABSENT; grep 'Evaporation Valley'/'water worlds around M dwarfs' across _papers/*.md => no match. docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:42.

findings.claim: 3.2 g/cc (low conf), tie-break per 'Owen 2019, Rogers 2021'
findings.source_says: not in cache — Rogers 2021 (2105.03446) absent; Owen 2019 (bibcode 2019AREPS..47...67O) has no arXiv id given and is absent. Cannot verify the photoevaporated-Neptune density basis offline.
findings.evidence: ls docs/phase3/_papers/2105.03446.* => ABSENT; no Owen 2019 cache file. docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:44.

findings.claim: H₂ ~80%, He ~15%, H₂O ~3% + hydrocarbon haze; cloud cover 0.90; global haze deck — per 'Owen 2019, Morley 2015, Kawashima 2019, Showman & Polvani 2011'
findings.source_says: not in cache — Morley 2015 (1511.01492), Kawashima 2019 (1905.02963), Showman & Polvani 2011 (1103.3101) all referenced but absent from _papers/; Owen 2019 has no arXiv. The composition fractions, optical-depth-5, 0.1–1 mbar haze altitude, and 0.90 cover cannot be checked.

findings.claim: true (high conf), τ_lock ≲ 10⁷ yr per 'Heller 2011 scaling'
findings.source_says: not in cache — Heller 2011 (1101.2156) referenced but absent from _papers/. The τ_lock ~10⁶-10⁷ yr scaling claim cannot be verified offline (note prose line 176 says ~10⁶ yr, the row says ≤10⁷ yr — consistent order-of-magnitude). The 1:1 conclusion for an 8.67 d / 0.078 AU planet is physically standard, so low severity.
findings.evidence: ls docs/phase3/_papers/1101.2156.* => ABSENT. docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:35,176.

findings.claim: 200 mSv/yr, '~80× Earth's 2.4 mSv/yr'
findings.source_says: The cached Atri 2019 paper (1910.09871) is in the cache but does not provide this value or an Earth 2.4 mSv/yr annual reference — it reports Mars RAD background ~210 µGy/day and per-event SPE doses, not an annual mSv/yr surface dose for a thick-envelope sub-Neptune. The report itself self-labels the 200 value as 'Rough order-of-magnitude synthesis estimate (not derived from any quantitative dose model)', so it is transparently unsourced.
findings.evidence: _papers/1910.09871.md:32 (Mars RAD '210 +/- 40 µGy/day'; model '218.5 µGy/day'); no '2.4 mSv' string in cache. docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:68.

findings.claim: Single Decisions cell carries both the chosen 2.6 R⊕ and an 'envelope-retaining alternative (no measured radius; Chen-Kipping mass-radius placeholder) kept as cfg variant' — while header states 'No documented divergences' and there is no '## Canonical alternatives' section.
findings.source_says: Policy: canonical/variant values should not be inlined in one Decisions cell. The actual variant figures (radius 3.17 R⊕, density ~1.7 g/cc) are placed in '## Open items', and the picks are labeled tie-break (not divergence), so this is borderline rather than a hard divergence-without-section violation.
findings.evidence: docs/phase3/hd-69830-b.md:42 (variant inlined in cell) and :26 ('No documented divergences'); variant detail at :338-342 ('Envelope-retaining cfg variant ... radius 3.17 R⊕').
